title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
Korean BBQ Dinner can be a little intimidating for foreigners in Korea. It’s hard to know what the dishes are, how to eat them and what to expect. The best places don’t even have English menus. So, we have created this event to show you the best of Korean BBQ in Seoul, to make you get the best out of this amazingly delicious cultural experience.
Everyone gathers around a grill in the middle of the table. Our local guide will explain how things are supposed to be eaten(and drunk) while the server brings plates of raw meat and lots of side dishes—called banchan (bon-chon)—then, helped by the guide, everyone can begin cooking and eating their own food and explore Korean culture through this unique foodie event!
Alcoholic drinks are part of this experience and will be served during the meal, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated Upgraded Drinks Package is available.
_\*Menu and food stops could change according to weather, season and availability._
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin by meeting outside Jongno 3-ga station, in front of Exit 4(there's plenty of other exits, make sure you take the number 4!)
We'll walk through one of the oldest and cutest Hanok Villages in Seoul, Ikseon-Dong.
Our guide will give you a bit of historical background about the area and then we'll see Ikseondong BBQ street and stop at one of our favourite Korean BBQ places and start feasting.
Our guide will show you how locals eat, drink and enjoy the best Korean BBQ, an experience which without a Korean person really loses its magic.
There will be plenty of meat(mostly Pork), served with many side dishes and rice: have you ever heard of words like Gochujang, Banchan or Ssambap? You’ll never forget them after this Dinner!
Beer and Soju are not to be missed on this occasion: you'll learn about the best Korean standards of beer and soju in a glass and what are the most classic Korean combos for that(and... maybe some games?)!
We'll finish the Dinner right inside of the BBQ place, in a Korean movie-like atmosphere.

Extra bottle of Soju

## WHY IKSEON-DONG HANOK VILLAGE?
The Hanok Village of Ikseon-Dong is right in the center of Seoul located in Jongno-gu.
Ikseon-Dong is a very small neighbourhood with a great vibe, and lots of traditional Korean houses (called Hanoks).
The atmosphere in the middle of these alleys with all these Hanoks is just fantastic! It is also an incredible place for your instagram photos. There are so many great angles from which you can take stunning shots.
The cool thing about the neighborhood is that the alleys are really small, so you feel like you’re walking around a completely different part of Seoul. The fact that Ikseon-Dong is a pedestrian area also adds to the special atmosphere.

# Secret Food Tours Chiang Mai
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide
Discover Chiang Mai with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
Also recommended by
 3 - 3 ½ hours
 Three Kings Monument, Old Towm
 6 stops
 12 people max
 English
## What you'll do
Discover the vibrant flavors, tantalizing aromas, and rich history of Northern Thailand’s food culture on an unforgettable culinary adventure. Led by our expert local guides, you’ll dive into Chiang Mai’s food scene like never before—sampling iconic street dishes, uncovering hidden culinary gems, and exploring the authentic tastes that make this city a food lover’s paradise.
### SEE ITINERARY
Kick off your tour in front of the historic Three Kings Monument before stepping into a hidden gem of a temple.
Start your morning like a local with a classic Thai breakfast—sticky rice, expertly grilled in a fragrant banana leaf.
Then, prepare to savor the flavors of authentic Chicken Satay paired with its iconic, creamy peanut sauce. Try it and tell us if it rivals Bangkok’s version!
Dive into the bold and vibrant world of Northern Thai cuisine.
Relish a richly spiced Larb Moo (a zesty pork salad) and Sai Ua, the famous Northern Thai sausage—each bite a powerful explosion of flavor that will elevate your experience.
Savor Chiang Mai’s signature dish, Khao Soi—silky coconut curry noodles, served in an unforgettable setting.
Pair it with stir-fried Pak Boong (Morning Glory), some Khanom Krok: sweet and savoury Coconut-rice mini pancakes, and a chilled Thai tea for the perfect balance of spice and refreshment.
Cool off with a vegan butterfly pea ice cream, and end on a sweet note with Bua Loi, warm and delicate rice balls floating in creamy coconut milk.
Finally, we’ll wrap up this flavorful journey at Wat Lok Moli, one of Chiang Mai’s most charming temples.
Oh, and don’t forget our Secret Dish, revealed only on the tour!
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances.
Book Your Tour
$49.99 / person
Lowest price guarantee
If you have any issues with payment, please email us at [contact@secrettours.com](mailto:contact@secrettours.com) with the city, date and time of tour, and how many adult and child's tickets you need so we can assist you.

What you'll taste
### THE FOOD
Grilled-in-banana-leaf Sticky Rice
Chicken Satay Skewers with Peanut Sauce
Larb Kua - spicy minced meat salad with roasted rice powder
Sai Ua – Northern Thai sausage
Stir fried Pak Boong (Morning Glory)
Khao Soi: Chiang Mai creamy Coconut curry Noodles
Khanom Krok: sweet and savoury Coconut-rice mini pancakes
Traditional Butterfly Pea Ice cream
Sweet Bua Loi(rice balls in Coconut cream)
Our delicious Secret Dish
### THE DRINKS
Chilled Lemongrass Infusion
Silky Thai Iced Tea
Water
Meeting Spot
We'll meet right in front of the Statue Three Kings Monument in Chiang Mai old Town. . If you have any questions. Please [email us](mailto:chiangmai@secrettours.com).
Three Kings Monument, Chiang Mai Old Town
[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/tK3MXJ7eVmrKQEYQA)
Mon - Sun 9:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## WHY ChIANG MAI OLD TOWN AND ITS SURROUNDINGS?
Chiang Mai Old Town is renowned for its rich cultural heritage, stunning temples, and some of the best authentic street food in Thailand. Visitors flock to this charming area to experience unique flavors that go beyond typical restaurant offerings, immersing themselves in the true essence of Chiang Mai.
As you explore the Old Town, you'll encounter beautifully preserved temples like Wat Phra Singh and Wat Chedi Luang, each with its own captivating history and intricate architecture. The vibrant atmosphere is filled with the aromas of Northern Thai cuisine, known for its bold flavors and fresh ingredients.
The Old Town features an array of food stalls and local eateries, showcasing traditional dishes and cultural venues, then, right outside of it, we'd discover where the locals like having their favourites such as Khao Soi and Sai Ua. Your guide will lead you through the enchanting streets where they grew up, revealing hidden gems and delicious food that highlight the lively culture and history of this captivating city. Enjoy the delightful fusion of food, culture, and spirituality that makes Chiang Mai an unforgettable destination.
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
Simply go to our "Book Now" page where we detail our tours. Select your tour, the time of your tour, and book it!
Are you looking for something special? Are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email.
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above.
Where the tour will end?
We end our tour 10 min away from the temple Wat Lok Moli, in Chiang Mai.
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
You can always tell our guides of any food allergies or special dietary requirements. We will do our best, however most of our food stops prepare only one dish, so vegetarians cannot be accommodated at this time. If you have any concerns, feel free to email us at [chiangmai@secrettours.com](mailto:chiangmai@secrettours.com) before booking.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are quite welcome, however it is a long walk and you may choose to find a babysitter at your hotel. It is free for any child 2 years or under. We can give them non-alcoholic drinks and you are free to enjoy the tour with your entire family of all ages.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
There is no alcohol included on this tour. Just inform our guide before your tour starts if you have any special request. During the tasting, water is available just ask your guide as you need more.
Is there enough food during your tour?
There is a lot of food on this tour and we recommend a light or no breakfast. As we travel you get many delicious treats that comprise a meal with fruit and sweets. You will not be hungry after the tour although please do tell us if we can get something more.
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 28 minutes to 35 minutes of walking, but it is not too difficult and is broken up between food stops. The guide walks at the pace of the tour members, you will never be rushed. There are no stairs on the tour or hills.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Where does your food come from?
Top - quality, fresh and local! From Vendors and restaurants. All vendors are carefully regulated so that the food will be safe to eat.
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Chiang Mai. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Chiang Mai?
Book our award-winning Secret Food Tours Chiang Mai before it fills up!

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
Step into the high-energy heart of Chinatown, the birthplace of the city’s legendary street food scene and Hua Lumphong, an old-style in central Bangkok. This Bangkok food tour is a sensory deep dive where sizzling woks, aromatic spices, and neon-lit alleyways reveal the true culinary soul of Thailand.
You’ll navigate vibrant market stalls to sample family recipes perfected over generations, from royal-style snacks to world-famous noodle soups. Between bites, we’ll uncover the history of this historic district, ensuring you experience Bangkok just like a local.
### SEE ITINERARY
Spicy Basil Chicken - Kick off your culinary tour in Bangkok with a quintessential Thai classic, featuring fragrant holy basil and jasmine rice for a perfect balance of heat and herb.
Shrimp Dumplings - Savor delicate, hand-folded dumplings bursting with fresh, succulent shrimp, a beloved Thai-Chinese staple.
Red Pork Steamed Buns - Peel back the fluffy exterior of traditional Salapao to discover a savory-sweet center of tender, slow-roasted red pork.
Thai Papaya Salad - Watch our local experts toss fresh green papaya, lime, and chilies to create a crisp, refreshing explosion of flavor.
Chinese Donuts with Pandan Custard - Indulge in golden, crispy Pa Thong Ko paired with a rich, aromatic green custard made from fresh pandan leaves.
Stewed Pork Knuckle - Experience the melt-in-your-mouth texture of pork knuckle braised in a complex, spiced soy broth until it falls off the bone.
Pork or Chicken Satay - Enjoy skewers of perfectly grilled, marinated meat served with a rich, velvety peanut dipping sauce.
Duck Noodle Soup - Pull up a plastic stool for a bowl of authentic, deep-flavored broth and tender duck, a local favorite that defines the neighborhood’s food culture.
Black Sesame Dumplings in Ginger Tea - End your journey with a warming bowl of nutty sesame dumplings floating in a spicy, rejuvenating ginger infusion.
Our final surprise is a handpicked Secret Dish that is a local treasure revealed only on the day of your tour.
**Upgrade your Secret Food Tour: Bangkok** with one locally brewed craft beer and one classic Thai cocktail, perfectly paired with a light local snack to enjoy as you drink.

Chinatown and Hua Lumphong are both known for being home to some of the best authentic street foods in Thailand. Everyone flocks to Chinatown in the evening to try unique foods, different to the usual restaurant foods that do not represent the real Bangkok!
Hua Lumphong is a distinct, old-style neighborhood in central Bangkok with a variety of food stalls at the market. Your guide will take you through his own neighbourhood, where he grew up, and you'll discover some of the finest food and hidden culture that makes this city such a special place.

## What you'll do
On our Culinary Tour in Istanbul - Kadikoy, you’ll enjoy the best Turkish dishes while discovering the stories behind them with each bite offering a taste of history.
By the end of the tour, you’ll have eaten some of Istanbul’s most delicious dishes, including lahmacun, menemen, and a variety of other Turkish delights, as well as, gained a better understanding of our culture and history.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin with a traditional Turkish breakfast featuring Menemen and Muhlama, perfectly paired with a cup of Turkish tea.
Next, you'll savor a freshly baked Pide, one of Turkey’s most beloved comfort foods.
Then, we’ll enjoy a tasting of Turkish wine followed by a cup of rich, aromatic Turkish coffee.
After that, you’ll try Lahmacun, a thin and crispy flatbread topped with minced meat, served with refreshing Ayran.
We’ll finish with a piece of heavenly Baklava, a sweet and flaky pastry layered with nuts and honey.

 English
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Istanbul - Spice Market and Historic Centre, you’ll explore the best spice market in Turkey, get lost like locals between backstreet shops and food stalls, and taste your way through the city’s mouthwatering local cuisine.
You’ll discover the famous spice bazaar, the most colourful, fragrant - and often most fun - place in the city, as visitors can taste all the goods vendors have to offer. We’ll also teach you what to buy, how to bargain, and the difference between real and fake saffron.
Make your way around the historical monuments and beautiful secret mosques as you learn about the fascinating history of our food and culture. By the end of your tour, you'll have sampled some of Istanbul's most delicious dishes and gained a better understanding of our culture and history.
### SEE ITINERARY
Navigate through the crowds of the world-famous spice market with your expert guide, tasting and learning how to shop for spices and what you can prepare with each one.
Sample wonderful teas and Turkish delights.
Dine on mouthwatering köfte meatballs in a local hole-in-the-wall spot tucked between the mosque and the market.
Dine on grilled fish brought straight from the fish market and prepared by local fishermen at a family-run stall.
Head to our charming seaside spot, where you’ll savour a selection of mezze, homemade breads, and kebabs grilled and baked right before your eyes, all while enjoying a beautiful view of the sea.
End the tour with a traditional dessert in Istanbul's trendiest district.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.

## WHY ISTANBUL’S SPICE MARKET AND EMINÖNÜ?
Eminönü, Istanbul’s historic centre, is one of the most historic and picturesque neighborhoods in the city, with centuries-old buildings that make the perfect combination for any traveler looking to experience both modernity and tradition on their trip.
Eminönü has an incredibly rich history and withholds many of Istanbul’s most important monuments such as, the Galata Bridge, the Golden Horn, the Galata Tower, Sultanahmet Camii (the Blue Mosque), and Aya Sofya (Hagia Sofia).
Meanwhile, the spice market is the most colourful, vibrant, energetic, fragrant - and often most fun - place in the city, as local vendors offer passersby all sorts of products to try. After all, what’s Turkish food without spice?

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Taipei, you'll journey through the vibrant streets of this dynamic city, discovering its rich food culture from a local's perspective.
Starting in the lively Shida Night Market and Taipei University areas. Explore the perfect mix of tradition and innovation, from bustling street food stalls to modern foodie hotspots like Yongkang Street.
These places tell the story of a city that honors its past while embracing the present and the future.
We know you’ll fall in love with the incredible flavors of our dishes and the fascinating stories behind them. From iconic Taiwanese staples to hidden culinary gems, every bite will deepen your appreciation of our culture and traditions.
If you like, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/taipei/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Start your journey by savoring the iconic Pork Bun with a delightful surprise—savory soup encased within its delicate folds. This rich, aromatic treat is sure to win your heart just before we enter the lively serpentine lanes of Shida Night Market.
From there, your guide will introduce you to local favorites like Wet tofu skin and Spring Onion bacon roll, grilled to perfection right before your eyes.
Get ready for a crispy twist with Taiwanese Fried Chicken at one of the best places for that in Taiwan.
Wander through Taipei’s bustling streets, making your way to the University district and vibrant Yongkang Street.
This area is a food lover’s paradise, offering a mix of local street eats and international delights: one of the best Bubble Teas ever will also be waiting for you here.
No visit to Taipei is complete without tasting Lu Rou Fan, the locals’ beloved braised pork rice dish.
As you savor its rich and savory flavor, your guide will share the secrets behind its preparation and why it holds a special place in Taiwanese hearts.
Experience a culinary gem from Tainan - some luscious Meatballs(which also contain shrimps!), immersed in a special sauce that celebrates the diverse culinary traditions of Taiwan: you might want to see them all after this.
Sink your teeth into a Taiwanese Scallion Pancake, layered with egg and aromatic basil, a fragrant bite you’ll reminisce long after the tour ends.
For a sweet finale, enjoy Dou Hua 豆花, the traditional Tofu Pudding served in its classic form with tapioca pearls, jelly and brown sugar.
And, of course, there’s always our Secret Dish, a delightful surprise that you’ll discover on the spot and can only be tasted in Taiwan!

Our guide will be waiting for you with an orange umbrella, so they're easy to spot!. If you have any questions, please [email us](mailto:taipei@secrettours.com).
Bellissimo Coffee Taiwan, No. 181號, Section 3
[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/YBbidY58isk3ZwJD8)
Tue - Sun 11:30 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## WHY TAIPEI CENTER, SHIDA MARKET, AND YONGKANG STREET?
Taipei’s heart is a melting pot of culture, history, and some of the most beloved street food in Taiwan. Visitors are drawn to this lively area to experience authentic flavors and immerse themselves in the heart of Taipei’s dynamic food scene.
Shida Night Market is famed for its youthful energy, thanks to its proximity to National Taiwan Normal University. Here, the streets come alive with students and locals enjoying an array of mouthwatering street food. From savory grilled sausage to crispy Taiwanese fried chicken, the market offers a mix of traditional dishes and modern culinary creations that capture the essence of Taiwanese street culture.
Yongkang Street, on the other hand, provides a more refined yet equally authentic food experience. Known for its diverse range of restaurants, street food stalls, and quaint teahouses, it’s a place where locals and visitors alike gather to savor the most iconic local dishes and try different Bubble teas. The street is also steeped in history, offering a glimpse of Taipei’s evolution while remaining true to its roots.
With our local guides we're sure that Shida Market, and Yongkang Street together showcase the culinary diversity and cultural richness that make Taipei an unforgettable destination.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/taipei/food-tours-taipei/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
Simply go to our "Book Now" page where we detail our tours. Select your tour, the time of your tour, and book it!
Are you looking for something special? Are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email.
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the number of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) We offer a [UPGRADE DRINKS package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/taipei/upgraded-drinks-package/) that gives you an extra drink.
Where is the meeting spot?
We'll meet in front of street entrance of Taipower building station Metro Exit 3, Songshan-Xindian Line, right between Bellissimo Coffee Taiwan, No. 181號, Section 3, Roosevelt Rd, Da’an District, Taipei City, 106 and No. 177, Section 3, Roosevelt Rd, Da’an District. Our guide will be waiting for you with an orange umbrella, so they're easy to spot! If you have any questions, please email us.
Our guide will be standing there to greet you with our orange Secret Food Tours umbrella so we are easy to spot!
Where the tour will end?
We end our tour next to Dongmen Station , from which it'll be very easy to go anywhere and our guides will be happy to assist with directions
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
You can always tell our guides of any food allergies or special dietary requirements. We will do our best, however most of our food stops preparing only one dish, so vegetarians cannot be accommodated at this time. If you have any concerns, feel free to email us at [taipei@secrettours](mailto:taipei@secrettours.com) before booking.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are quite welcome, however it is a long walk and you may choose to find a babysitter at your hotel. It is free for any child 2 years or under. We can give them non-alcoholic drinks and you are free to enjoy the tour with your entire family of all ages.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
Just inform our guide before your tour starts if you have any special request. During the tasting, water is available just ask your guide as you need more.
Is there enough food during your tour?
There is a lot of food on this tour and we recommend a light or no breakfast. As we travel you get many delicious treats that comprise a meal with fruit and sweets. You will not be hungry after the tour although please do tell us if we can get something more.
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 32 minutes of walking, but it is not too difficult and is broken up between food stops. The guide walks at the pace of the tour members, you will never be rushed. There are no stairs on the tour or hills.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 10 people maximum. If you are more than 10 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
Top - quality, fresh and local! From Vendors and restaurants. All vendors are carefully regulated so that the food will be safe to eat.
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Chiang Mai. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Taipei?

## What you'll do
Embark on a culinary journey through Dubai's vibrant gastronomic scene with our VIP Secret Food Tours! Starting at the luxurious Armani Hotel, going up the iconic Burj Khalifa, we'll indulge in exquisite delicacies while soaking in breathtaking views of the city skyline and Arabian Desert.
Discover the culinary icons inside Dubai's famous luxury shopping area, uncover the mall's captivating history and architectural marvels and try both the local food and some internationally recognised venues.
On your culinary tour we visit Souk Al Bahar, offering a modern twist on traditional Middle Eastern markets, where we'll experience waterfront dining and vibrant shopping experiences. From traditional Emirati dishes to innovative fusion cuisine, join us as we delve into the rich flavours and cultural heritage of Dubai, creating unforgettable memories one delicious bite at a time.
Please NOTE, this is a VIP experience and there are dress code requirements for certain venues: no full tracksuits or flip flops allowed.
### SEE ITINERARY
We will kick off on Floor 122 of the Burj Khalifa to enjoy three canapés and a Virgin Cocktail in true Dubai style with magnificent views across the city.
Past Fashion avenue we will sample local hummus, freshly baked Arabic bread, olive oil, Emirati Machboos, and for the adventurous, on lucky days, Camel Milk!
Discover Michelin Recommended Black Pepper Beef Xiaolongbao and a special Sweet & Sour fried Chicken from North-East China in bustling ChinaTown.
Learn about tales of Dubai's past, from its ancient roots to its modern-day marvels, thanks to the vision of some enlightened Sheiks.
We'll also have the chance to explore the enchanting Souk al Bahar, and marvel at the magical Fountain Show.
As with all our tours there is our Secret Dish (Michelin recommended).
End with a traditional Emirati coffee and succulent local dates.
Cocktail in The Clouds at Burj Khalifa
A glass of Lebanese Wine

## Why have we chosen Burj Khalifa, Dubai mall and Souk al Bahar?
These landmarks epitomize the essence of Dubai's cosmopolitan charm and offer a myriad of experiences that cater to a diverse range of interests.
Burj Khalifa symbolizes Dubai's ethos of innovation and modernity. As the tallest building in the world, it provides visitors with unparalleled panoramic views of the city, offering a glimpse into Dubai's breathtaking skyline and view.
Adjacent to Burj Khalifa lies Dubai Mall, a sprawling complex that encapsulates the city's penchant for luxury, entertainment, and gastronomy. Boasting an array of upscale boutiques, gourmet restaurants, and entertainment attractions, Dubai Mall offers a sensory feast for visitors, making it an ideal destination for culinary exploration.
Souk al Bahar will then add a touch of tradition and cultural authenticity to our tours. Nestled amidst the modernity of downtown Dubai, this traditional marketplace offers a glimpse into Dubai's rich heritage, with artisans crafting exquisite wares against the backdrop of stunning waterfront views. It provides a unique juxtaposition of old-world charm and contemporary luxury, adding depth and diversity to our tour experience.
Our guided experiences provide insider knowledge and recommendations from locals, ensuring an unforgettable journey through the heart of Dubai. With their central locations and accessibility, these landmarks serve as ideal starting points for discovering the richness of Dubai's offerings, making them the perfect choice for our tours.

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
Welcome to the Secret Food Tour in Zurich! Join us as we embark on a culinary adventure through the heart of this vibrant city, where you'll taste your way through the best local spots and discover the authentic flavours of Swiss cuisine.
Discover mouthwatering cheeses at a local deli, local spirits and liqueurs, historic restaurants, street food, and indulgent chocolates from Switzerland's oldest chocolate shop. We'll guide you through hidden gems and iconic eateries that showcase Zurich's rich food culture.
Whether you're a local or a visitor, this tour promises a delicious journey filled with unique dishes, local stories, and the best of Swiss hospitality. Come hungry and ready to explore!
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/Zurich/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Start the tour with a classic Swiss breakfast with medicinal properties.
Indulge in a rich Swiss hot chocolate at a Grand Cafe.
Join the locals at an iconic sausage shop for a Zurich classic served with sauerkraut and punchy mustard.
Discover all things cheesy at a local cheesemonger, tasting some of the finest Swiss cheeses paired with hay schnaps and their legendary cheese tart.
Dine on decadent Alpine classics like Rösti with a creamy stew paired with local wine.
Sample fondue bites dipped in a Kirsch liqueur.
Visit a local bottle shop to taste historic gin.
Sample a selection of the finest Swiss chocolates at the oldest chocolate shop in the city.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in addition to the ones already served on the tour:
Local Swiss beer
Alpine schnaps
[ADD UPGRADED DRINKS PACKAGE](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/zurich/upgraded-drinks-package/)

Meeting Spot
We will meet in between the Water Fountain and Dunkin Donuts at Bellevue tram stop Address: Bellevuepl. 2, 8001 Zürich, Switzerland. The guide will hold an orange umbrella, so they’re easy to spot. If you have any questions, please [email us](mailto:zurich@secrettours.com).
Bellevuepl. 2, 8001 Zürich, Switzerland
[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/mAoKRcNJiwPt9Qw46)
Monday - Sunday 10.30 a.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## WHY ZÜRICH ALTSTADT?
Zurich's Old Town, Altstadt, is the perfect setting for our Secret Food Tour, as its narrow, cobbled streets are steeped in centuries of history and culture. This historic part of the city blends tradition with modern vibrancy, making it the ideal backdrop to explore Zurich’s culinary heritage.
Here, you’ll not only savour the city’s most famous dishes, but also discover hidden culinary treasures tucked away in quaint, centuries-old buildings.
The Old Town’s rich history and lively atmosphere makes it the perfect place to taste Zurich's flavours while learning the stories behind them.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/zurich/food-tours-zurich/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we give details on our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! If you are looking for something special or if you are organising an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email.
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the number of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (who doesn't?) We offer an [UPGRADE DRINKS package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/zurich/upgraded-drinks-package/) that gives you 2 drinks on top of what's already on the tour.
Where is the meeting spot?
We will meet in between the Water Fountain and Dunkin Donuts at Bellevue tram stop Address: Bellevuepl. 2, 8001 Zürich, Switzerland. Your guide will be in front of the statue, holding an orange umbrella.
If you have any questions, please [email us](mailto:zurich@secrettours.com) .
We begin our tours at 10:30am, and suggest you arrive 10 minutes earlier. If you have any questions, please email us.
Where the tour will end?
The tour will end near Parradplatz. Adress Paradeplatz, 8001 Zürich, Switzerland , an ideal location to go anywhere in the town or the region.
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please contact us at [zurich@secrettours.com](mailto:zurich@secrettours.com) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate children. Please contact us at [zurich@secrettours.com](mailto:zurich@secrettours.com)
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem, we have different options. Just inform our guide before the tour starts.
Is there enough food during your tour?
Think of the tour as a tasting menu. As we travel you get many delicious treats that comprise a meal with dessert. But there is a lot of food, you will not be hungry after the tour.
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be approximately 24 minutes of walking, but it is not too difficult and is broken up between food stops. During the duration of the tour we will walk nearly 0.7 miles. Our guide walks at the pace of the tour members, you will never be rushed, but if you are on a wheelchair, write to us before booking.
How many people max are on this tour?
Since this is a VIP experience, we take 10 people maximum. If you are more than 10 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to accommodate you.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drinks are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel and we cannot run the tour due to extreme weather for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Zurich?

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
Embark on our exclusive Secret Food Tour: Montreal - Mile End, a journey through Montreal's hippest foodie neighborhood. Immerse yourself in a culinary exploration strategically located at the crossroads of diverse cultures, allowing you to savour the city's finest culinary offerings.
As you indulge in the mouthwatering delights, uncover the captivating stories behind some of Montreal's most iconic architectural gems as you immerse yourself in their majestic beauty.
Join us on this remarkable adventure, where every bite and every step will reveal the hidden treasures of Montreal's remarkable food scene and captivating cultural heritage. It's a journey you won't want to miss!
### SEE ITINERARY
Your guide will meet you on the outskirts of Montreal's artsy Mile End neighborhood, where you'll kick things off at a beloved neighbourhood counter — a passion project born from friendship, where everything is homemade and the sandwiches have quietly become some of the best in the city.
After a leisurely stroll down Parc Avenue, you'll enjoy our delicious Secret Dish — a savory pastry bite straight from the Mediterranean, with a twist you won't see coming. A truly unique and remarkable bite that will take you by delightful surprise!
Moving on, no visit to Montreal would be complete without sampling one of the city's namesake bagels — a fresh Montreal bagel, still warm from the wood-fired oven, served with cream cheese.
Did you know that street gnocchi was a thing? In Montreal it is! From a legendary family-run walk-up window, you'll taste pillowy homemade gnocchi smothered in a rich, slow-cooked tomato sauce — served piping hot in a classic takeout container.
After which, you'll dig into a classic poutine: crispy fries, squeaky cheese curds, and a rich gravy, piled high and absolutely unapologetic.
Fall/Winter: And to wrap things up, you'll sip authentic Italian coffee on a beautiful terrasse at a historic café while indulging in a delicious hand-piped cannoli.
Spring/Summer: And to wrap things up, cool down with a scoop of artisan ice cream from one of Mile End's most beloved seasonal spots — the perfect sweet ending to a flavour-packed afternoon.
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances.

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Toronto, you’ll explore two of the city's most iconic and authentic neighborhoods - St. Lawrence and the Distillery District. Lose yourself in the storied halls of the St. Lawrence Market, a must-visit destination for foodies that was established in 1803 and is one of the city's oldest landmarks. Here, you will taste some of Toronto's best culinary offerings and immerse yourself in the local food culture.
From there, you’ll venture onto the Distillery District, a pedestrian-only area that boasts charming cobblestone streets and beautiful Victorian-era buildings. This district was once the largest distillery in the world and has since been transformed into a hub for arts, culture, and entertainment.
After the tour, you'll have had the opportunity to explore local galleries, boutiques, and artisan shops, as well as sample some of Toronto's finest local delicacies. Get ready to discover the hidden treasures that make Toronto a foodie and culture lover's paradise.
### SEE ITINERARY
Our journey starts at the St. Lawrence Market, Toronto’s historic marketplace dating back over 200 years.
First up, we’ll visit a pub known for making one of the best versions of the "peameal bacon sandwich", also known as Canadian bacon.
Then we'll visit a local fish market, where a delicious surprise awaits - our Secret Dish.
Next, you'll indulge in a popular no-bake dessert whose origin is shrouded in mystery.
After that, we’ll walk over for a taste of the rich Italian roots that exist not only in Toronto but all of Canada, where you'll enjoy a comforting trio of Alfredo, Pesto & Tomato Sauce Tortellini - a local favorite loaded with rich flavor.
Then we’ll head into the historic Distillery District to visit a local craft brewery, where you’ll devour a delicious serving of classic poutine - crispy fries topped with squeaky cheese curds and savory gravy, the ultimate Canadian comfort food.
And for a great end, you'll devour the mouth-watering goodness of a classic Canadian pastry, the famous Butter Tart.

## WHY THE ST. LAWRENCE MARKET?
As Toronto's oldest market, the St. Lawrence Market's property has had a market as early as 1803. The building itself has many of its original structures still in place from 1845. We chose this spot as our main focal point on our tour because it still remains a place for history, culture and is a part of Toronto's identity.
The area that was built around this market is reflective of how old and new can coexist in cuisine, architecture and history. This is the best place to discover some of Toronto's finest cuisine at the same time as learn more about the city's fascinating history!

## WHY JORDAAN AREA?
The Jordaan area has a long history of assimilating merchants, sailors, workers, and immigrants. This open arm atmosphere combined with our quaint streets, and small canals named after flowers, have created a unique area within Amsterdam. Here, families have stayed for generations, and they speak a unique dialect of their own. Another treasure to spring out of this historic enclave is our traditional Dutch folk music, "smartlap". In addition, the Jordaan area is connected to one of the most important canals in Amsterdam. As with many areas within the Netherlands, changes are ongoing. Jordaan has been able to balance its rich cultural history with the changes that time brings, so visitors can enjoy lively bars and restaurants along with smaller, quaint, traditional businesses, all within the same winding streets.
[\\

title: vancouver/food-tours-vancouver
text:
Gastown is one of the most stylish neighbourhoods in the world and the first settlement of Vancouver. Its cute, European style streets make you feel like you are in a different country. Best yet, the trendy shops, bars, nightclubs and restaurants bring a unique culture to the area that can entertain anybody. Learn about the first original settlement of Vancouver and how it almost didn't survive to tell the tale on multiple occasions. Discover its rough and rocky past and how it has turned the corner to become a hip and trendy area with many attractions for both locals and tourists. Come see this area for yourself and enjoy some of the delicacies that Vancouver is so well known for. Vancouver's large Asian population and proximity to the ocean, has resulted in it being home to the most authentic and freshest sushi in North America.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/vancouver/food-tours-vancouver/#BOOKING)

## What you'll do
[Tour disponible en español](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/paris/chocolate-and-pastry-tour-es/)
On our Secret Food Tour: Paris - Pastries & Chocolates, you'll enjoy a variety of classic French desserts and sweets. Indulge in an explosion of sweet treats from artisanal chocolates, crêpes, and delicious macarons to award-winning pastries and the creamiest ice creams.
We have two areas for you to choose from, both are equally delicious with mouthwatering pastries and chocolates. You can choose between the following districts: **Montmartre** or **Saint-Germain**.
### SEE ITINERARY
MONTMARTRE
We’ll start with a lovely fluffy meringue tasting.
Then, we’ll meet an artisan chocolatier and you'll enjoy some of the best chocolates Paris has to offer.
Next is a classic of the French sweet street food culture, the crêpe, served in a variety of flavours.
In the summertime, you’ll be treated to an ice cream as a refreshing bite, while the rest of the year a freshly baked pastry or chocolates.
One cannot come to Paris and not have an éclair. It’s an absolute must!
Last but not least, as you are nearing a famous monument in the area, you’ll enjoy macarons accompanied by either a hot or an iced cocoa.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish, which in this case has been freshly baked for you.
SAINT-GERMAIN
We’ll start with a freshly baked ‘welcome treat’.
We’ll try a macaron from the "Picasso of the Macaron".
Then, we’ll meet an artisan chocolatier and you'll enjoy some of the best chocolates Paris has to offer.
Next is a classic French pastry: choux à la crème.
We’ll then stop at a renowned bakery for its shortbread cookies.
Last but not least, as you are nearing the end of the tour we’ll have the original CBS - Salted Butter Caramels - waiting for you.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.

title: SEE ITINERARY
text:
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll start our food tour with a traditional welcome sweet.
Then, we'll visit one of the oldest salumerias in the city to learn more about the local meat products and famous pastas.
You'll enjoy Parmigiano Reggiano, prosciutto di Parma, mortadella di Bologna, and many more regional cold cuts in one of the oldest osterias in the world, accompanied by a glass of our local wine.
Next you will taste fresh, handmade pasta made just for us at a local trattoria with a glass of local wine.
Then we will visit the main local food market, the Quadrilatero, and we'll enjoy a tasting of different types of balsamic vinegars from Modena to discover how this amazing tradition is still deeply rooted in this region.
And to end the tour on a sweet note, we will finish our culinary adventure with one of the best traditional artisanal gelato in the city.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.

## WHY THE QUADRILATERO?
We selected the Quadrilatero neighbourhood because it is the vibrant heart of Bologna. Built in the Middle Ages, it's nestled inside the oldest set of the city’s walls, right next to the old Roman road connecting the East to the West. This area is the place to be for foodies in search of the authentic flavours of the city.
Quadrilatero has an ancient tradition of housing most of the craft guilds within it. Jewelers, butchers, delicatessens, greengrocers, bakeries, and traditional trattorias are all found in this historic area.
Furthermore, next to the Quadrilatero lies an area with hidden canals, historic buildings, towers, and statues. On our tour we will guide you off the beaten paths, show you unexpected sights, and tell you amazing stories about the origins and traditions of this city.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/bologna/food-tours-bologna/#BOOKING)

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
[Tour disponible en español](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/paris/paris-le-marais-es/)
On our Secret Food Tour: Paris - Le Marais, you'll enjoy a variety of classic French dishes, starting with a viennoiserie or croissant and freshly baked breads, then enjoying a classic croque-monsieur in the very restaurant where Jim Morrison spent some of his time in Paris. Next, you’ll taste French cheese paired with red and white wine. Visiting the Le Marais district also means seeing the Jewish Quarter of Paris and tasting some fantastic falafels. Then, off to a world of sweetness for some incredible chocolates and colourful macarons, all topped off with another dessert to finish in style.
Our tour starts at the bustling heart of Le Marais near the famous rue de Rivoli, one of the main streets of the city. During the tour, we will walk through the old streets of this district, cross the Place des Vosges, and see some of the oldest medieval houses of Paris. You will also witness how the locals live in this part of Paris and how the city's history and Jewish influence has shaped its gastronomy.
### SEE ITINERARY
First, you will taste an authentic French croissant or viennoiserie and learn their respective stories.
Then, we’ll stop at a local bakery to collect some amazing French bread, before stopping at a cheese store to pick up some cheese for later.
Next, we’ll dine on a delicious croque-monsieur in Jim Morrison's favourite restaurant with wines and cheeses.
As we stroll through the streets of Le Marais, you’ll see countless reminders of the nobility’s power during the Renaissance, before arriving in the Jewish district - the backbone of Le Marais - where you will indulge in freshly made falafel.
Then, we’ll go to an amazing macaron and chocolate shop, where quality meets refinement.
And finally we’ll finish our culinary journey with dessert.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.

We chose the Le Marais district for one of our Paris food tours because you can't go to Paris and skip Le Marais!
Here, you will feel a unique atmosphere and enjoy the beauty of medieval Paris. Le Marais is made up of many outstanding buildings of historic and architectural importance, is multiculturally rich, and is ahead of its time when it comes to fashion and food. title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Paris - Montmartre, you will discover the true spirit of this breathtaking neighbourhood. When Parisians buy food, they head to high-end boutiques to select the best ingredients for their meals. We’ll do the same, tasting exquisite samples of food along the way.
Your local guide will share the history behind the gastronomy, the area, and the local shops, as they purchase the best cheeses, fine wines, delicious cured meats, as well as mouthwatering crêpes and fresh pastries. And of course our Secret Dish! We'll finish our route at a secret location to enjoy our meal like the locals.

We'll then stop in a boulangerie where you'll sample classic French breads and learn everything about them.

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
Join us on the Secret Food Tour: Austin and let our expert guides and your taste buds take you on a culinary adventure through the bustling streets of Downtown Austin, located in the heart of the live music capital of the world. Discover the flavors of Texas as you indulge in a variety of dishes, including the famous BBQ brisket and Instagram-worthy breakfast tacos, among many other mouthwatering options. And of course, we have a surprise Secret Dish to unveil. Quench your thirst with unique and exciting drinks at select stops, or upgrade to our thoughtfully curated Upgraded Drinks Package for an even more elevated experience.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll meet outside of Fareground, downtown Austin's beautifully modern food hall.
First up, you'll dig into some Central Texas pit-style BBQ brisket served with the best pinto beans ever.
Next, you'll enjoy a breakfast taco, a local favourite with a cult-like following.
On our stroll, we'll say "howdy" to Willie Nelson, an Austin icon, and pass by where Austin City Limits is held.
Afterwards, we will walk through the Historic Warehouse District of Downtown Austin taking in the Historic Buildings and the Iconic Tower that started Austin's tall building boom.
Then it's time for a cool down with a smoothie made with local Texas wildflower honey.
After several great bites, we'll take a stroll down Congress Ave to see and talk about some of our protected historic buildings.
And finally, as if you weren’t already full, we are right back where we started a Modern Food Hall once known as a Bank Office, we'll round things out with a classic creation, given a culinary twist and made just for our tour.
Upgrade your Secret Food Tour: Austin, experience with two drink selections chosen to pair with the food you'll be served. You must be 21 and older and have a valid ID.
Celis White (Witbier)
Cantero Margarita
Cinnamon Still Austin Bourbon Cocktail

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Austin - South Congress, you'll escape the hustle and bustle of Downtown to discover a living, breathing Texan landmark.
Our experienced guides will take you on an eclectic journey through Austin's vibrant and unique dining, nightlife, and shopping district the locals call "SoCo." On your adventure, you're likely to stumble upon delicious diner favorites, savory street tacos, and refreshing sweet treats. Throughout the experience, you'll see firsthand why this town is so fabulously "weird" and learn why we want to keep it that way! South Congress isn't just any old street in Austin, it's the artery that leads straight to the heart of Texas.
Food will be served with water when available. Or if you prefer, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/austin/south-congress-upgraded-drinks-package/) is available for purchase.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin in front of Austin's most famous mural, a testament to the city's creative and expressive spirit.
First up, you'll delight in a specialty iced coffee drink with a cult-like following to help you cool down in that hot Texan sun.
Next, you'll discover why Austin has been named the #1 taco city in America as you chow down on authentic Mexican street taco with Lebanese roots.
After which, it's time to learn what the locals actually order at the city's hippest pizza joint.
On our walk, you'll discover firsthand how "weird" our city really is as you pass by the eclectic shops and colorful murals that make up this quirky artistic enclave.
Of course, no visit to SoCo would be complete without stopping by the most amazing candy shop the world has ever known.
And finally, relax in a one of a kind diner that is sure to get your gears turning with a wonderfully southern treat - a Chocolate Chip Pecan Cookie!
As with all our tours, there's also our delicious Secret Dish.

## WHY SOUTH CONGRESS?
South Congress (aka "SoCo") isn't just an iconic street in the middle of Austin, it's a bonafide Texas landmark. This neighborhood is known for its quirky boutiques, colorful murals, and iconic eateries, making it a must-visit destination that perfectly embodies the city's unique spirit of keeping things "weird." It's also home to some of the city's most famous landmarks, including the viral "I love you so much." mural at Jo's, and a constant supply of live music to give Dirty Sixth a run for its money.
So, whether you're a music lover, a fashionista, or a foodie at heart, South Congress promises to be an unforgettable experience, providing a snapshot of Austin's dynamic and ever-evolving charm.

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour in Boston’s North End, you'll savor the freshest seafood and the famous Italian dishes that have been a staple in the North End for the past 100 years. As you explore the vibrant culinary scene, you will learn about the city's 400-year history, one bite at a time.
On this culinary tour, you'll sample famous clam chowder, a fresh lobster roll, delightful Arancini, a classic Chicken Parmesan sandwich, and a mouth-watering dessert. Plus, our exclusive Secret Dish!
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin our journey exploring the Blackstone Block Historic District where you'll enjoy New England clam chowder in one of the oldest restaurants in America!
Next, you'll discover the secrets of the Boston Public Market, an urban food hall and marketplace where the locals shop. And speaking of secrets, your guide will show you how to bypass the long lines to get the best lobster roll in town!
Now it's time to venture into the historic North End, Boston's very own Little Italy.
Don't forget to say "hi!" to Paul Revere and other American legends as your guide leads you through parts of the Freedom Trail.
And, of course, a visit to the North End wouldn't be complete without indulging in the quintessential Italian pizza – a perfect balance of crispy perfection and mouthwatering melted cheese
And, finally, conclude your adventure learning all about Boston's legendary Cannoli Wars while sampling one of the city's best.
As with all our tours, there's our delicious Secret Dish.
COLONIAL ALE
DOWNEAST “ORIGINAL BLEND” CIDER
ESPRESSO MARTINI

## WHY THE HISTORIC NORTH END?
With nearly 100 restaurants and bakeries nestled into just 0.36 square miles, let your local guide lead you to the North End's hidden gems.
Walk in the footsteps of American legends; Samuel Adams, Paul Revere, Ben Franklin, John Hancock, John F Kennedy, and even some infamous characters like Charles Ponzi.

Our tour starts in front of the Port of Call Food + Brew Hall, where we'll kick off a delicious exploration of Charleston's vibrant food scene.
Next, you'll dig into some South Carolina-style BBQ, believed to be the oldest barbeque in the world!
As you continue the tour, you'll take a leisurely stroll through the Historic Charleston City Market and delve into the history of the African slave trade's impact on the local cuisine. Discover how classic ingredients such as benne seeds and collard greens were introduced to the local diet and have since become a staple of Lowcountry cuisine.
Of course, no visit to the Lowcountry would be complete without sampling some of the freshest seafood our city offers!
Satisfy your sweet tooth with a special handmade pecan cluster crafted from scratch using locally sourced ingredients from Lowcountry farmers, purveyors, and chefs. It's creamy, flavorful, and simply irresistible!

## WHY THE FRENCH QUARTER?
Charleston is one of the oldest settlements in the United States, and walking through the French Quarter feels like stepping back in time. The city's preservation society goes to great lengths to ensure Charleston looks nearly the same as it did 300 years ago! During the tour, you will see Charleston's single-house architecture, learn why it is known as the Holy City, and discover other fun facts that make Charleston unique.
The French Quarter showcases some of Charleston's most historic churches and buildings. It also features highly rated restaurants, the city market, and fun boutiques. The French Quarter is truly a mix of Charleston's rich history and promising future.

### SEE ITINERARY
COLONIAL ALE
Brewed only in Boston and exclusively available on draft at the city's oldest restaurant, this extraordinary beer was crafted in reminiscence of the style of ales that colonists were drinking in 1630. It is traditionally made using the time honored four vessel brewing process and includes an ingredient used by the earliest American beer makers - blackstrap molasses - which adds a natural sweetness to the ale's iconic bold hoppy notes.
DOWNEAST “ORIGINAL BLEND” CIDER
Fermented at the Downeast Cider House in Boston this hard cider is juicy and sweet! Smooth and refreshing, perfectly balanced. Like the cider, you grew up with drinking!
ESPRESSO MARTINI

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Charleston - Upper King St. Brunch, you'll discover a thriving, contemporary community of restaurants, bars, and coffee shops located just north of the Historic District.  Our experienced guides will take you on a brunch-fueled journey down Charleston's second-most historically and architecturally significant street, one so important that it was named after the King of England!
Along the way, you'll indulge in savory and decadent brunch creations that help showcase the kind of culinary innovation that shapes modern Southern cuisine. And don't even get us started on all of the gorgeous historic homes hiding behind every corner! Upper King isn't just any old street, it's a vibe, and it's one best served with baked goods and drinks!
Food will be served with water when available. Or if you prefer, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/charleston/upper-king-st-brunch-upgraded-drinks-package/) is available for purchase.
### SEE ITINERARY
We begin on the north end of Upper King Street, once the main route into colonial Charles Towne and named in honor of King Charles II of England.
Start your journey with a sweet-and-savory Southern classic, a surprise bite designed to set the tone for the delicious experience ahead.
Our second stop is a truly Southern staple! You’ll taste the crispy, tangy flavor of fried green tomatoes, followed by shrimp and grits and pimento cheese, served hot and full of regional flair.
Enjoy this trio of Lowcountry favorites at a local tavern frequented by the cast of Bravo’s Southern Charm. Drink Upgrade: Pair it with a bold Bloody Mary, a true brunch icon.
Paying homage to Charleston’s confectionery traditions, let’s try a Pecan Cluster made specially to enhance this journey and the sweet tooth's guests.
Now it's time to cool down with a refreshing Southern Sweet Tea, a beloved regional drink, as you meander past the gorgeous homes and towering cathedrals that line nearby streets named after some of our historic residents.
Are you still hungry? We hope so! Next, indulge in one of Oprah’s Favorite Things: the cult-favorite hot little biscuit that has become a Charleston legend.
End your tour on a sweet note with moist banana bread and sea salt butter, paired perfectly with a crisp glass of Santome Prosecco (Drink Upgrade).

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Paris - Notre Dame "The Heart of Paris", we’ll take you on a culinary journey across the birthplace of the French capital. On this tour, you'll enjoy a variety of magnificent classic French food, such as freshly baked viennoiseries, some of the best cheeses you have ever tasted, savoury pastries and tarts, scrumptious desserts, and of course, our Secret Dish.
During our tour, you will walk through medieval streets passing by many famous landmarks, such as the Wall of Philip Augustus, the Notre Dame, the Panthéon, and more! You will realise how the locals live in this part of Paris and how its history has influenced its gastronomy.

First, you’ll be welcomed with a freshly baked ‘welcome bite’.
Then, you will cross the Seine River and reach Île Saint-Louis, the cradle of the ancient Roman city of Lutecia, grabbing a macaron on the go, just because it's Paris and we can.
Crossing the Seine River again you will reach Île de la Cité, where the Notre Dame Cathedral stands, before using ‘Le Petit Pont’, the smallest of the 37 bridges along the Seine, to reach the Latin Quarter where a cheesemonger and bakery await us.
After collecting more food we will reach our secret location for a well deserved feast filled with flavours, aromas, stories about French culture and gastronomy, and of course, wine.

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Chicago, you'll enjoy a variety of foods with culinary influences from the people who built this city.
You'll experience all the city's biggest food hits, many dishes with roots dating back over 150 years. This moving feast incorporates a walk through some of the city's world renowned architecture and public art in the famed Chicago Loop District. Experience our famous deep dish pizza, taste the amazing Italian beef sandwich, enjoy gourmet popcorn, and lots of other treats. Plus, there is our mouth-watering secret dish that you can only find out about on the day of the tour!
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin our journey visiting the Harold Washington library next as you bask in the majesty of the city's Postmodern cathedral of books.
Did someone say Oprah? At our next stop, you'll discover one of her "favorite things" - freshly popped gourmet popcorn!
After which, it's time to devour a classic Chicago-style hotdog topped with all the fixings. (No ketchup allowed!)
Sears or Willis? It depends on who you ask! Discover our delicious Secret Dish inside what used to be the world's tallest building.
Of course, no visit to Chicago would be complete without sampling our famous Italian Beef sandwich.
It’s now time for a slice of deep dish pizza, a marvel of flavor and engineering.
And, finally, did you know that the brownie was invented here? At our last stop you'll hear about its creation and indulge in a modern version of this tasty Chicago treat!

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Cincinnati, you'll explore three distinct historic neighborhoods all connected by a modern electric streetcar.
Our experienced guides will take you on an amazing foodie adventure through three of Cincinnati's most iconic neighborhoods while traveling like the locals do - by streetcar! On your journey, you'll sample handmade specialties at family-owned businesses in the Findlay Market, indulge in celebrity favorites and viral social media stars in trendy Over-The-Rhine, and learn why the "Queen City" sings down at The Banks. Throughout the experience, you'll see firsthand why Mark Twain once said that if the world were to end, he'd want to be right here when it did! Cincinnati was once the largest westernmost city in America, earning it the nickname "Queen City of the West," and memories of its regal past are waiting to be discovered around every street corner.
Food will be served with water when available. Or if you prefer, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/cincinnati/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available for purchase.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin at Cincinnati's historic Findlay Market, one of the oldest surviving and continuously operating public markets in the country.
At our first stop, you’ll enjoy a sizzling slice of Goetta — a Cincinnati favorite made from pork, beef, oats, and spices. Brought by German immigrants, it’s a hearty tradition passed down through generations.
After which, you'll indulge in a decadent treat delicately hand-crafted by one of our city's most beloved chocolatiers.
Now it's time for our first of two rides on The Connector, Cincinnati's modern electric streetcar.
As we explore the trendy Over-The-Rhine district, you'll hear stories of our proud German heritage while cooling down with a scoop of Oprah's favorite ice cream.
Of course no visit to Cincinnati would be complete without sampling our famous chili, a local legend with surprising Mediterranean roots.
And finally, it's time to raise a toast with a warm Belgian waffle as you bask in the majesty of the John A. Roebling Suspension Bridge, engineering prototype for the Brooklyn Bridge in New York.
As with all our tours, there's also our delicious Secret Dish.

## WHY THE STREETCAR?
Cincinnati is made up of dozens of vibrant neighborhoods due to its rapid growth and the influx of immigrant groups arriving during the American Westward Expansion. Each of these historic enclaves has its own distinct character, history, and attractions. To visit only one is not really to experience Cincinnati! Luckily, we have The Connector, a modern electric streetcar that not only allows us to explore three of the Queen City's crown jewels in the same amount of time it would normally take to visit just one but also serves as a tribute to our eclectic public transportation past, which includes canals, funiculars, and even an abandoned subway system!
So, hop on board and get ready for a foodie adventure through a true slice of Americana while traveling like the locals do—we're sure you'll enjoy the ride!
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/cincinnati/food-tours-cincinnati/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) we offer an [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/cincinnati/upgraded-drinks-package/) that gives you a specialty coffee drink (hot or iced, depending on the season), a local craft beer or hard cider, and a refreshing local hard seltzer.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/cincinnati/food-tours-cincinnati/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
The meeting spot is in front of Jane’s at Findlay Market at Elm & Elder, 45202 Elm St, Cincinnati, OH 45202. Your guide will be there with an orange umbrella and a big smile!
Where the tour will end?
Our tour ends at the Sing the Queen City Sign at The Banks, located at 100 Theodore M. Berry Way, Cincinnati, OH 45202. .
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please contact us at [cincinnati@secrettours.com](mailto:cincinnati@secrettours) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome to our tour if of an appropriate age to enjoy.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
Think of the tour as a tasting menu. As we travel you get many delicious treats that comprise a meal with dessert. But there is a lot of food, you will not be hungry after the tour.
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 45 minutes walking but it's not too difficult and there are no hills or stairs. If you walk slowly, then our guide will walk slowly as well.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drinks are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel and we cannot run the tour due to extreme weather for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Cincinnati?

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
Howdy y’all! On our Dallas Food Tour in Deep Ellum, you’ll experience true Southern hospitality and discover why our cuisine has always been a culinary melting pot of different cultures. Our guides will lead you through the art and music-filled streets that make up the foodie paradise that is Deep Ellum and you'll sample our famous BBQ brisket, award-winning Texas chilli, buttery handmade biscuits, and lots more. And don't forget our delicious Secret Dish, which can only be described as "downright addicting!"
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/dallas/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin on Main Street in the heart of a former warehouse district turned funky nightlife, restaurant, and shopping area.
First up, you'll dig into some "cowboy style" BBQ, served with all the fixin's.
Next, you'll savor a cup of Texas Style Chili from one of Deep Ellum’s oldest restaurants
On our walk, we'll explore some of the murals and walk about Deep Ellum the Dallas neighborhood for all things urban art.
Are you ready for a flavor explosion? At our next stop, our delicious Secret Dish is sure to put a little fusion of flavor in your mouth.
And finally, it's pie o'clock somewhere as Dallas' favorite dessert meets a beloved Southern Pecan Pie
