### SEE ITINERARY
Cadillac - white wine
This family business has been passed on from mother to daughter since 1881. Recently awarded the Gold Medal Gilbert & Gaillard 2018, the Cadillac from le Domaine du Vic will be a fantastic opportunity for us to show you that sweet wines are not just for desert.
Liqueur de génépi or cognac
A cognac from Maison Augier. House established in 1643. In 1643, Philippe Augier creates his own house of Cognac. The Augier family will then continue to produce eaux-de-vie from the best vintages, in keeping with their own constant quality requirements. The family has been scrupulously following the traditional methods and production from Cognac region, in the Southwest of France.
OR
Liqueur de Génépi. Elixir from the Alps. Since 1821, Dolin has been making this amazing "Coeur de Génépi" in the greatest respect for nature, the richness of its flavour comes from the slow maceration of genepi plants in fine alcohol, the rigorous selection of plants allow the elaboration of their liquor rich in aromas, where only the core of the distillation is collected.

## WHY MALÁ STRANA AREA?
We selected Malá Strana as our tour location because it is one of the greenest and most historically interesting parts of our city. Originally founded as the "New Town" in the 13th century, it was created as a royal town and the King invited expelled German craftsmen and merchants to settle here. During its turbulent history this area was destroyed by Hussites in 1419, and burned to the ground again in the 16th century. What we have today is a city rebuilt from the ashes in a Renaissance style with some of the most stunning baroque architecture in the world. This historic district set against the Vltava River is filled with hidden churches, palaces, narrow streets, and beautiful parks. Czech culture was literally created here, famous writers and composers lived in this district and used the area for inspiration to create masterworks that have stood the test of time.

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Denver - Downtown, you'll enjoy a variety of classic local foods.
Our experienced guides will take you on a culinary adventure along the historic streets made famous by prospectors, miners, and early American entrepreneurs during the Colorado Gold Rush. On your journey, you'll hear thrilling tales of confidence men, saloon girls, and bank robbers while sampling mouthwatering local specialties that pay tribute to both our proud past as well as our thriving modern food scene. Today, Denver is one of the fastest growing cities in America, and we cannot wait to share with you the incredibly rich and ethnically diverse culinary world that awaits in The Mile High City.
**Food will be served with water when available. Or if you prefer, a thoughtfully curated** [**Upgraded Drinks Package**](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/denver/upgraded-drinks-package/) **is available for purchase.**
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin right in the middle of Denver's historic Ballpark District, home to Coors Field and the Colorado Rockies.
First up, you'll dig into a gourmet smoked sausage made with locally-sourced elk, a favorite of the late Anthony Bourdain.
Next, you'll learn what exactly green chili is and why we put it on pretty much everything!
As we make our way into Lower Downtown (aka "LoDo"), you'll hear how wild the Wild West really was and see what remains of our classiest bordello.
Next you'll indulge in a Colorado menu stable, a Bison hamburger!
After, you'll explore local architectural icons like the Ice House, Union Station, and the Oxford Hotel as you stroll through the oldest part of The Mile High City.
To bring our culinary journey to a memorable close, we will offer  a  delicate and satisfying sweet bite of cheese, fig and ruby port to conclude the entire event.
As with all our tours, there's also our secret dish.
Colorado Craft Beer
Wynkoop Flagship Draft
Red Sangria

As you walk in the footsteps of our storied history, you will also be walking past the many stories that make up the gleaming high rises that dot today's skyline, bringing the past and future together in perfect harmony. ## What you'll do
We'll guide you through the vibrant heart of Houston, Downtown, and unravel the secrets of its exceptional culinary landscape.
Houston Downtown is a melting pot of diverse cultures and flavors that truly sets it apart. The culinary scene in this bustling hub proudly wears the crown of being the most internationally influenced within the Lone Star State.
Houston Downtown is a melting pot of diverse cultures and flavors that truly sets it apart. The culinary scene in this bustling hub proudly wears the crown of being the most internationally influenced within the Lone Star State.
At the end of the tour, you’ll have sampled some of Houston’s most famous and delicious food and beverages, gaining a better understanding of our culture and traditions.
In Houston Downtown every bite promises a new dimension of taste and history, let us show you the Southern Hospitality!
We’ll end the tour in POST Market with a sweet treat.
If you want to perfect your experience, we offer a well-structured [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/houston/upgraded-drinks-package/), so all the food will be paired with quality drinks chosen just for you.

Before we start exploring this multicultural city, we’ll learn about the Clock Tower and an Iconic stop by an to Downtown Houston, Discover a traditional southern comfort delight, with boudin and bacon.
After we will explore the historic City Hall, a living testament to the city's transformation.
Indulge in Cajun flavor with a wonderful bite from the Bayou, a Shrimp Po'boy!
Recharge with Brisket Eggrolls, a unique Asian and Southern style fusion!
Walk along Main Street, absorbing its vibrant culture, street art and urban evolution.
Then stop by an excited new addition to Downtown Houston, an authentic Quesabirria Taco.

 12 people max

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Munich, immerse yourself in the heart of Bavarian culinary culture. Indulge in traditional Bavarian dishes like pretzels, weisswurst, and of course Munich’s legendary beers, while exploring historic beer halls and cellars where centuries-old brewing traditions come alive. Wander through a vibrant local market, full of the region's finest products, from artisanal cheeses to fresh baked goods. Along the way, you'll uncover the stories behind Munich’s culinary heritage, making this a truly authentic and delicious adventure. Prost!
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/munich/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
First you'll visit a cult classic café for their freshly baked Schmalznudel - a chewy, warm Munich doughnut.
Do as the locals do and tuck into a Bavarian breakfast of wheat beer, weisswurst, sweet mustard, and pretzels all before the church bells ring at noon!
Discover the must-try Munich street food Leberkäse from a famous butcher.
Graze your way through the Viktualian Market before enjoying a board of local cheeses, artisanal breads, and local smoked charcuterie, all paired with a honey wine.
Tour an iconic beerhall sample and uncover the fascinating story of Munich's brewing history over an ice cold beer.
Indulge in a fine homemade German dessert.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in
addition to the ones already served on the tour:
Kräuterlikör - A herbal mountain schnaps
Obst - A refreshing apple & pear schnaps

## WHY Munich’s Old Town?
We chose Munich’s Old Town for our Secret Food Tour because it’s the heart of Bavarian tradition and flavour. From the Hofbräuhaus to smaller local restaurants, from the Viktualian Market to street food stalls, this historic district is brimming with centuries-old architecture, iconic beer halls, and vibrant markets, offering the perfect setting to explore Munich’s rich culinary heritage.
From savouring traditional dishes to uncovering the stories behind them, the Old Town provides an authentic and unforgettable taste of Bavarian culture.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/munich/food-tours-munich/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we give details on our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! If you are looking for something special or if you are organising an event? Just email us at [Group & Private](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) Food tour and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email.
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the number of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) We offer a [upgraded drinks](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/munich/upgraded-drinks-package/) package that gives you 3 extra drinks.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/munich/food-tours-munich/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
We'll meet in front of Noah’s Pizzeria on the corner of Sebastien platz, Sebastianspl. 11, 80331 München, Germany
We will send you meeting place details before the day of the tour. The meeting spot is . Your guide will be carrying an Orange umbrella so will be easy to spot.
Where the tour will end?
The tour will end in MarienPlatz right in the city centre so it is easy to walk anywhere after or take public transport home.
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
You can always tell our guides of any food allergies or special dietary requirements. We will adapt the menu so you can fully enjoy the tour! If you have any doubts or concerns, feel free to email us at [munich@secrettours.com](mailto:munich@secrettours.com) before booking. We have had many vegetarians doing the tour with us and they all enjoyed it very much.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Yes, we can definitely accommodate children. Please contact us at [munich@secrettours.com](mailto:munich@secrettours.com) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your family.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem, we have different options. Just inform our host before the experience starts.
Is there enough food during your tour?
Think of the tour as a tasting menu. As we travel you get many delicious treats that comprise a meal with dessert. But there is a lot of food, you will not be hungry after the tour.
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do. We cannot promise but we will try our best to sort something out for you.
How many people max are on this tour?
Since this is a VIP experience, we take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to accommodate you.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own food?
Our host will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform us via [munich@secrettours.com](mailto:munich@secrettours.com) before booking and we will do our best to make you happy..
Where do your Ingredients come from?
The ingredients are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel and we cannot run the tour due to extreme weather for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Munich?

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Florence – Sant’Ambrogio, we’ll show you the most authentic side of Florence, as you eat, sip, and savour your way through the local market, taste olive oil, sample traditional salamis and cheeses paired with fine local wines, and more.
By the end of your tour, you'll have tasted some of Florence's most delicious dishes and gained a better understanding of our culture and history.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/florence/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll stop by one of the most beautiful cafés in the city to learn how local Florentines do breakfast.
We’ll go off the beaten track and visit the Sant'Ambrogio Market where Florentines buy local produce.
You’ll sample a variety of the Tuscan cheeses, meats, and hams that make the region so famous.
You'll then try Lampredotto - the legendary Florentine street food.
We’ll visit a local trattoria and dine on regional Tuscan pastas and soups.
Lastly, we'll cool off with a fresh gelato from an artisanal shop.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in addition to the ones already served on the tour:
Chianti - Italian red wine

Prosecco

## WHY SANTA CROCE AND SANT’AMBROGIO?
Santa Croce and Sant’Ambrogio are the only two districts where you can still find real Florentine people. Away from the museums and the crowds, you'll find a moment to relax, experience a still thriving atmosphere, and meet local people in their shops.
Santa Croce Basilica is one of the most important monuments in the history of both Florence and Italy. Also known as "the Temple of the Italian Glories", it's a must-see monument, and the perfect meeting point for our food tour. As you walk through the streets, you'll see how important this district has always been for both the city and the country - from its time as the leather district where Florentine families built fortunes as early as the 12th century, to later becoming a symbol of a unified Italy.
After this, we’ll continue towards the Sant'Ambrogio Market, which is full of locals buying and talking in their own colourful way. You'll try some of the freshest foods Florence has to offer, and meet proud locals who love their city and their culture. Eat and drink as the Florentines do on our Secret Food Tour: Florence - Sant’Ambrogio.
[\\

_If you purchase our Upgraded Drinks Packages on **MON-SAT**_
### SEE ITINERARY
Chianti - Italian red wine
Nothing is better than a glass of Chianti, crisp and fresh, perfect for pairing with the savory Florentine food. Among all the Tuscan red wines, it's the one that is meant to be drunk younger and not aged for long. In general, Chianti Classicos are described as medium-bodied wines with firm, dry tannins. The characteristic aroma is cherry but it can also carry nutty and floral notes as well.
Morellino di Scansano - Italian red wine
The wines are dry reds from the Sangiovese grape variety, known here as Morellino. They're produced in the rolling coastal hills around the medieval village of Scansano. Scansano's viticultural origins date back to Etruscan times, and the quality of the local wines has been documented for centuries. This style of Sangiovese differs from its northern brethren due to the ripeness levels it can achieve in southern Tuscany. The result is a rounder and more savory style of wine.

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Key West Old Town, we'll walk far from the tourist traps of Duval to show you the real city and its best food. You'll gain insight into how the diverse cultural influences of Key West have shaped the island into a unique destination that's globally renowned for its exceptional gastronomic experiences. Our local guides will take you on a journey to explore the history and culture of "The Key to the Caribbean," sharing their passion and passing down stories that have been told from generation to generation.
You'll discover the oral history of Key West's prosperity and how treasures transformed this small island into a worldwide attraction.
Enjoy traditional Key West bites, taste the freshness of ingredients that only a coastal city can offer, and uncover flavors you won't find anywhere else in the world. Plus there’s our amazing secret dish that you can only find about it on the day!
### SEE ITINERARY
We’ll begin our journey with a wonderful iced coffee, freshly brewed from hand-roasted beans, paired with a slice of delicious homemade cake.
Next, we’ll dive into our delicious Secret Dish, showcasing the incredible flavors of Caribbean cuisine—a significant influence in Key West.
Following that, you'll wander down Duval Street to a local haunt where you'll discover the island's best Cuban Cuisine - a truly exciting treat.
Now it is time to cool off with a rum tasting from one of the oldest distilleries in Key West.
From there, you'll indulge in conch fritters.
And to top it all off, you'll enjoy a local favorite: Key lime pie dipped in chocolate!

## What you'll do and Taste
Here at Secret Food Tours, we wanted to make the best wine tour. We have heard often, wine tasting seems a little intimidating. So, we have decided to take a lighter approach to wine pairing; more drinker friendly, with mouthwatering foods and some easy to remember techniques to make picking and pairing any wine super easy.
A few minutes from either the Moulin Rouge or Montmartre in the famous district of Pigalle, renowned for its bars and nightlife, our authentic 1870 wine cellar awaits you. There you will be introduced to simplified techniques of pairing your wines with food with live demonstrations.
Your host is a dedicated foodie who enjoys nothing more than sharing with you not just wine facts but also food and interesting stories, so through the pairings you will also share the French culture built around wine and the "guinguettes" of Paris' then suburb.
Like with all our tours, you will be served fine wines paired with a selection of delicious cured meat, cheese, fresh bread and even a macaron. Don’t forget our Secret Wine!
### SEE ITINERARY
One red wine from Savoy region
One white wine from the Loire Valley
One sweet white wine from Bordeaux
One crémant from Burgundy

## WHY MONTMARTRE?
We chose the Montmartre district for our Paris Wine Tour because of the area's historical link to the entertainment, nightlife and drinking culture. Until the French Revolution a defence wall surrounded the city, at each of its entrance gates, legions of open bars, the "Guinguettes" appeared, there, the locals would get outside of the city to slum it at the open bars, siping untaxed cheaper wines and dancing waltz on the air of the local bands. This has been widely depicted by Lautrec and the Impressionist movement. By joining us on this Wine Pairing experience we will share this atmosphere with you.

### SEE ITINERARY
Folie de l'Espinose - white wine
Made with the traditional method, its fine and elegant bubbles are produced with a settle mix of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir and Folie Blanche. Ideal for "Apéritif" or for a festive dessert, let this wonderful quality sparkling wine seduce you!
Cadillac Bordeaux Moelleux - white wine
This Bordeaux comes from a family business that has been passed on from mother to daughter since 1881. The Cadillac, from le Domaine du Vic, is a sweet wine that should be enjoyed with your meal, not just dessert.
Cognac
At the end of your tour you'll enjoy a 1st Cru of Cognac appellation aged in oak barrels for decades. After aging, The Maitre de Chai blends eaux-de-vie of different ages, to create a cognac with a harmony of unique flavors.

## WHY MONTMARTE?
The hill of Montmartre has always been known for its windmills and vineyards and in the 18th century, when Montmartre was just outside of the city of Paris, wine bars and restaurants began to flourish as they offered tax free beverages to thirsty citizens. With the flow of Parisians came the artists, with Picasso, Renoir, Dufy and many more all calling Montmartre home at one time or another, all helping to foster a culture and atmosphere not found anywhere else in the city.
While the buildings have replaced the vineyards, and the windmills have almost all but disappeared, the party atmosphere and spirit of community that has surrounded the artists, residents and visitors of the area for generations, still remains anchored to the sides of the hill.

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Venice, escape the crowds with your expert food guide as you explore local bars, restaurants, and cafés through the maze of Venice's charming streets and canals. Witness and taste the everyday life of the lagoon’s locals, with their unique charm and hospitality.
Discover the gastronomic history of the city enjoying little succulent cicchetti, paired with the one and only original Spritz, which was invented in the Veneto Region. The culinary tour will be enriched with a huge variety of specialities which will leave you with an unforgettable savoury memory of Venice. Smell the magic atmosphere and flavours that envelop the city, in a less crowded and more local way!
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/venice/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available
### SEE ITINERARY
We’ll start the tour visiting an ancient café and tasting a couple of traditional welcome cookies.
Next, you’ll have the privilege of trying the famous cicchetti - local tapas from Veneto - paired with the real Select Spritz while you have a romantic view of the canals in front of you.
We’ll then try Prosecco, the drink the area is famed for.
In a colourful open air market, we will delight your palate with a local meatball or typical tramezzini - Italian finger sandwich.
A traditional bacaro, Venetian tavern, will serve us a glorious main dish and Veneto’s famous polenta.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in addition to the ones already served on the tour:
Ombra - local wine
An extra glass of Select Spritz

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Los Angeles, you’ll be taken into the heart of Venice Beach to discover a lively and vibrant neighborhood with a rich foundation and of course, mouth-watering dishes. You'll experience firsthand what happens when great food meets culinary creativity.
We'll enter a community that fosters stunning street art, both on the main roads and weaving through the city neighborhoods. Venice Beach is a community that embraces diversity through art, culture, cuisine, and hospitality.
At the end of your tour, you'll have sampled some of Los Angeles' most delicious dishes and gained a better understanding of our culture and history.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin our journey taking in the fresh ocean breeze on the iconic Venice Fishing Pier.
First up, you'll enjoy what the locals call a surfer's breakfast, an antioxidant-rich smoothie bowl topped with granola, bee pollen, and hemp seeds.
Next, you'll enjoy a picturesque walk through Venice's famed canals as you discover that they aren't just in Italy!
After which, our delicious Secret Dish will show you that sometimes the tastiest things are plant-based.
At our next stop, you'll devour an authentic Argentinian Empanada as you admire the meatheads pumping iron at Muscle Beach.
And, of course, no visit to the California coast would be complete without a crispy fish taco right on the boardwalk.
And speaking of the boardwalk, make sure your phone is charged so you can snap some photos of the area's iconic graffiti walls or grab a video at one of the world's most famous skateparks.
But keep that phone handy because we'll be rounding things out with some totally #gramable quesabirria dipped in consomé.
Delight with a refreshing dish of shaved ice and Dole whip straight out of the Hawaiian Islands

 Skip the overpriced tourist traps

Also recommended by

 10 people max

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Milan, you’ll enjoy a vast variety of dishes and drinks that will not only showcase Milanese traditions, but also exemplify the city’s multiculturalism.
On our tour, we’ll take you around the Navigli area and explain why it’s so special. Then, an intriguing historical prelude will introduce you to the most typical dish in Milan: risotto. We'll have the privilege to eat it and also learn about the preparation modalities and the secrets behind this magnificent plate.
Furthermore, through food, we’ll show you why Milan is Italy’s most multicultural city, a magnet for people from across the country to move to. As a result, it’s the only Italian city where you can truly taste dishes from every region, from Sicilian and Calabrese to Neapolitan and Pugliese.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/milan/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Start your culinary journey with a true Lombard classic: a delicious risotto with Taleggio cheese and pears.
Explore the trendy and lively Navigli neighbourhood while tasting arancina, one of Italy’s most iconic street foods.
Pass by some of Milan’s oldest churches before trying a local pride: the unique Milanese-style pizza.
Make a delightful stop to enjoy a traditional Pasticciotto, golden and flaky pastry filled with rich, creamy custard.
Italian coffee with its special “cremina” will give you a boost of energy, just in time for our final sweet treat.
End on a high note with one of the city’s finest gelatos. Smooth, natural, and crafted with exceptional care.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish paired with a seasonal side.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in addition to the ones already served on the tour:
Negroni Sbagliato
Caffè corretto

## WHY NAVIGLI?
Our guide will escort you around the most lively parts of Navigli, since this area has the most to offer in terms of culture, history, and of course, delightful food. The canal system has very ancient origins and is still one of the most characteristic and fascinating aspects of this city. You’ll find Milan to be a vibrant, eclectic, and dynamic city with a remarkable cultural openness to the world. It’s easy to see people from all over the globe living here, and not just for fashion.
Navigli is packed with aperitivo venues, gorgeous views, and a relaxing vibe, adding to its appeal as a unique destination. We will walk past the main places that everyone wants to discover, but off the beaten path, as we show you our beautiful monuments, culture, and food.

From bold street eats to inventive culinary creations, this tour brings you face-to-face with the energy and diversity that define LA’s food scene in a small-group or private experience.
From the buzzing energy of Grand Central Market to the rich traditions of Little Tokyo and the colorful charm of Olvera Street, you’ll sample a variety of local favorites and hidden gems. Navigate the maze of historical streets filled with delicious sights and smells as your expert guide leads you through the beautiful chaos of Sicily's markets.
By the end of your tour you will have tasted some of Palermo's most delectable dishes and gained a deeper appreciation of our culture and heritage.

## WHY THE OLD TOWN AND THE VUCCIRIA AND BALLARÒ MARKETS?
Palermo’s Old Town and the Vucciria and Ballarò markets have been chosen as the locations for our tour for several reasons. First and foremost, it allows you to fully immerse yourself in the rich cultural and gastronomic heritage of Palermo - a historical and cultural gem filled with fascinating stories and traditions.
The Vucciria and Ballarò markets are renowned for their incredible variety of fresh veggies, seafood, meats, and artisanal products. These markets provide a feast for your senses, offering you the opportunity to taste the authentic and delicious flavours of Sicily, while immersing yourself in the local culture in its amazing, and at the same time, craziest side.
For food enthusiasts, these markets represent a unique culinary adventure where you can sample traditional Sicilian dishes, snacks, and sweets, creating lasting gastronomic memories. Not only does visiting these markets enrich your personal experience, but also supports local vendors and artisans, contributing to the vitality of these vibrant neighbourhoods.
Notably, Palermo's Old Town is not only about food, it’s also a place filled with history, and recognized by movie fans because of its iconic scenes from ‘The Godfather’. The Old Town is steeped in history, characterized by narrow winding streets, ancient architecture, and historical landmarks related to Greeks, Arabs, and even Vikings. Our tour provides valuable context and insights into the city's fascinating past.
In conclusion, a tour of Palermo's Old Town and its iconic markets offers a multifaceted experience that combines culture, history, food, and community engagement.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/palermo/food-tours-palermo/#BOOKING)

## WHY THE OLD TOWN AND THE VUCCIRIA AND BALLARÒ MARKETS?
Palermo’s Old Town and the Vucciria and Ballarò markets have been chosen as the locations for our tour for several reasons. First and foremost, it allows you to fully immerse yourself in the rich cultural and gastronomic heritage of Palermo - a historical and cultural gem filled with fascinating stories and traditions.
The Vucciria and Ballarò markets are renowned for their incredible variety of fresh veggies, seafood, meats, and artisanal products. These markets provide a feast for your senses, offering you the opportunity to taste the authentic and delicious flavours of Sicily, while immersing yourself in the local culture in its amazing, and at the same time, craziest side.
For food enthusiasts, these markets represent a unique culinary adventure where you can sample traditional Sicilian dishes, snacks, and sweets, creating lasting gastronomic memories. Not only does visiting these markets enrich your personal experience, but also supports local vendors and artisans, contributing to the vitality of these vibrant neighbourhoods.
Notably, Palermo's Old Town is not only about food, it’s also a place filled with history, and recognized by movie fans because of its iconic scenes from ‘The Godfather’. The Old Town is steeped in history, characterized by narrow winding streets, ancient architecture, and historical landmarks related to Greeks, Arabs, and even Vikings. Our tour provides valuable context and insights into the city's fascinating past.
In conclusion, a tour of Palermo's Old Town and its iconic markets offers a multifaceted experience that combines culture, history, food, and community engagement.

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Sorrento, we’ll take you through the town’s most charming areas, offering a local’s perspective on the unique food culture that makes this coastal gem so special.
This tour will engage all your senses, from the vibrant colours and sounds of the streets to the enticing aromas of Sorrento’s culinary delights and the timeless beauty of its historic homes that often housed nobility.
Soak in the stunning views of palaces and churches that have been lovingly maintained for over a century and continue to be part of daily life. We’re certain that you’ll fall in love with both our food and rich history.
By the end of your tour, you’ll have tasted some of Sorrento's most beloved dishes and gained a deeper understanding of its culture and traditions.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/sorrento/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Our journey begins in the heart of Piazza della Vittoria, offering one of the most breathtaking views of the coast. From there we’ll stroll through the cobbled streets of the Old Town, exploring its artisan shops and ancient eateries.
As we meander through narrow streets leading to the scenic Villa Comunale, a small park with beautifully manicured gardens overlooking the sparkling Mediterranean Sea, we’ll taste the delicious potato gateau.
Next, we’ll discover the connection between the legendary singers Caruso and Lucio Dalla and Sorrento at a family-run trattoria that’s been passed down for three generations. Here, you’ll savour two iconic dishes invented in Sorrento, handmade gnocchi alla Sorrentina and authentic cannelloni, paired with a glass of local red wine.
As we spot lemons everywhere, we’ll be inspired to visit the town’s oldest limoncello producer, where you’ll witness the process behind this famous liqueur and enjoy a tasting.
We’ll then continue towards the elegant Piazza Tasso, where we’ll step away from the busy streets to discover a hidden gem: a family-run shop offering a delightful taste of local cheeses - fiordilatte and provola - along with freshly grown Sorrento tomatoes drizzled with extra virgin olive oil, and one of the most beloved local dishes: eggplant parmigiana.
You’ll also learn about the history and significance of lemons in Sorrento’s culinary traditions, with a stop at a local market to explore local produce and try lemon amaretti cookies.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in addition to the ones already served on the tour:
Limoncello cocktail
Costa D'amalfi - Local white wine

Monday - Sunday 11.00 a.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## WHY SORRENTO’S OLD TOWN?
Sorrento’s Old Town winds through the picturesque streets of Via Pietà, Via San Cesareo, Corso Italia, and Via Tasso, offering a perfect blend of history, culture, beauty, and amazing food.
Discover the elegance of Sorrento’s historical buildings, and the serene San Francesco complex with its beautiful cloister, a sought-after venue for both Italian and international weddings. Sorrento’s past comes alive as you explore historic residences, ancient seats like Porta and Dominova, and charming piazzas.
As you wander through the narrow lanes, artisan shops will captivate you with Sorrento’s famous inlaid woodcraft, alongside vibrant food markets, and small family-run food producers. Visit a local limoncello factory, where you can witness the creation of this beloved liqueur and enjoy a refreshing sample. The town’s culinary traditions extend further, with trattorias offering local specialties - think of handmade pasta, and the famous gnocchi alla Sorrentina, topped with rich tomato sauce and cheese.
Here, it’s hard to miss the fresh cheeses, including creamy fiordilatte (cow-milk mozzarella), delightful sweet treats, and lemon-infused pastries.
Sorrento’s Old Town is not only a journey through history and architecture, but also a feast for the senses, where every corner invites you to savour its rich culinary heritage and beauty.

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
Get ready to dive into the legends, rhythms, and high-octane soul of Miami’s Cuban heart on our Little Havana Food Tour! While the neon lights of South Beach get the fame, the real party is happening on the historic streets of Calle Ocho, where family-run ventanitas and sun-drenched plazas serve up the most authentic energy in Florida. This isn't just a walk; it’s a sensory explosion through a neighborhood officially designated as a "National Treasure".
Our adventure kicks off at the Bay of Pigs Monument in the storied Cuban Memorial Boulevard Park. After hearing the epic stories of resilience that built this community, we’ll swing by the José Martí Memorial and hit up a traditional Cuban bakery. You’ll be treated to sweet, flaky guava pastelitos and savory ham croquetas that are basically a tropical hug for your taste buds.
Next, we’re going behind the scenes at a real-deal cigar factory to watch master rollers work their old-world magic. We’ll even duck into a Guayabera store to check out the crisp linen shirts that define Miami cool before knocking back an original Cuban coffee, a social ritual that’ll give you the ultimate local spark.
The flavor keeps coming as we sink our teeth into a perfectly pressed Cuban sandwich and a side of crispy mariquitas. As you feast, your guide will share the wild transition of this area from a quiet suburb to a world-class cultural hotspot. Then, we’ll sit down for a full traditional Cuban meal that shows off the very best of island soul food.
But wait—there’s more! You’ll also get to try our Secret Dish, a surprise treat we only reveal on the day of the tour. It’s the perfect bridge between classic traditions and Miami’s modern, creative edge.
We’ll wrap things up at the legendary Domino Park, the absolute epicenter of local life. Between the rhythmic click of tiles and the laughter of the players, you’ll experience Little Havana at its most genuine. This tour is way more than just a meal—it’s an unforgettable journey through the heritage and hustle that make Miami’s Cuban heart beat.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/miami/upgraded-drinks-package-little-havana/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Our journey begins at the Bay of Pigs Monument and the José Martí Memorial while hearing stories of community resilience.
Then, we’ll stop by a beloved local bakery to treat you to sweet, flaky guava pastelitos, savory ham croquetas and delicious Cuban soda.
For this next stop, you’ll go behind the scenes at a real-deal cigar factory to watch master rollers work their old-world magic.
How about a visit to a local shop to check out the crisp linen shirts that define Miami cool and learn why they are a neighborhood staple?
Let's gather at a walk-up window to knock back an original Cuban coffee, a social ritual that will give you the ultimate local spark.
Now, a quick stop for a perfectly and unforgettably pressed Cuban sandwich paired with a side of crispy mariquitas.
We are finally getting to the end of our stroll and will enjoy the most delicious Cuban meal; we'll sit down together for a full Cuban meal that showcases the very best of island soul food.
We’ll reveal our Secret Dish, a surprise treat that we only share with you on the day of the tour.
It's time to wrap up our adventure at the legendary epicenter of local life, where we’ll witness the rhythmic click of tiles and the authentic heart of the community.

## WHY LITTLE HAVANA?
Choosing Little Havana means immersing yourself in a neighborhood officially designated as a "National Treasure," where the air is a rich tapestry of roasted coffee, artisanal tobacco, and the rhythmic energy of Calle Ocho. As the vibrant soul of Miami and the "American home of the Cubans," this district serves as a living bridge between a storied exile history—honored at landmarks like the Bay of Pigs memorial and Domino Park—and a world-class culinary scene where traditional Caribbean flavors meet modern creativity. Whether you are watching master cigar rollers preserve century-old methods or indulging in a legendary Cuban sandwich paired with a fresh, minty Mojito, Little Havana offers an authentic, sensory-driven journey that captures the enduring heart and heritage of the Cuban spirit.

# Miami Art Deco Cocktail Tour
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide

Also recommended by
 2½ - 3 hours
 Miami, South Beach
 3 stops
 12 people max
 English
## What you'll do
This tour is tailor-made for architecture, history and cocktail enthusiasts! Our knowledgeable guide will lead you on a captivating journey through the South Beach area, unveiling the fascinating stories that have shaped it into an iconic part of Miami's history. You'll gain insights into the various phases that Ocean Drive has undergone throughout the 70s, 80s, 90s, and 2000s.
Discover how the presence of Miami's mafia played a pivotal role in the development of luxurious Art Deco buildings, often hosting clandestine casinos without police interference (you will learn how!). As you explore, you'll encounter remarkable Art Deco pieces, and at select moments, indulge in three handpicked cocktails within the opulent lobbies of renowned Art Deco hotels.
**Secure your spot on this engaging tour using the calendar on your right, and prepare to engage all your senses with our Art Deco Cocktail Walking Tour in South Beach!**
### SEE ITINERARY
Your tour will start inside the lobby of The Betsy Hotel, Built in 1942 by L. Murray Dixon, the architect responsible for many famous Miami Beach Art Deco hotels, The Betsy stands as the lone surviving example of Florida Georgian architecture on Ocean Drive today. Where we are going to Kick off the libanations with a curated Sangria, one of the top selling cocktails of the house.
Next, stroll along Ocean Drive as our guide unveils hidden secrets and highlights classic Art Deco design elements that distinguish Miami's Art Deco. Pass by iconic hotels like the McAlpin, Crescent, and Cavalier, with our guide sharing their history.
Time for another drink! Enjoy a refreshing Mojito at a famous Art Deco hotel built in 1939, known for its appearances in films like "A Hole in the Head" (1959), "There's Something About Mary" (1998), and "Any Given Sunday" (1999), steeped in cinematic charm.
Our guide will delve into the intriguing history of the mafias that shaped South Beach's development, fueled by drug money. Understand the price South Beach paid during its tumultuous past as a dangerous locale.
Before our final cocktail, explore renowned hotels like The Victor, Dream Hotel, and more.
Wrap up your Art Deco Cocktail Tour with a visit to the iconic Kent Hotel, a charming Art Deco gem crafted by L. Murray Dixon. Nestled in the heart of Miami Beach's lively South Beach neighborhood, it's the perfect spot to savor a delightful Sex on Miami Beach cocktail and soak in the vibrant atmosphere.

Looking to gift a memorable experience?

What you'll taste
### THE DRINKS
Mojito A timeless Cuban cocktail, that combines fresh lime, mint, sugar, white rum, and soda water for a refreshingly zesty and effervescent drink. Perfect for capturing the essence of summer and Havana's lively vibe, served over ice with a mint garnish, promising a sip of tropical bliss.
Sex on Miami Beach A light, fruity blend of vodka, cranberry juice, orange juice, and peach schnapps—a creation by Ted Pizio from the 1980s. It combines sweet and tangy flavors for a smooth, refreshing drink that captures the essence of Miami's vibrant beach scene.
Sangria A Spanish delight, blending red wine, fresh fruit, brandy, and sweetness. This vibrant concoction is steeped to perfection, melding the flavors into a refreshing, fruity beverage perfect for social gatherings and warm evenings.
Meeting Spot
We’ll meet inside the Betsy Hotel, located at 1440 Ocean Dr, Miami Beach, FL 33139, United States, our guide will be waiting for you at the lobby. If you have any questions. Please [email us](mailto: support@secrettours.com).

[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/85QeusvJfYCpMD768)
Everyday 4pm
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## WHY SOUTH BEACH?
We chose South Beach as the destination for our tour because it offers an amazing experience that captures the essence of Miami like no other place. South Beach is a world-renowned hotspot known for its cool atmosphere and iconic Art Deco architecture.
You'll find pristine sandy beaches with crystal-clear turquoise waters, providing the perfect backdrop for relaxation and recreation. The area's historic pastel-colored buildings along Ocean Drive showcase the unique Art Deco style, making it a visual delight for architecture and design enthusiasts.
But South Beach is not just about its stunning scenery. It's also a hub of culture, nightlife, and entertainment. You can explore trendy restaurants, shop at stylish boutiques, and unwind at beachfront bars.
Join us on our tour, and let us show you why South Beach is an amazing location that will leave you with unforgettable memories of Miami!

## What you'll do
Get ready to dive deep into the flavors, stories, and rituals that define Miami’s Cuban soul on our Little Havana Cigar & Rum Experience. Over the course of three immersive hours, we’ll explore the iconic streets of Calle Ocho and surrounding Little Havana, where tradition, craftsmanship, and celebration come together in the most authentic way.
Your journey begins in the heart of the neighborhood, guided by passionate local storytellers who bring to life the history of Cuban heritage and the waves of immigration that shaped this community. As you walk past colorful murals, historic landmarks, and lively gathering spots, you’ll uncover how Cuban culture took root in Miami and continues to thrive today.
As we stroll through Calle Ocho, you’ll be introduced to the rituals that define Cuban social life. From the legacy of cigar-making to the rhythms of music and conversation that fill the streets, each stop reveals a new layer of Little Havana’s identity. You’ll learn how rum, cigars, and community became inseparable symbols of Cuban tradition, both on the island and in exile.
This experience is all about indulgence and discovery. You’ll enjoy a curated rum tasting, sampling distinct Cuban-style rums while learning how to recognize their aromas and character. Classic cocktails like mojitos and Cuba libres are part of the journey, paired with an introduction to authentic Cuban cigars, including insight into the craft, history, and techniques passed down through generations.
To balance the bold flavors, we’ll savor beloved Cuban comfort dishes, including croquetas de jamón, alongside traditional Cuban pastries that locals have enjoyed for decades. And of course, no experience would be complete without our incredible secret dish, a final surprise that captures the essence of Cuban comfort and creativity.
By the end of the tour, Little Havana won’t feel like a place you visited,  it will feel like a story you were part of. Join us for an unforgettable walk through flavor, heritage, and tradition, where every sip, bite, and story connects you to the heart of Cuban Miami.
**Book your private tour now using the calendar on your right and prepare to captivate every one of your senses with our Cigar and Rum Experience in Little Havana!**
### SEE ITINERARY
Begin the journey at this historic landmark that represents Cuba’s struggles, resilience, and deep spiritual connection to Miami. Learn about the exile story, faith traditions, and the powerful symbolism of this site, while keeping an eye out for Little Havana’s famous free-roaming chickens.
Step into one of Little Havana’s most beloved bakeries to enjoy crispy croquetas de jamon alongsinde a refreshing cuban soda and a classic guava pastelitos paired with the bold punch of traditional Cuban coffee. A perfect introduction to the flavors that define Cuban comfort food.
Watch master cigar rollers at work as your guide explains the history, craftsmanship, and cultural importance of cigar-making within the Cuban community.
Sip a freshly prepared mojito and delight yourself while learning how these staples became central to Cuban social life.
Enjoy classic Cuban comfort dishes: cuban sandwich perfectly paired with a refreshing Cuba libre, highlighting the balance of savory and sweet flavors.
Discover the nuances of an authentic Cuban cigar, while taste an incredible strawberry daiquiri at one of the most traditional restaurants at the Calle Ocho area.
End the culinary portion with our exclusive secret dish, a flavorful surprise that captures the heart of Cuban tradition and creativity.
Conclude the experience at the legendary Domino Park, where your guide will share insights into the cultural importance of dominoes while you observe locals engaged in one of Little Havana’s most cherished traditions.

## WHY LITTLE HAVANA?
Choosing Little Havana means immersing yourself in a neighborhood officially designated as a "National Treasure," where the air is a rich tapestry of roasted coffee, artisanal tobacco, and the rhythmic energy of Calle Ocho. As the vibrant soul of Miami and the "American home of the Cubans," this district serves as a living bridge between a storied exile history, honored at landmarks like the Bay of Pigs memorial and Domino Park, and a world-class culinary scene where traditional Caribbean flavors meet modern creativity. Whether you are watching master cigar rollers preserve century-old methods or indulging in a legendary Cuban sandwich paired with a fresh, minty Mojito, Little Havana offers an authentic, sensory-driven journey that captures the enduring heart and heritage of the Cuban spirit.

# Miami Art Deco Walking Tour

## What you'll do
Step into the sultry embrace of South Beach's Tropical Art Deco movement on our immersive Art Deco Walking Tour. As you traverse the palm-lined boulevards and sun-kissed streets, you'll encounter a fusion of Art Deco elegance and vibrant tropical flair that defines the architectural landscape of this iconic destination.
Delve into Miami's captivating history, where tales of gangsters and clandestine casinos are interwoven with the city's architectural evolution. Learn how Miami's underworld figures left an indelible mark on the cityscape, shaping the development of opulent Art Deco buildings that doubled as glamorous fronts for illicit activities.
Against the backdrop of swaying palm trees and azure skies, discover the unique blend of Art Deco motifs infused with tropical elements—lush foliage, exotic motifs, and vibrant color palettes that evoke the spirit of paradise found in South Beach.
Our expert guides will lead you to the famed Art Deco hotel lobbies where Hollywood film history was made, offering a glimpse into the glitz and glamour of a bygone era.
**Secure your spot on our Art Deco Walking Tour today and prepare to be transported to a bygone era of glamour, intrigue, and tropical splendor!**
\*Kindly be informed that this tour does not encompass food tastings or cocktails. For tour experiences featuring culinary delights and beverages, we invite you to explore our other Miami tours.
### SEE ITINERARY
Our adventure starts at the Wolfsonian, where we begin our exploration into the heart of Art Deco, examining its defining characteristics and providing tips for identifying them in architecture.
Along the way, our expert guides share stories of the Tropical Art Deco style's origins as we pass by iconic sites like The Victor, Dream Hotel, McAlpin, Crescent, and Cavalier. Step into the opulent lobbies of these magnificent hotels and marvel at their beauty.
Learn how the presence of gangster mobs and casinos injected wealth into the area, and, beyond that, shaped the Art Deco designs we see today.
Continuing our journey along Collins Avenue, immerse yourself in the naval-inspired Art Deco motifs and historic sites that capture the lively atmosphere of Miami Beach in the '70s and '80s. Our guides will tel you everything about South Beach and its decades!
Wrapping up our tour, your guide will share fascinating insights and personal stories about Ocean Drive, ensuring you leave with cherished memories and a deeper appreciation for Miami's vibrant Art Deco scene.

## WHY SOUTH BEACH?
We chose South Beach as the destination for our tour because it offers an amazing experience that captures the essence of Miami like no other place. South Beach is a world-renowned hotspot known for its cool atmosphere and iconic Art Deco architecture.
You'll find pristine sandy beaches with crystal-clear turquoise waters, providing the perfect backdrop for relaxation and recreation. The area's historic pastel-colored buildings along Ocean Drive showcase the unique Art Deco style, making it a visual delight for architecture and design enthusiasts.
But South Beach is not just about its stunning scenery. It's also a hub of culture, nightlife, and entertainment. You can explore trendy restaurants, shop at stylish boutiques, and unwind at beachfront bars.
Join us on our tour, and let us show you why South Beach is an amazing location that will leave you with unforgettable memories of Miami!

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: East Nashville, you'll enjoy a variety of classic Southern foods.
From mouthwatering pulled pork and spicy hot chicken to handcrafted crustburgers at a critically acclaimed restaurant, our experienced guides will take you to the hip streets and quaint neighborhoods of historic East Edgefield and Five Points for an unforgettable and delicious Southern gastronomic adventure. Experience Southern hospitality at its finest as you indulge in the legend and lore of these hot Nashville enclaves.
Plus, don't forget about our Secret Dish, a culinary award-winning treasure that takes center stage on our food journey!
All the food will be served with water, when available. Or if you prefer, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/nashville/east-upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
You'll begin your journey with a treat that’s as smooth as Tennessee Whiskey.
After your sweet start, we’ll mosey over to an iconic restaurant and bar for an unforgettable gastronomical introduction to East Nashville.
For your third stop we head to the historic site of Nashville’s first Food Hall for a Memphis-style Sandwich creation of West Tennessee BBQ by an internationally renowned chef.
Next, you'll discover the secrets of East Nashville, its colorful history, musical and cultural ties.
We stop in at a diner-inspired restaurant by a James Beard Award-winning chef for a dish featured in Netflix’s “Somebody Feed Phil”.
Now it's time to venture into the historic East Edgefield neighborhood.
We take a stroll to the iconic 5-Points and pass legendary music venues, local shops and restaurants and soak up the reimagined craft of this great neighborhood.
We wrap up with our delicious Secret Dish – a recipe so loved by southerners, you can taste the hospitality.

## What you'll do
Embark on our Secret Food Tour: Nashville - 12South and discover one of the city's hottest and trendiest avenues! Our knowledgeable guides will lead you through this vibrant neighborhood that has become Music City's ultimate destination for those who are looking for a good time!
Prepare to be amazed as you stroll through the streets lined with hip coffee shops, stylish restaurants, and beautifully restored buildings. Within walking distance, you'll find an eclectic mix of the coolest shops in town.
Keep your eyes peeled, as you may even stumble upon the dress shop frequented by a famous movie star, ready for an Instagram-worthy moment. During this adventure, you will indulge in the best bites and drinks that the area has to offer.
All the food will be served with water. Or if you prefer, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/nashville/12-south-upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.

After your tasty start, we’ll stroll through the charming 12 South neighborhood, stopping for a fresh seasonal donut that’s as vibrant as the shops and murals surrounding us.
Next, we’ll settle in for a flavorful brisket quesadilla paired with spicy green beans — a nod to Nashville’s love for bold flavors and smoky indulgence.
Your journey continues with a rich Southern hot pork and grits casserole — hearty, comforting, and a true Tennessee classic.
As we explore the iconic 12 South strip, your guide will share insights into the neighborhood’s transformation, from its historic roots to its current status as a hub of local creativity, style, and cuisine.

Accentuated by Music Row, 12South sets a proverbial stage for an amazing journey regaling the legends of several of the city's most notable historic sites and figures while showcasing a curated selection of the hottest food spots that help to define the neighborhood as the culinary capital of today's Nashville.

## WHY THE VILLAGE?
We chose the Greenwich Village district for our New York City food tour because of the district's diverse restaurant scene, deep-rooted history, architecture, and its timeless charisma. While the rest of the city was subject to the 1811 grid system, the Village was able to escape this fate because of its charming, meandering streets.
The powers that be could not be bothered to try to reroute the winding streets found here. But their loss was our gain! This was a big win, both for those residents and for us because we get to take a step back into time and stroll outside of city life without actually leaving the city!

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
We hope you brought an appetite! Milwaukee has a burgeoning culinary scene that blends innovation and farm-fresh ingredients, offering a place and flavor to satisfy all palates! The diverse food scene honors the past and welcomes the changing times and tastes.
On our Secret Food Tour: Milwaukee, our experienced guides will show you the city's historic buildings, storefronts, and restaurants that span cultures and cuisines along the Milwaukee River. After a full day of tastings, you will feel like a local talking about who has the best brats, cheese curds, fish fry, and custard.
And if all of that doesn't convince you to spend an afternoon with us, "The Fonz" gives this tour a double thumbs up! AYYY!
### SEE ITINERARY
Our tour starts in front of Usinger's Famous Sausage, where you will have an opportunity to explore over 40 different varieties of gift boxes containing gourmet sausage, Wisconsin-made cheese, and delicious extras! A perfect purchase to commemorate your time in the city.
Our first taste is at a Milwaukee staple, where you will discover how a humble shop now provides some of the best gourmet cheese in the city. It also features our delicious Secret Taste of the tour!
Next, you'll visit Milwaukee's original German beer hall, which features beer imported from the Munich Hofbräuhaus. Here you will become an expert on "wursts."
Discover the rich flavors of Milwaukee's diverse food scene with a visit to a Latin gem that brings authentic taste and culture to every dish.
Last but certainly not least, you will end the tour with a sweet treat synonymous with Wisconsin, the flavor of the day custard!

### SEE ITINERARY
Weissgerber Amber American Lager
The original recipe of the Weißgerber family. Contract brewed in Wisconsin since 1984. Roasted caramel malts gives the beer its subtle caramel character and reddish-amber hue. American Tettnanger hops provide a soft sweetness and gentle hop aroma.
Milwaukee style Bloody Mary
Milwaukee’s Bloody Marys are legendary for their over-the-top flair. More than a drink, they’re towering edible showcases piled high with cheese, sausage, pickles, and even sliders. It’s a Sunday brunch ritual, and a delicious tribute to Wisconsin’s unapologetic love of beer, meat, and dairy. In Milwaukee, the Bloody Mary isn’t just a cocktail—it’s an experience.
Mexican Marguerita
Milwaukee’s vibrant Mexican community has left a bold mark on the city’s food scene. Here, Margaritas aren’t just a drink—they’re a celebration of tradition, flavor, and the city’s deep ties to Mexican culture. You will sip your way through the heritage that makes Milwaukee so flavorful.

title: ABOUT THE TOUR
text:
## ABOUT THE TOUR
[Tour disponible en español](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/new-york/chinatown-and-little-italy-food-tour-nyc-es/)
Welcome to the Chinatown & Little Italy Secret Food Tour — the ultimate foodie adventure for pizza lovers and Asian cuisine enthusiasts alike!
Get ready to experience the rich history, traditions, and flavors of New York City's most internationally recognized ethnic neighborhoods. Our 5-star cultural and gastronomic adventure promises to be an experience that will leave you with a newfound appreciation for the city and its diverse communities.
You will be eating all the way from Five Points to Chinatown, and from Little Italy to Nolita! As you journey through the streets with us, our knowledgeable guides will captivate you with fascinating stories about these vibrant places.
New York City is full of hidden gems, with this highly curated tour in Lower Manhattan, you'll have the chance to uncover some of the city's best-kept secrets, long known only to the locals!
**Book your tour from the calendar on the right and come hungry!**
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin in historic Columbus Park, once known as Five Points, the treacherous battleground featured in Martin Scorsese's "Gangs of New York”.
First up, you'll enjoy handmade fried dumplings from a beloved local hotspot.
Next, prepare to savor a culinary delight with roots tracing back to 1940s Hong Kong, where the artful fusion of Cantonese roast pork and Western baking techniques gave birth to a beloved culinary masterpiece.
On our walk, we'll pass through Chinatown's bustling streets and hear stories of the neighborhood's first Chinese-owned businesses and the notorious "bloody angle."
Now it's time to experience the true magic of NYC as you turn a corner and realize that the Chinese businesses suddenly turn Italian!
In Little Italy, we also have a special sweet treat for you at a classic New Yorker Italian bakery that dates its origins to 1892.
After which, you'll explore a legendary bar featured in several mafioso films and TV shows and have a slice of pizza fit for The Godfather, himself!
Twirl a forkful of tender pasta crowned with slow-simmered, melt-in-your-mouth meatballs in rich marinara sauce, a comforting bite that feels like stepping into an Italian grandmother’s kitchen.
As with all our tours, there's also our delicious Secret Dish.

## Why Chinatown & Little Italy?
We chose the Chinatown and Little Italy regions of New York City for our food tour because of their unique and vibrant cultural backgrounds. These areas are world-famous for their authentic cuisine and lively atmosphere, making them the perfect destination for food enthusiasts. Our tour has been carefully curated to showcase the best of what these neighborhoods have to offer, featuring local favorites that you won't find anywhere else.
In Little Italy, you'll get to indulge in classic Italian dishes, while experiencing the rich history and traditions of this iconic neighborhood. And in Chinatown, you'll have the chance to taste traditional Chinese delicacies like dumplings and baked roast pork buns, while exploring the vibrant markets and shops that make this area so unique.
With our expert guides leading the way, you'll get an insider's perspective on these amazing spots. Don't miss out on this memorable culinary adventure in the heart of New York City!

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Philadelphia, you'll be led through the busy streets of Philly for a mouth-watering three-hour urban gastronomy tour in the central and historic areas of the 'City of Brotherly Love'.
You'll get to try an innovative twist on the soft pretzel, the pretzel Philly cheesesteak made with ingredients sourced from vendors in the Reading Terminal Market. We'll also try an amazing Malaysian dish in Chinatown. You will travel to the heart of the Emerald Isle too; plus there will be two sweet treats, one will be a life changing Pennsylvania Dutch plus the Secret Dish, which will only be revealed on the tour.
### SEE ITINERARY
First, you'll taste and see the diversity of gastronomy in the expansive Reading Terminal Market, where you'll taste a deliciously warm donut from an Amish owned vendor.
Next you'll discover the influence of other cultures in Philly as we walk through the Friendship Arch in Chinatown and enjoy an authentic Malaysian dish called Roti Canai. It's a Malaysian flatbread that peels apart in tender layers, each bite melting in your mouth and leaves you craving more.
After that we will make our way to an iconic Irish tavern, in where we will stop to try a dish that's been made the same way since the 1860's.
From there we will make our way back to the lovely Reading Terminal Market to try a sourdough soft pretzel topped with aromatic fennel seeds with an innovative twist on a Philadelphia favorite from a local artisanal bakery.
As with all our tours, there's our delicious Secret Dish.
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances.

 English
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour in Naples, you will experience the raw, pulsing heart of Italy’s most flavorful city on this Naples food tour. We’ll lead you away from the chaotic tourist hubs into the soul of the Decumani, where century-old palaces hide world-class street food and every alleyway has a story to tell.
From the sizzle of deep-fried specialties to the aroma of authentic Neapolitan pizza served in a setting you’d never find on your own, this is an insider’s look at why Naples remains the world’s culinary capital.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, if you would like to step up your experience, we have a specially curated [**Upgraded Drinks Package**](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/naples/upgraded-drinks-package/) that you can select when booking your Naples Food Tour.
### SEE ITINERARY
We meet at the historic Dante Square to uncover Neapolitan history before tasting an aperitivo and then diving into a classic fried appetizer, the city's favorite way to spark the appetite.
Stroll through Port’Alba, an ancient gateway lined with bookstalls, where we’ll listen for whispers of a legendary ghost.
Follow the fragrance of wood-fired dough to Via dei Tribunali, where we’ll skip the standard parlors for an authentic Neapolitan pizza tasting hidden inside a stunning hidden location.
Explore the world-famous nativity street, San Gregorio Armeno, to see master craftsmen at work before pausing to dine on local charcuterie and enjoy a glass of wine.
We’ll move on to tasting a rich, traditional pasta sauce that defines home cooking in Naples.
Walk the iconic Spaccanapoli, the narrow ancient street that literally "splits Naples" in two, revealing the city’s most authentic neighborhood life and hidden culinary gems before enjoying an espresso the local way.
We’ll continue to walk down the bustling Via Toledo, the city’s premier shopping thoroughfare, as we make our way toward a sweet finale.
We conclude at a legendary historical pastry shop to indulge in a local pastry just steps from where our journey began.
Listed last for a reason, this final bite is a hand-picked Neapolitan surprise that perfectly captures the local spirit.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in addition to the ones already served on the tour:
Italian lager
Meloncello cream / Cicerenella shot
Amaro - Italian herbal liqueur

## WHY THE OLD TOWN AND DECUMANI DISTRICT?
The Old Town of Naples stands out as one of the most lively areas in the city. This vibrant district hosts a lively open-air market where street vendors showcase a dazzling array of fruits, vegetables, fish, and delectable treats. Amidst this lively scene, you'll encounter charming artisan shops, a bustling nightlife, captivating street art, and a strong sense of community.
Exploring the Decumani district is an absolute must for a truly comprehensive experience of Naples. Named after the old Roman roads, Decumani constitutes the historic heart of the city. Our guided tour will lead you through the squares, winding streets, and ancient churches that witnessed Italy's most significant royal empire.

## What you'll do
Upgrade your Secret Food Tour: Naples, experience with three drink selections chosen to pair with the food you'll be served. You must be 18 and older and have a valid ID. This upgrade can only be purchased in addition to the food tour.
_Due to the nature of creating a well balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please_ [_contact us_](mailto:naples@secrettours.com) _prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs._
### SEE ITINERARY
Italian lager
Beer is the most common drink to have while eating pizza, because it's light and fresh, perfect for the hot Neapolitan climate! Lagers are improving in quality and variety, and you'll be able to try the country's most popular, one of the best on the market, and 100% Italian.
Meloncello cream / Cicerenella shot
Italy is also famous for the making of traditional liqueurs, very different from the common spirits, these are obtained by infusion, using local herbs, spices, and fruits. Discover how, by following the ancient original recipes and only using fruits or mixed-herbs, alcohol, and sugar, we are able to produce delicious liqueurs of various flavors. As Meloncello(or melon cream liquer), or, sometimes as an alternative we'll be able to offer a Cicerenella shot, a liqueur based on anise and orange)
Amaro - Italian herbal liqueur
After a sumptuous Italian meal, and before a relaxing siesta, Neapolitans often have a shot of amaro, usually called "ammazzacaffé" ( literally coffee-killer ). The tradition was born among the aristocratic classes, where it was common to move to a different room to smoke and have a cognac or brandy after dinner. Italian amaros are herb-based liqueurs whose composition changes according to the region where it's made, and can vary from very bitter to a delicate bitter-sweet, making it fit for every palate.

This vibrant district hosts a lively open-air market where street vendors showcase a dazzling array of fruits, vegetables, fish, and delectable treats. Amidst this lively scene, you'll encounter charming artisan shops, a bustling nightlife, captivating street art, and a strong sense of community.
Exploring the Decumani district is an absolute must for a truly comprehensive experience of Naples. Named after the old Roman roads, Decumani constitutes the historic heart of the city. Our guided tour will lead you through the squares, winding streets, and ancient churches that witnessed Italy's most significant royal empire.

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: San Francisco, the Mission District, you'll see how immigrants shaped the neighborhood by creating brilliant artwork, beautiful architecture and sharing their fantastic food! Originally home to Irish, German, and Italian immigrants, it is now the epicenter of San Francisco's Latino culture.
The Mission District is full of vibrant murals that offer a visual history lesson featuring themes ranging from cultural heritage to social-political statements. We will take a stroll through the neighborhood to get an up-close experience.
Our tours are more than just the food. Our local guides connect the dots between history, culture, and cuisine to provide you with a deep understanding of the Mission District's thriving culinary scene!
### SEE ITINERARY
We begin by taking you to the best taqueria for a Mission-style burrito.
Next, we bring the heart of Mexico to your table, serving a Quesabirria.
No stop to the Mission District would be complete without tasting traditional sweet bread. We source ours from a well-loved, 65-year-old bakery.
You'll enjoy mariquitas, a snack widely recognized as one of the best street foods in Cuba.
We will visit a small San Francisco-owned small-batch bean-to-bar craft chocolate maker for a sweet treat! (Hint: it's not Ghirardelli). Here we will enjoy our delicious Secret Dish.

# Authentic Neapolitan Pizza Making Class
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide
Discover Naples with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
Also recommended by
 1 and a ½ -2h
 14 people max
 English & Italian
## What you'll do
Welcome to Naples, the birthplace of pizza and the heart of Italian culinary tradition.
In this immersive Naples pizza making class, you’ll do more than just make & eat pizza - you’ll learn how to make a true Neapolitan pizza from scratch, guided by a local pizzaiolo (pizza chef) who lives and breathes this iconic dish.
The pizza making class starts by crafting your own dough using time-honored techniques and just the right balance of flour, yeast, and water. As it rises, you'll discover the importance of each ingredient: sweet San Marzano tomatoes, fresh mozzarella di bufala, fragrant basil, and extra virgin olive oil. Simple, regional, and deeply flavorful.
Then it’s time to stretch, top, and fire your pizza in a traditional Italian wood-fired oven. During the pizza making class, you'll learn exactly why Naples is the gold standard for pizza worldwide. And yes, you'll taste the difference.
But the pizza making class experience doesn’t end there. As with every Secret Food Tour, we’ll surprise you with a Secret Dish, a special local bite you won’t find in any typical cooking class in Naples.
Come hungry, leave a pizza making pro. And take home memories (and skills) that will last a lifetime.
### SEE ITINERARY
We’ll meet our local pizzaiolo at a Secret location in the heart of old town Naples. Then it’s aprons on and hands washed as we dive into the essentials of Neapolitan pizza making.
You’ll learn how to mix, knead, and shape the perfect dough using time-honored techniques and traditional ingredients. Each pair will prepare a dough recipe designed for two, ideal for recreating the magic at home. Flying solo? Don’t worry, you’ll have your own to work with.
Your guide will walk you through the key local ingredients, from San Marzano tomatoes to buffalo mozzarella.
While your dough rests and you wait for your turn at the wood-fired oven, relax with a drink and soak in the atmosphere. Then, fire up your pizza and watch it bubble, crisp, and char.
As always with Secret Food Tours, there’s a little something extra - a Secret Dish awaits to surprise and delight you.

 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide

Also recommended by
 3 - 3½ hours
 North Beach and Chinatown
 5 stops
 12 people max
 English
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: San Francisco - North Beach and Chinatown, you’ll experience the most iconic parts of “The City by the Bay.” Our experienced guides will take you on a delicious journey through the Gold Rush days of the Barbary Coast. While most tour companies only focus on one neighborhood over the other, we dive deep into both to offer you a taste of the best of both worlds. On your journey, you’ll bite into pizza with a surprise topping straight from the Pacific bay! You will explore West Coast coffee culture, savor dim sum like you’ve never tasted, and find yourself wanting more of our secret bite! You’ll also discover the inner workings of our world famous cable cars, investigate the naughty red light district, uncover where the “The Godfather” was written and conceived, and wander through the alleys where the original miner 49’ers cashed in their sacks of new found gold. All in one afternoon!
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin in front of the San Francisco Cable Car Museum and learn about the world's only working manual cable car system!
Then, we will start our Italian journey by fueling up with a flight of coffees highlighting three local coffee roasters.
On our walk, we see the “big brother” to the Golden Gate Bridge and perhaps will find some Zen along the way!
Now, it’s time to stop at an establishment with a 45+ year history for a delicious slice of pizza that embodies an authentic North Beach experience!
While we make our way to enjoy Italian Ice cream, we will see the church where Marilyn Monroe and Joe DiMaggio wed!
Next, the family-owned shops and markets filled with Asian vegetables, spices, and fish welcome us into Chinatown!
We will settle in the heart of the neighborhood and enjoy proper dim sum as we take in all the beautiful sights and delicious smells!
Then we will see one of the first and oldest still-operating Chinese temples in the United States.
Lastly, as with all our tours, there's our delicious Secret bite!

Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## WHY NORTH BEACH & CHINATOWN?
North Beach and Chinatown are two distinct immigrant neighborhoods in San Francisco. They are culturally and aesthetically different, yet their streets mesh seamlessly together. Chinatown is the oldest and largest Chinese community outside of Asia. It is known for its historic back alleys filled with temple incense, mah-jongg tile clatter, and distant echoes of revolution. On the other hand, North Beach is San Francisco’s “Little Italy” and has historically been home to a large Italian American population, mainly from Northern Italy.
Both North Beach and Chinatown have lively atmospheres, distinctive food, a rich history, and cultural significance across the country.
