# Kuala Lumpur Food Tours
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide
Discover Kuala Lumpur with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
Also recommended by
 3 - 3½ hours
 Jalan Balai Polis, City Centre, 50000 Kuala Lumpur
 12+ stops
 12 people max
 English
## What you'll do
On this Secret Food Tour, we’ll take you off the beaten path to discover hidden gems, historic cafés, local hawker stalls, charming bakeries, traditional tea shops, and so much more.
As we walk through KL’s historic city centre, we’ll uncover fascinating stories behind the city’s temples, colonial buildings, street art filled backstreets and bustling markets, while indulging in over 12 must-try dishes that showcase Malaysia’s incredible culinary heritage. From the rich spices of Arab and Indian traders to the wok-fried mastery of Chinese settlers, the fragrant traditions of the Malay kitchen, and the colonial influences of the Dutch, Portuguese, and British, this tour is a journey through centuries of flavor.
Along the way, we’ll meet local vendors, watch them prepare authentic dishes before our eyes, and taste the true essence of Kuala Lumpur—from sizzling street food to age-old family recipes known only to the locals. This truly is the melting pot of the world, so come hungry and ready to explore
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomic experience, some dietary restrictions may not be accommodated. Please contact us prior to booking so we can assist you. Menu subject to seasonality, availability and time
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/kuala-lumpur/upgraded-drinks-package/#) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Breakfast like a local in a charming coffee shop hidden in the old colonial post office
Enjoy a Nasi Lemak and learn about the National dish
Sample a rich and royal Rendang
Learn how to make a fluffy Roti Chanai made in front of your eyes with a freshly pulled tea
Sitt with the locals over creamy and aromatic bowls of Laksa
Discover a selection of seasonal and exotic fruits and how to eat them
Visit a Hindu Temple and join in for a must try Banana Leaf Curry after
Meet the Uncles and Aunties behind Chinatown's most beloved street stalls sampling their sweets.
Cendol at the Central Market
As with all our tours there is also our Secret Dish
Experience set with favors of Sarawak in 3 shots
[ADD UPGRADED DRINKS PACKAGE](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/kuala-lumpur/upgraded-drinks-package/)
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances.
Book Your Tour
$49.99 / person
Lowest price guarantee
If you have any issues with payment, please email us at [contact@secrettours.com](mailto:contact@secrettours.com) with the city, date and time of tour, and how many adult and child's tickets you need so we can assist you.
**If a tour is fully booked, or you are looking to book a \*Group or Private Tour - please email us about [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) options.**
\*Please note - Group & Private Tours are specifically organised for your group and therefore subject to a premium price.
Looking to gift a memorable experience?
We've got you!
[Buy as a gift](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/buy-gift/)
What you'll taste
### THE FOOD
Steamed Kaya bread with sambal eggs
Nasi Lemak
Curry Laksa
Assam Fish Laksa
Roti Canai & Dipping Curries
Exotic fruits
A selection of Banana Lead Curries
Clam Noodles
Fried banana
Bambo cakes
Custard tart
Apem Balik Peanut pancake
Ice Cendol
Secret Dish
### THE DRINKS
Teh Tarrik (a theatrical froffy Pulled tea)
Local Kopi (Coffee)
Refreshing Iced Lemon
Bottled Water
Meeting Spot
We'll meet In front of the entrance to the Four Points Hotel Chinatown. If you have any questions. Please [email us](mailto: kualalumpur@secrettours.com). Our guide will have an orange umbrella and a huge smile.
Jalan Balai Polis, City Centre, 50000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur.
[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/Jc2fW6DhV6FghjqA8)
Daily 10:00 a.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## Why China Town & Historic City Centre?
We chose Kuala Lumpur’s Chinatown and City Centre because as the birthplace of Kuala Lumpur they represent the true heart of the city's diverse culinary scene. This area is a melting pot of Malay, Chinese, and Indian influences, where age-old family run hawker stalls sit alongside heritage shophouses and bustling markets, colourful street art filled lanes and so much more. Visit local temples alongside food stalls to understand the religion, history and good culture of the area, the old Central Market as well as bakeries, tea shops and historic cafes. The vibrant streets, rich history, and mouth watering street food make it a must-visit destination for any food lover.
No trip to Chinatown is complete without exploring Petaling Street, famous for its street vendors and hidden food gems. In the City Centre, you'll experience the contrast of historic eateries and modern flavors set against the backdrop of KL’s iconic skyline. During your tour, our expert guides will share fascinating stories about the area’s history, food culture, and local traditions.
By the end of the tour, you’ll have a deep appreciation for Kuala Lumpur’s incredible food scene, a satisfied palate, and plenty of insider tips on where to eat next!
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/kuala-lumpur/food-tours-kuala-lumpur/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we give details on our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! If you are looking for something special or if you are organising an event? Just email us at [Group & Private](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) Food tour and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email.
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the number of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) We offer a [upgraded drinks](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/kuala-lumpur/upgraded-drinks-package/) package that gives you 3 extra drinks.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/kuala-lumpur/food-tours-kuala-lumpur/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
We'll meet In front of the entrance to the Four Points Hotel Chinatown.
Jalan Balai Polis, City Centre, 50000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur.
We will send you meeting place details before the day of the tour. The meeting spot is . Your guide will be carrying an Orange umbrella so will be easy to spot.
Where the tour will end?
The tour will end in Jalan Balai Polis, City Centre, 50000 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur.
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
You can always tell our guides of any food allergies or special dietary requirements. We will adapt the menu so you can fully enjoy the tour! If you have any doubts or concerns, feel free to email us at [kualalumpur@secrettours.com](mailto:kualalumpur@secrettours.com) before booking. We have had many vegetarians doing the tour with us and they all enjoyed it very much.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Yes, we can definitely accommodate children. Please contact us at [kualalumpur@secrettours.com](mailto:kualalumpur@secrettours.com) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your family.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem, we have different options. Just inform our host before the experience starts.
Is there enough food during your tour?
Think of the tour as a tasting menu. As we travel you get many delicious treats that comprise a meal with dessert. But there is a lot of food, you will not be hungry after the tour.
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do. We cannot promise but we will try our best to sort something out for you.
How many people max are on this tour?
Since this is a VIP experience, we take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to accommodate you.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own food?
Our host will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform us via [kualalumpur@secrettours.com](mailto:kualalumpur@secrettours.com) before booking and we will do our best to make you happy..
Where does your food come from?
The ingredients are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel and we cannot run the tour due to extreme weather for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Kuala Lumpur?

## What you'll do
[**THIS TOUR IS NO LONGER RUNNING. PLEASE REVIEW OUR OTHER BOSTON FOOD TOURS. ⤴**](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/boston)
_Due to the nature of creating a well balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please_ [_contact us_](mailto:boston@secrettours.com) _prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs._
### SEE ITINERARY
GEORGE HOWELL COFFEE
Enjoy a hot or iced coffee roasted by local legend George Howell. He's the reason people talk about coffee like wine and why growers are paid better wages for their hard work. He also invented the infamous Frappuccino, a receipt Starbucks bought from him in 1994, which was first crafted right here in Cambridge.
LOCAL CRAFT BEER
Jack's Abby's Shipping Out of Boston is a malty, slightly sweet, and lightly hoppy beer that pays homage to the past while building a bolder future.
BOSTON TIKI TEA PARTY
A perfect tiki drink featuring black tea-infused rum and port wine, strong and full of spices, reminiscent of an old style rum punch, but more tropical.

## WHY HARVARD SQUARE?
Harvard Square isn't just the centerpiece of a famous university—it is one of the quintessential cornerstones of American history. Not only was this area one of the first Puritan settlements in the New World, but it was also the birthplace of George Washington's Continental Army, whose victories ultimately led to American independence. Over the years, this neighborhood has attracted its fair share of famous residents, including Franklin Delano Roosevelt, T.S. Eliot, E.E. Cummings, Julia Child, and John Lithgow. It is an intellectual and offbeat area that seamlessly blends history and learning with contemporary arts and entertainment—all while maintaining its quaint small-town charm. And, yes, it is also the epicenter of prestigious Harvard University, where students keep the neighborhood alive and thriving while honoring its storied past.
So, whether you're a history lover or a foodie at heart, Harvard Square promises to be an unforgettable experience, offering a snapshot of Americana around every corner.

Secret Food Tour: Pittsburgh
# Pittsburgh Food Tours
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide
Discover Pittsburgh with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
Also recommended by
 3 - 3.5 hours
 Downtown Pittsburgh
 12 people max
 English
## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Pittsburgh, you’ll explore the beating heart of Steel City—Downtown Pittsburgh.
Our experienced guides will lead you on a vibrant journey through the rich history, diverse culture, and thriving food scene of Pittsburgh’s bustling downtown neighborhood. Along the way, you'll indulge in classic comfort foods, iconic local specialties, and modern takes on beloved regional flavors. As you enjoy each dish, you’ll discover how we evolved from an industrial powerhouse into a dynamic, food-loving city with a deep appreciation for its working-class roots and immigrant influences. Downtown Pittsburgh is a place where history and innovation collide. From the towering glass skyscrapers with historic charm to our unexpected love for pickles, this tour will take you beyond the obvious and into the true flavors of "The 'Burgh".
Food will be served with water when available. Or if you prefer, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/pittsburgh/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available for purchase.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin in majestic Point State Park, the apex of the Golden Triangle, where the Monongahela and Allegheny Rivers meet to form the Ohio River.
First, you'll sample our famous pepperoni roll—a bite originallyp opularized by local coal miners for its portability.
As we stroll through the neighborhood, you'll hear how our Cultural District has transformed into a dynamic arts and entertainment scene and learn about The Three Sisters.
Then, it's time to indulge in our most famous sandwich, stacked high with all the fixings—Yinz guys hungry yet?!
Of course, no visit to Pittsburgh would be complete without paying tribute to the local favorite: Pierogies!
Next you will be be visiting a location with over 45 years of history in Pittsburgh, famous for their Mediterranean fare helping to feed those on the piers as well as Jackie Onasis and her family!
Finally have an iconic Pittsburgh treat, renowned for being a decadent dessert considered a "must have" by Pittsburgh locals.

## Why Downtown?
Downtown Pittsburgh isn’t just the city’s central business district—it’s the historic and cultural heartbeat of the Steel City. This dynamic neighborhood is a blend of old and new, where grand Gilded Age architecture stands alongside modern glass skyscrapers, and where historic markets and legendary eateries share the streets with cutting-edge restaurants and craft breweries. From the lively energy of Market Square to our iconic bridges spanning the three rivers, Downtown Pittsburgh is packed with history, culture, and a deep-rooted passion for food. It’s home to world-class theaters in the Cultural District, fascinating museums, and a thriving sports scene, all within walking distance.
Whether you’re a history buff, a sports fan, or a foodie at heart, Downtown Pittsburgh provides an unforgettable experience—one that tells the story of a city that has reinvented itself while staying true to its hardworking, innovative spirit.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/pittsburgh/food-tours-pittsburgh/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) we offer an Upgraded Drinks Package that gives you a refreshing seasonal margarita, local craft beer, and a specialty milkshake.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/pittsburgh/food-tours-pittsburgh/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
The meeting spot is in front of the Fort Pitt Blockhouse in Point State Park at 601 Commonwealth Pl Building C, Pittsburgh, PA 15222.. Your guide will be there with an orange umbrella and a big smile!
Where the tour will end?
The Pittsburgh tour ends inside The MilkShake Factory Downtown, located at 314 Fifth Ave, Pittsburgh, PA 15222.
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please contact us at [pittsburgh@secrettours.com](mailto:pittsburgh@secrettours.com) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 45 minutes walking but it's not too difficult and there are no hills or stairs. If you walk slowly, then our guide will walk slowly as well.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drinks are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Pittsburgh. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Pittsburgh?
Book our award-winning Secret Food Tour: Pittsburgh before it fills up!

## What You'll Do
On our Secret Food Tour: Detroit, you’ll explore the beating heart of Motor City—Downtown Detroit.
Our experienced guides will lead you on a rich journey through the heart of Detroit, home to incredible history, diverse cultures, and a thriving food scene boasting many of the cities favorite local eats. Along the way, you'll indulge in classic comfort foods, local staples, and the iconic dishes Detroit is known for. As you enjoy each dish, you'll discover how the city became one of the largest and richest cities in America fueled by the hearty classic dishes that make up the Motor City's food scene today. From the titans of industry who occupied Detroits famed skyscrapers, to the beloved working-class heroes that built the cars that got America moving, and the rich immigrant communities that sought their fortunes in Detroit--communities made their mark on the cities iconic foods that you'll experience on your dive into the true flavors of Motown.
Food will be served with water when available. Or if you prefer, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/detroit/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available for purchase.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin at the Spirit of Detroit sculpture welcoming visitors to the city that was once among the richest in America.
First, we'll take you to a classic pub to sample a Detroit original and local favorite: corned beef egg rolls.
Next, you'll learn about the city's famous hot dog wars and see if you can decide who is the true originator of the Coney Dog
As you stroll through Downtown Detroit, you’ll learn about the neighborhood’s incredible journey from its golden age to its recent rebirth and renaissance.
And of course, no visit to downtown would be complete without a stop at our secret spot—a sweet treat destination founded by a famous Detroit writer, serving up something delicious with a heartwarming story behind it.
Then, it's time to cool down with the classic Boston Cooler. Don't let the name fool you, this iconic float is a Detroit Original!
Of course, no visit to Detroit is complete without a slice of their famous Detroit-style pizza. You'll see why this slice is halfway between Chicago and New York in more ways than one!
Finally, we'll end in the Greektown neighborhood to finish the tour with a classic Mediterranean pastry.

## Why Downtown?
Downtown Detroit isn’t just the city’s bustling center; it’s the vibrant heart of a city rich in history and innovation. This dynamic area seamlessly marries the historic with the contemporary, showcasing stunning Art Deco buildings alongside sleek modern architecture. As you stroll through the streets, you'll experience the lively atmosphere of Campus Martius Park, where locals gather for events and relaxation, and the iconic Detroit Riverwalk, offering breathtaking views of the waterfront.
Just a short walk away, Greektown stands as a testament to Detroit's diverse heritage, where the aroma of traditional Mediterranean cuisine fills the air. This lively neighborhood is known for its charming streets lined with family-owned restaurants and vibrant shops, creating a warm and welcoming atmosphere. Whether indulging in authentic Greek dishes or exploring the rich history of the area, Greektown invites visitors to savor every moment.
Whether you’re a history enthusiast, a sports fan, or a lover of great food, a tour through Downtown and Greektown Detroit promises an unforgettable journey. Here, every corner tells a story of a city that has risen from its industrial roots to become a beacon of creativity and resilience.

## What You'll Do
Dive into the enchanting blend of cultures and flavors that define Honolulu's iconic Waikiki neighborhood. Nestled against the backdrop of the legendary Diamond Head, Waikiki has been a cultural crossroads for centuries, showcasing a unique culinary scene influenced by native Hawaiian traditions, Asian flavors, and global cuisines.
Your walking journey begins at the entrance to Waikiki at King David Kalākaua's Statue. From this picturesque starting point, you'll explore the vibrant streets of Waikiki while savoring its diverse culinary offerings.
Strolling through the lively streets, notice your palate as it dances with the flavors of this dynamic neighborhood. While soaking in the beauty and history that makes Waikiki popular, you will walk away with a better understanding of the culture that makes the Hawai’ian Islands truly special.

## Why Downtown?
Waikiki, a tropical paradise on the shores of Oahu, has long been a cherished destination for travelers from around the globe. Once a playground for Hawaiian royalty, it became a bustling hub of culture and commerce in the late 19th century, attracting visitors with its stunning beaches and vibrant atmosphere.
As Waikiki grew, it transformed into a melting pot of traditions and influences, reflected beautifully in its diverse culinary scene. The flavors here are a delightful fusion of native Hawaiian ingredients, Asian spices, and global culinary techniques, showcasing the rich history of cultural exchange that defines the area.
Wander through the bustling streets lined with palm trees and surf shops, where you’ll encounter everything from traditional poke to innovative fusion dishes. The vibrant markets and eateries offer a taste of Hawaii’s agricultural bounty, with fresh seafood, tropical fruits, and locally sourced ingredients taking center stage.
With breathtaking views of Diamond Head and the sparkling Pacific Ocean, a food tour of Waikiki is not just a culinary journey but a chance to immerse yourself in the spirit of aloha. Experience the unique blend of history, culture, and cuisine in this iconic neighborhood, creating unforgettable memories in paradise.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/honolulu/food-tours-honolulu/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/honolulu/food-tours-honolulu/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
You'll meet your guide at the King David Kalākaua Statue at 2050 Kalākaua Ave, Honolulu, HI 96815. Your guide will be there with an orange umbrella and a big smile!
Where the tour will end?
The Honolulu tour ends at the Central Waikiki near the International Market Place
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please contact us at [honolulu@secrettours.com](mailto:honolulu@secrettours.com) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 45 minutes or 1.5 miles walking, but it's not too difficult and there are no hills or stairs. If you walk slowly, then our guide will walk slowly as well.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drinks are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Honolulu. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Honolulu?
Book our award-winning Secret Food Tour: Honolulu before it fills up!

## WHAT YOU’LL DO
On our Secret Food Tour: Hamburg, climb onboard on a culinary journey and follow your expert food guide as you eat your way through the biggest port in Northern Europe where history, culture, and cuisine come together in one of Europe’s most fascinating port cities.
From the iconic red-brick warehouse district to the winding canals of the Old Town, we’ll explore Hamburg's culinary traditions and hidden gems. Along the way, you’ll savour all the must-try iconic dishes of the city. From freshly roasted coffee in Europe’s coffee capital and traditional sailor dishes washed down with Schnaps in one of Hamburg's oldest and most famous maritime restaurants to harbourside fish snacks, historic candy shops, street food, and more! Experience the local flavours that have fed sailors, merchants, and locals for centuries.
Uncover the fascinating secrets of a diverse city built on its culinary imports and exports, torn apart by fire and war, and rebuilt by local spirit. Whether you're a curious local or an adventurous visitor, this tour offers an unforgettable taste of Hamburg’s food heritage. Come hungry and ready to discover the flavours of the North, Ahoy.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgrade Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/hamburg/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Start your day like a true Hamburger with a Franzbrötchen - a comforting local cinnamon pastry.
Visit Hamburg's famous warehouse district and try a freshly roasted coffee from a historic venue.
Stop at a wood-panelled local fish shop along the canal for the iconic Fischbrötchen - a must try sandwich in Hamburg. Savor both the fried fish sandwich and the fish burger with a delicious Hamburg Kola Classic
Try Labskaus, a traditional maritime dish in one of the oldest restaurants in Hamburg served with fried egg, pickles, and red beet, paired with the oldest Schnaps in the city.
in a leafy local neighborhood visit a local pearl for an iconic Hamburg puffer - Reibekuchen
We bring things full circle with a Rote Grütze - a local cherry and berry compote dessert.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish which we will find at an old-fashioned shop on one of the oldest streets in the city.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in addition to the ones already served on the tour:
Bismarck Korn - Hamburg Schnaps
Local fruit Schnaps
[ADD UPGRADED DRINKS PACKAGE](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/hamburg/upgraded-drinks-package/)

Speicherstadt Coffee Roastery, Kehrwieder 5, 20457 Hamburg, Germany
[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/7xFhUCfePR9Y6z1H7)
Mondays - Sundays 11:00 a.m.
Our guide will have an orange umbrella and a huge smile.
## Why Hamburg?
When we tailored this tour, we wanted to cover all the aspects of the city - moving from the firmly inland district of St. Georg, with its expressionist and art nouveau architecture, towards Altstadt and its numerous canals and bridges, where you can admire old German-style houses that survived the Altstadt fire of 1842, which destroyed about one-third of the area, standing next to modernist buildings such as the Elbphilharmonie.
As a gate to and from the rest of the world, Hamburg has imported and exported flavours that have nowadays defined the city’s culinary signature. This food tour is the perfect opportunity to discover new dishes, gently push a few limits, and burst a few myths.

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
Join us for an exclusive food tour in the heart of Florence’s vibrant San Lorenzo neighborhood. Escape the tourist crowds and immerse yourself in the authentic culinary treasures of this historic area. Discover the lively neighborhood, indulge in handmade pasta, and enjoy a curated selection of the finest local cheeses, cold cuts, and wines expertly paired to enhance your experience.
Throughout this immersive journey, you’ll not only savour some of Florence’s most iconic dishes, but also learn about the city’s rich cultural and culinary heritage from our expert guide. The experience will take you beyond your taste buds, as you’ll gain insights into the flavours, history, and culture that make Florence a culinary capital.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/florence/upgraded-drinks-package-san-lorenzo/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Start with a taste of Florence’s iconic savoury pastry at a historic bakery.
Sample Lampredotto, the king of Florentine street food, at one of the neighborhood’s best food stalls.
Savour a selection of local cold cuts, cheeses, bread, and regional wine - the ingredients that make Tuscany’s cuisine so famous.
Enjoy a regional pasta at a historic restaurant, accompanied by a unique Tuscan olive oil tasting.
Cool off with a delicious scoop of fresh gelato from an artisanal gelato shop.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in addition to the ones already served on the tour:
An additional glass of local wine
A Florentine digestive or sweet wine

Looking to gift a memorable experience?

### THE FOOD

Meeting Spot

## Why San Lorenzo?
The San Lorenzo neighborhood is the perfect place to explore Florence's authentic, local food scene. It’s a lively, historical district where you’ll experience the essence of Florence without the tourist crowds. The area is a hub of local culture and culinary traditions.
This district was once a thriving leather hub that, since the 12th century, enabled many Florentine families to prosper. Today, it stands as a symbol of Italy's unification and continues to embody the spirit of the nation.
The tour begins behind the iconic Basilica di San Lorenzo, a key historical landmark that represents the city’s rich history, and as you stroll through San Lorenzo’s vibrant streets, you’ll see how the past and the present converge in one of Florence's most authentic neighborhoods.

Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/glasgow/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Start your tour with the iconic haggis, neeps, and tatties (it’s practically illegal to leave Scotland without trying them!), and the wonderfully creamy Cullen Skink - a Scottish chowder.
Savour the freshest Scottish oysters!
What about a chicken tikka masala? Turns out it was invented in Glasgow!
Indulge in a mouthwatering Cranachan, the king of Scottish desserts, and find out why we adore it!
Sip on a fantastic single malt Scotch. Welcome to Scotland!
Bask on a selection of some of the greatest Scottish cheeses!
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in addition to the ones already served on the tour:
A pint of Tennant’s - THE Scottish beer!
Hills and Harbours gin
[ADD UPGRADED DRINKS PACKAGE](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/glasgow/upgraded-drinks-package/)
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances.
\*Please note: We can’t accommodate pets on our food tours.
Book Your Tour
£89.99 / person
Lowest price guarantee
If you have any issues with payment, please email us at [contact@secrettours.com](mailto:contact@secrettours.com) with the city, date and time of tour, and how many adult and child's tickets you need so we can assist you.
**If a tour is fully booked, or you are looking to book a \*Group or Private Tour - please email us about [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) options.**
\*Please note - Group & Private Tours are specifically organised for your group and therefore subject to a premium price.
Looking to gift a memorable experience?
We've got you!
[Buy as a gift](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/buy-gift/)
What you'll taste
### THE FOOD
Cullen Skink - creamy Scottish chowder
Haggis, neeps and tatties
Oysters
Chicken Tikka Masala
Cranachan - traditional raspberry dessert
Scottish cheese
Our Secret Dish
### THE DRINKS
Two glasses of single malt Scotch
Non-alcoholic beverages for those who don’t consume alcohol
For more drinks see upgrade above
Meeting Spot
We'll meet at the Oran Mor, Directly underneath the Church Tower on the Great Western Road side of the Church.
Address: Oran Mor, Glasgow G12 8QX.
If you have any questions. Please [email us](mailto:glasgow@secrettours.com).
Oran Mor, Glasgow G12 8QX
[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/3UtZABDXEuf3F4Q96)
Mondays - Sundays 11:00 a.m.
The guide will be waiting for you with an Orange Umbrella
## WHY GLASGOW’S WEST END?
Just a few blocks away from the Hillhead Underground Station, we’ll take you away from the classic city centre and bring you into the lively area of the West End.
There, Glaswegians gather for a stroll in the Botanic Gardens and admire its tropical plant collection, hunt for vintage vinyl, comics, and clothes on Ruthven and Dowanside Lanes, or spend some time at the Hunterian Museum or the University of Glasgow. But, at the end of the day they all gather around Hillhead Station and Ashton Lane to get a drink at the pub, a chippy, or a curry.
Don’t miss out on our tailor-made tour of Glasgow’s West-End!
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/glasgow/food-tours-glasgow/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/glasgow/food-tours-glasgow/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
You can always tell our guide of any food allergies or special dietary requirements. We will adapt the menu so you can fully enjoy the tour! If you have any doubts or concerns, feel free to email us at [glasgow@secrettours.com](mailto:glasgow@secrettours.com) before booking. We have had many vegetarians doing the tour with us and they all enjoyed it thoroughly.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you should wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 40 mins of walking but it is not too difficult. If you walk slowly, then our guide will walk slowly as well.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 10 people maximum. If you are more than 10 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Where does your food come from?
Top - quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Glasgow. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Glasgow?

## Why San Lorenzo?
The San Lorenzo neighborhood is the perfect place to explore Florence's authentic, local food scene. It’s a lively, historical district where you’ll experience the essence of Florence without the tourist crowds. The area is a hub of local culture and culinary traditions.
This district was once a thriving leather hub that, since the 12th century, enabled many Florentine families to prosper. Today, it stands as a symbol of Italy's unification and continues to embody the spirit of the nation.
The tour begins behind the iconic Basilica di San Lorenzo, a key historical landmark that represents the city’s rich history, and as you stroll through San Lorenzo’s vibrant streets, you’ll see how the past and the present converge in one of Florence's most authentic neighborhoods.

 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide

Also recommended by

 Dubrovnik

 12 people max

## What you'll do
On your Secret Food Tour: Dubrovnik, join us on a journey through the cobbled streets of this stunning Adriatic city, uncovering its history via its incredible cuisine.
As we explore the Old Town and its hidden alleys, you'll sample traditional Dalmatian dishes, from freshly baked burek and local seafood delicacies to centuries-old recipes passed down through generations. Along the way, we’ll visit bustling markets, family-run eateries, and tucked-away taverns where locals gather to eat, drink, and share stories.
You'll taste artisanal cheeses, cured meats, enjoy wine tastings, dine in historic taverns, and finish with a sweet surprise from one of Dubrovnik's most beloved dessert spots. Between bites, we’ll uncover the city’s history, from the Republic of Ragusa to modern-day, through the lens of its culinary heritage.
Whether it's your first time in Dubrovnik or you’re returning for more, this tour is the perfect way to discover the city’s soul through its food. Come hungry and ready to uncover the secrets of the Adriatic table.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/dubrovnik/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Kick off with a freshly baked Burek - a Balkan filo meat or vegetable pie - made in front of you.
Explore a bustling farmers market to sample regional candies and delicacies.
Enjoy a beautiful charcuterie board of regional meats and cheeses at a local wine bar with a refreshing rosé.
Try the Croatian black ink seafood risotto and historic meat stew paired with wine at a local taverna.
Visit a family-run fish shop for fried oysters by the harbour.
Cool off with a seafront artisanal ice cream made with local ingredients from lavender to Dubrovnik bitter oranges.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in
addition to the ones already served on the tour:
Skin Contact’s orange wine (or a regional alternative)
Local bitter liqueur

\*Please note: We can’t accommodate pets on our food tours.

What You'll Taste
### THE FOOD

Black risotto
Šporki Makaruli - meat ragu macaroni
Regional cheeses and prosciutto

Local rosé wine

Homemade honey herbal digestive
Water

Meeting Spot
We meet right next to the Hard Rock Cafe entrance at Onofrio’s Large Fountain, Poljana Paska Miličevića 2000, 20000 Dubrovnik. If you have any questions, please [email us](mailto:dubrovnik@secrettours.com).
Onofrio's Large Fountain next to the Hard Rock Cafe entrance - Poljana Paska Miličevića 2000, 20000, Dubrovnik
[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/nHDuwQWKb2Y4acc96)
Monday - Sunday 10:30 a.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella and a big smile, so that they are easy to spot.
## Why Dubrovnik Old Town?
Dubrovnik’s Old Town is the perfect setting for our Secret Food Tour, with its maze of stone-paved streets, ancient city walls, and centuries of rich cultural heritage. With your expert local foodie navigate the historic markets and ancient tavernas in this UNESCO World Heritage site that offers an unforgettable backdrop to explore the culinary soul of the Adriatic.
Within these historic walls, you’ll not only taste traditional Dalmatian dishes, but also uncover hidden food spots tucked into charming alleyways and historic buildings that have stood the test of time.
The Old Town’s unique blend of medieval atmosphere, coastal beauty, and deep-rooted food traditions makes it the ideal place to experience the flavours of Dubrovnik while discovering the stories that shaped them.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/dubrovnik/food-tours-dubrovnik/#BOOKING)

However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel and we cannot run the tour due to extreme weather for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).

## What you'll do
Upgrade your **Secret Food Tour: Chicago** experience with a curated drink package that perfectly complements the city's rich culinary heritage. You must be 21 or older and have a valid ID to participate. This upgrade can only be purchased in addition to the food tour.
_Due to the nature of creating a well balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please_ [_contact us_](mailto:chicago@secrettours.com) _prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs._
### SEE ITINERARY
Goose Island 312 Urban Wheat Ale
This iconic local beer is brewed right here in Chicago by Goose Island Beer Company. Named after the city's 312 area code, this smooth, citrusy wheat ale is a hometown favorite and represents the innovative spirit of Chicago's craft beer scene. It’s a must-try for anyone wanting a true taste of the city.
Brownie Old Fashioned
A decadent twist on the classic cocktail, our Brownie Old Fashioned pays tribute to one of Chicago’s sweetest contributions to the world: the brownie. The first-ever brownie is widely believed to have been created in the kitchen of the Palmer House Hotel in the late 19th century. This rich, chocolatey cocktail is both a nod to history and a flavorful finish to your food journey.

title: SEE ITINERARY
text:
### SEE ITINERARY
Your journey begins in the heart of Old Quebec with a refined appetizer pairing that showcases the region’s wild ingredients—earthy local mushrooms and a delicate duck breast carpaccio that nods to the city’s deep French roots.
After a short stroll through centuries-old streets, you’ll warm up with a comforting trio of traditional Québécois staples: a silky pea soup, savory pâté chinois, and chewy maple taffy pulled fresh for you, just like at the cabane à sucre.
Next, you’ll be welcomed into a cozy 200-year-old home for a hearty bistro-style main: slow-cooked brisket with mashed potatoes, root vegetables, and rich gravy. Adults can enjoy a local wine chosen to match the flavors.
As we continue through the old town’s charming alleys, prepare for a sweet stop featuring a melt-in-your-mouth “sucre à la crème” and a bite of what many call the best croissant in Quebec City.
Finally, no tour would be complete without Quebec’s most iconic dish—poutine! But this isn’t just any version: it’s served with a creative twist and, for adults, paired with a splash of artisanal local gin.
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances.

Meeting Spot

La Buchette Restaurant at 24 Sainte-Anne Street

Daily 11:00 a.m
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella and a big smile, so that they are easy to spot.
## Why Old Quebec City
Old Quebec is a living museum—a UNESCO World Heritage site where centuries-old stone buildings, winding cobblestone streets, and fortified walls set the stage for an unforgettable culinary journey. This compact, walkable neighborhood has been the heart of Quebec’s cultural and gastronomic life since the 17th century. Here, French-Canadian traditions mix with modern creativity, offering everything from rustic comfort foods to refined local delicacies made with seasonal ingredients like maple, wild berries, and artisanal cheeses.
So, come explore with us! Old Quebec is more than just a historic district—it’s a place to connect with the city’s soul, meet passionate makers, and taste the stories that have shaped this one-of-a-kind place for over 400 years.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/quebec-city/food-tours-quebec-city/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, as well as a wine and local gin tasting (for guests age 18+)
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://secretfoodtours.com/quebec-city/food-tours-quebec-city/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
You'll meet your guide in front of La Buchette Restaurant at 24 Sainte-Anne Street
[»»CLICK HERE FOR DIRECTIONS««](https://maps.app.goo.gl/VpF3YJKJGU2Dxwoo7)
Where the tour will end?
This tour will end at a restaurant in Old Quebec City (exact ending location may vary based on day of the week, time of year)
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
Please let us know when you are booking the tour and we will do our very best to accommodate all dietary restrictions so that you can fully enjoy the tour as well. You can always tell our guide of any food allergies or special dietary requirements at the start of the tour as well. If you have any doubts or concerns, feel free to email us at [contact@secrettours.com](mailto:contact@secrettours.com) before booking. We have had many vegetarians doing the tour with us and they all enjoyed it thoroughly.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 35 mins of walking but it is not too difficult and is broken up between food stops. During the duration of the tour we will walk just over one kilometer. The guide walks at the pace of the tour members, you will never be rushed.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 16 people maximum. If you are more than 16 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
Top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable here in Quebec City. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Quebec City?

title: What you'll do
text:
## What you'll do
Step into the historic heart of the city and discover Old Montreal through its rich flavors and timeless streets.
On this guided walking tour, you’ll explore cobblestone lanes, grand architecture, and local favorites that reflect the city's deep French-Canadian roots and global influences. Along the way, we’ll stop to enjoy iconic Montreal bites like a classic smoked salmon bagel, a taste of locally made charcuterie and cheeses, and the city’s beloved poutine.
You’ll also uncover hidden gems and stories behind the food, from old-world pastries to vibrant multicultural dishes that reflect the neighborhood’s evolving identity.
We finish the tour with a cheers at a local microbrewery, raising a glass to the flavors and history that make Old Montreal so unforgettable.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin our culinary journey inside one of Old Montreal’s most breathtaking buildings — a former grand bank turned café and co-working space. Here, you’ll enjoy a classic smoked salmon bagel, a staple of Montréal cuisine, in an unforgettable setting that blends heritage with modern flair.
Next, we stroll through the cobblestone streets to a charming local épicerie, where we sample a curated board of Québec cheeses and artisanal charcuterie. This is your chance to savor the flavors of the province, all in one delicious bite.
Depending on the day of your tour, you'll experience one of two unique culinary stops:
\- Thursday to Sunday: We dive into a hearty and flavor-packed Mati Salad topped with 24-hour braised lamb — slow-cooked to perfection and full of Mediterranean flair
\- Monday to Wednesday: You'll get a taste of tradition with a Nopales Taco, featuring cactus prepared the way it’s been done for generations in Mexico — a family recipe brought straight to Montreal.
(In summer we only go to Mati. We no longer go to Catrina even on the off season. The Mati salad was replaced by their seasonal watermelon salad with feta, honey and pistachios that is a complete hit)
No food tour in Montreal would be complete without poutine! We go all in with a rich, smoky, and indulgent version featuring the city’s famous smoked meat. (Don’t worry — there’s a delicious vegetarian option too.) We’ve replaced this stop (Franklin's Subs and Suds) with Boqueria, where we taste a Spanish-inspired blend of poutine and patatas bravas. It features Serrano ham, chipotle pepper gravy, Sherry vinegar, and of course, curd cheese. Fusion foods at their best!
For something sweet, we stop by one of Old Montreal’s hidden gems to try a canelé — a small French pastry with a caramelized crust and a soft, custardy center. It’s a little bite of heaven.
To finish, we raise a glass at one of the city’s best local microbreweries. You'll sip a small pour of craft beer brewed right in the heart of Old Montreal. Cheers to a delicious adventure!
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances.

## Why Old Montreal?
Old Montreal is where the city began — a neighborhood steeped in centuries of history, cobblestone charm, and enduring culinary tradition. Once a bustling hub of trade and commerce along the St. Lawrence River, it’s now home to some of the city’s most iconic architecture, hidden alleyways, and a diverse food scene shaped by French-Canadian roots and global influences.
From classic dishes that have stood the test of time to modern bites inspired by the neighborhood’s evolving identity, Old Montreal offers a one-of-a-kind tasting journey. Here, history and flavor go hand in hand — whether you’re biting into a warm pastry from a tucked-away patisserie or enjoying a local beer in a former convent turned brewery.
So, come explore with us! Old Montreal isn’t just a place to see — it’s a place to taste, connect, and experience the rich layers of a city that feeds both body and soul.

## What you'll do
On your Secret Food Tour through Belém, follow your local guide on a carefully curated journey through one of Lisbon’s most captivating neighbourhoods. From charming riverside eateries to hidden gems frequented by locals, this tour will take you through a diverse selection of stops that celebrate the essence of Portuguese cuisine. You’ll savour everything from traditional petiscos and fresh seafood to the iconic Pastel de Belém, all while discovering the culinary traditions that have shaped this historic district.
As you stroll past iconic landmarks, such as the Jeronimos Monastery and the Monument to the Discoveries, you’ll experience a neighbourhood known for its vibrant food scene, where street vendors and fine dining coexist in harmony. Whether indulging in century-old recipes or modern creations, you’ll discover what makes Belém truly special.
This tour promises to leave you with a sense of ‘saudade’— a nostalgic longing for the incredible flavours and memories you’ll create along the way.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/lisbon/upgraded-bel%C3%A9m-drinks-package/) is available.

You'll then enjoy a golden-crusted cod, perfectly nestled on a bed of potatoes and spinach.
Try a variety of artisanal chocolate followed by the iconic Pastel de Bélem - Portugal’s famous custard dessert.

## Why Belém?
We chose the Belém district for one of our Lisbon food tours because of its rich culinary heritage, historical landmarks, and beautiful gardens. Belém is home to the renowned Pastel de Belém, the birthplace of the iconic custard tarts first created in the 19th century, offering visitors an authentic taste of tradition.
Known for its grand monuments and cultural significance, Belém’s cuisine reflects Portugal’s deep history, featuring dishes like bacalhau and delicious local street food. In its charming cafés and food stalls, you’ll experience authentic flavours passed down through generations.
From the grandeur of the Jeronimos Monastery and the Belém Tower to the serene gardens and riverside views, this district offers a perfect blend of history, culture, and incredible food. Join us for an unforgettable food tour in one of Lisbon’s most scenic areas!

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour of Minneapolis , you'll taste your way through the bold flavors and hidden culinary gems of two of the city's most historic and trendsetting neighborhoods.
Led by a passionate local guide, this tour is your gateway to the heart of Minneapolis’s evolving food scene—where Scandinavian roots, immigrant traditions, and modern innovation collide on every plate.
You’ll explore the North Loop’s red-brick warehouses transformed into buzzing bakeries, gastropubs, and award-winning kitchens. Then, cross the iconic Mississippi River into St. Anthony, where cobblestone streets and river views set the stage for stories of flour barons, fur traders, and new-generation chefs reinventing Midwestern cuisine. You’ll hear how European and Indigenous food traditions have shaped the city’s identity—reflected in the flavors you’ll taste.
Whether you're a first-time visitor or a curious local, you’ll leave this tour full of food, stories, and a deeper connection to Minneapolis's deliciously diverse culture.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin our tour at a lively food hall in the heart of the North Loop, Minneapolis’s former warehouse district turned culinary hotspot. This space buzzes with the energy of local chefs and creative food entrepreneurs.
You'll indulge in a slice of blistered, coal-fired pizza topped with thoughtfully sourced ingredients and baked in a high-heat oven for a perfect char and chew. Considered one of the best slices in the Twin Cities, it’s a beloved local take on an East Coast classic.
Then, it’s time for a true Minnesota staple: golden, beer-battered cheese curds. Fresh, squeaky, and straight from the fryer, these bites are the ultimate local comfort food; crispy on the outside, melty on the inside and paired with a unique ketchup made right on sight.
After filling up on North Loop favorites, we’ll stroll across the scenic Hennepin Avenue Bridge, taking in views of the Mississippi River and downtown skyline as we make our way into St. Anthony Main—the city’s oldest neighborhood.
There, you’ll dig into a plate of handmade pierogies, soft and pillowy with rich fillings that reflect the area's Eastern European heritage. Served with traditional accompaniments, they’re a taste of the immigrant stories that helped shape Minneapolis.
We’ll end our tour on a sweet note with a scoop of small-batch ice cream, crafted locally using seasonal ingredients. Whether you go for something fruity, creamy, or adventurous, it’s the perfect way to cool down and reflect on your culinary journey.
As with all our tours, there's also our delicious Secret Dish.

## Why Minneapolis?
The North Loop and St. Anthony Main neighborhoods are where Minneapolis’s past and future meet—historic, picturesque, and buzzing with culinary creativity. Once the industrial heart of the city, the North Loop has transformed into one of the Midwest’s most vibrant food destinations. With its iconic red-brick warehouses now home to chef-driven restaurants, craft breweries, and artisan bakeries, the neighborhood blends old-world charm with cutting-edge flavor.
Just across the Mississippi River lies St. Anthony Main, the oldest part of Minneapolis, where cobblestone streets and riverfront views offer a dramatic backdrop to a thriving food and arts scene. Here, you'll find a celebration of local heritage, immigrant stories, and Indigenous influences—all reflected in the neighborhood’s dishes, from European comfort food to globally inspired street eats.
So, come explore with us! These neighborhoods aren’t just places to eat—they’re places to connect, discover, and feed both your body and your curiosity.

## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/minneapolis/food-tours-minneapolis/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
You'll meet your guide outside the Graze Food Hall at 520 N 4th St, Minneapolis, MN 55401. Your guide will be there with an orange umbrella and a big smile!
Where the tour will end?
The tour will end in the St. Anthony neighborhood about 1.25 miles away from the starting location.
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please contact us at [contact@secrettours.com](mailto:honolulu@secrettours.com) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 45 minutes walking but it's not too difficult and there are no hills or stairs. If you walk slowly, then our guide will walk slowly as well.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 14 people maximum. If you are more than 14 people, please do not hesitate to email us and will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drinks are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Minneapolis. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Minneapolis?

## Why Minneapolis?
The North Loop and St. Anthony Main neighborhoods are where Minneapolis’s past and future meet—historic, picturesque, and buzzing with culinary creativity. Once the industrial heart of the city, the North Loop has transformed into one of the Midwest’s most vibrant food destinations. With its iconic red-brick warehouses now home to chef-driven restaurants, craft breweries, and artisan bakeries, the neighborhood blends old-world charm with cutting-edge flavor.
Just across the Mississippi River lies St. Anthony Main, the oldest part of Minneapolis, where cobblestone streets and riverfront views offer a dramatic backdrop to a thriving food and arts scene. Here, you'll find a celebration of local heritage, immigrant stories, and Indigenous influences—all reflected in the neighborhood’s dishes, from European comfort food to globally inspired street eats.
So, come explore with us! These neighborhoods aren’t just places to eat—they’re places to connect, discover, and feed both your body and your curiosity.
