## What you'll do
Discover the flavors, stories, and hidden corners of Canada’s capital on our Ottawa Food Tour through the historic ByWard Market. Since 1826, this bustling neighborhood has been the city’s gathering place, where farmers, chefs, and artisans come together to share their craft. Today, it remains Ottawa’s culinary hub, brimming with aromas of sizzling comfort foods, sweet local delicacies, and international influences that reflect the city’s vibrant character.
As we walk, you will hear the fascinating history of Ottawa and its famous market while stepping into some of its most beloved establishments, including the city’s oldest tavern. Along the way, you will indulge in iconic Canadian favorites such as warm, sugary Beavertails, rich poutine topped with squeaky cheese curds, and velvety local maple syrups. You will savor molasses-glazed duck, sample artisanal cheeses, and bite into a renowned sandwich layered with bold global flavors. To finish, a decadent sweet treat will bring our journey full circle.
Our Ottawa Food Tour is more than a meal. It is a chance to explore the heart of the city, connect with its history, and taste the foods that define its culture. Join us in the ByWard Market for an unforgettable adventure of sights, flavors, and stories that will leave you hungry for more.
### SEE ITINERARY
We’ll begin our journey with a Canadian classic: a warm, golden BeaverTail dusted in sugar. As you enjoy this sweet treat, you’ll take in beautiful views of the Ottawa River while learning how the city first became the capital of Canada.
Our next stop brings you into a historic tavern where time seems to stand still. Within its centuries-old walls, you’ll savor one of Canada’s most iconic comfort foods: rich, hearty poutine.
From there, you’ll sample a curated selection of local cheeses, each crafted by regional producers who carry on proud dairy traditions.
Next, we’ll step inside a historic building where you’ll indulge in tender molasses-glazed duck. The balance of sweetness and depth in this dish offers a taste of Canadian culinary creativity rooted in tradition.
No Ottawa Food Tour would be complete without experiencing maple syrup, one of the country’s most iconic exports. You’ll explore its subtle variations in flavor and learn why this golden nectar has been a cornerstone of Canadian culture for generations.
To end on a sweet note, we’ll treat you to a final indulgence that ties together the flavors of the tour, leaving you with the perfect memory of Ottawa’s culinary spirit.
And, of course, as with all our tours, there is also our delicious Secret Dish.

## What you'll do
On our **Secret Food Tour: Warsaw**, you’ll discover the rich flavors and resilient spirit of Poland’s capital - a city rebuilt with heart, filled with stories, and seasoned with history. From old-world cafés to hidden local favorites, we’ll guide you through a delicious journey celebrating Warsaw’s traditional and modern cuisine.
Experience the city’s culinary soul through its most iconic comfort foods, authentic markets, and vibrant neighborhoods. Taste your way through **7+ local dishes and drinks**, from hearty pierogi to crispy potato pancakes and sweet Polish waffles, while uncovering the legends and resilience that define this remarkable city.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [**Upgraded Drinks Package**](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/warsaw/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
We’ll begin in front of the Marie Curie Museum, where your local guide will set the scene for a day of flavor and history.
Our first stop is a traditional Milk Bar, a relic of the communist era and still beloved today. Here, you’ll enjoy classic Polish potato pancakes and apple pancakes with sour cream, along with a comforting cup of tea, as we learn how these humble eateries fed a nation.
We’ll then stroll through the Barbican, this time not in battle but in peace, admiring the defensive walls that protected Warsaw’s Old Town for centuries.
At a beloved local delicatessen, you’ll sample a smoked sausage while hearing stories about the Old Market Square and how it became the city’s beating heart.
Next, we’ll visit a retro-style tavern - once home to trattorias, wineries, and osterias - to enjoy a cold Polish beer, pickled cucumbers, and a sweet final shot, the traditional Polish way to toast new friendships.
No visit to Warsaw would be complete without Pierogi, Poland’s signature dumplings, which you’ll enjoy in a charming Old Town restaurant paired with a mug of hearty soup.
To end on a sweet note, we’ll taste the new Polish classic - the Gofr, a crispy waffle topped with mascarpone cheese and more, the perfect farewell bite to your Warsaw adventure.
As with all our tours, there’s also a delicious Secret Dish waiting to be discovered.
When you purchase our Upgrade Package, you'll enjoy the following drinks, in addition to the ones already served on the tour:
A shot of VERY strong polish liqueur
Polish mulled WINE

Meeting Spot
Your guide will meet you at the Next to the entrance to **Maria Skłodowska-Curie Museum, Freta 16, 00-227 Warszawa.** If you have any questions. Please [email us](mailto:warsaw@secrettours.com).
Maria Skłodowska-Curie Museum
[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/EGnH6tTAGucfMwUXA)
Mon - Saturday 10:30 a.m
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella and a big smile, so that they are easy to spot.
## WHY THE OLD TOWN AND THE FORMER JEWISH GHETTO OF WARSAW?
We begin our journey in Warsaw’s Old Town - a place that looks centuries old, but is actually one of the most extraordinary reconstructions in the world. Almost completely destroyed during World War II, it was rebuilt brick by brick, using paintings by Canaletto and old photographs as guides. Today, it stands as a symbol of resilience, creativity, and pride.
The Old Town has always been the heart of Warsaw. This was where royal processions passed on their way to the castle, where merchants gathered on the Market Square, and where travelers stopped to eat, trade, and share news from across Europe. At its center stands the Royal Castle, once the seat of Polish kings and the stage for some of the country’s most important historical moments. Around it, cafés and restaurants fill the same streets where nobles once rode their carriages and street performers entertained crowds. It’s living history - elegant, proud, and full of energy.
From there, we make our way toward the area that once formed part of the Jewish Ghetto. Before the war, Warsaw was home to one of the largest Jewish communities in the world - more than 350,000 people. It was a city within a city, full of life, music, learning, and trade.
Warsaw isn’t just a capital; it’s a survivor, a city that’s been torn down and rebuilt more than once, each time stronger and more alive. Today, its streets are lined with modern art galleries, cozy bars, craft beer cellars, and traditional eateries serving family recipes passed down for generations.
By the time we reach the Market Square, with the mermaid statue watching over it, you’ll see how Warsaw holds its contrasts proudly - royal and raw, tragic and joyful, historic and modern all at once. Here, the spirit of old Poland meets the rhythm of the new one. And somewhere between a bite of _pierogi_ and a sip of local beer, you’ll feel what the locals know: this city may have been rebuilt, but its soul never left.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/warsaw/food-tours-warsaw/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) We offer a [upgraded drinks package](https://secretfoodtours.com/warsaw/upgraded-drinks-package/) that gives you 2 drinks.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://secretfoodtours.com/warsaw/food-tours-warsaw/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
The meeting spot is Next to the entrance to Maria Skłodowska-Curie Museum, Freta 16, 00-227 Warszawa. Our guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella and a big smile!
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
Due to the nature of creating a well balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please contact us at contact@secrettours.com prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 40-50 minutes of walking but it's not too difficult. If you walk slowly, then our guide will walk slowly as well.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If your are a group of more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
We work only with best eateries and pubs in the Warsaw. The food and drink we buy is top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
The weather in Warsaw is unfortunately quite unpredictable. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this. For more info see our [Terms and Conditions.](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/)
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Warsaw?

## What you'll do
Join us on our Richmond Food Tour and taste your way through Shockoe Bottom, one of the city’s oldest and most vibrant neighborhoods. Once a bustling riverfront market, today this historic district blends cobblestone charm with a bold, modern food scene that’s redefining Southern cuisine.
As we walk, you’ll uncover the stories that shaped Richmond while savoring a curated lineup of local favorites. Begin by experiencing global inspiration with a flavorful duck bao bun, bursting with Thai street-style flair. Then, savor a classic Southern she-crab soup, rich and comforting.  Next, indulge in Richmond’s signature sourdough, Southern-inspired pizza — a dish that perfectly captures the city’s inventive culinary spirit.
We’ll also visit a beloved mother-daughter dessert shop, where more than 30 varieties of cookies are baked fresh daily. And, as always, you’ll enjoy our signature Secret Dish, revealed only on the tour!
Our Richmond Food Tour is more than a meal — it’s a delicious journey through history, hospitality, and hidden gems that make Richmond one of the South’s best-kept culinary secrets.
Book your Richmond Food Tour today and discover the city’s most mouthwatering stories, one bite at a time.

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Rio de Janeiro, you’ll experience the vibrant tastes and rhythms of Copacabana, where the beach, music, and food come together to create one of the most iconic neighborhoods in the world. As you explore the city’s rich culinary traditions, you’ll learn about Rio’s cultural history and bohemian soul, one delicious bite at a time. On this tour, you’ll sip refreshing coconut water by the seaside, taste a tropical pineapple sandwich, savor a beloved Brazilian seafood dish with African and Indigenous roots, enjoy traditional galeto (charcoal-grilled chicken), try a unique pastel paired with fresh sugar cane juice, and of course, discover our Secret Dish!
All the food will be served with water or if you prefer, non-alcoholic beverages. A thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drink Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/rio/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available..
### SEE ITINERARY
We’ll begin right at the famous Copacabana beach with a chilled coconut water, the perfect way to refresh yourself as you take in the golden sands and lively beachfront atmosphere.
From there, we’ll admire the world-famous Copacabana Palace, a symbol of Rio’s golden age and a cultural landmark that has hosted royalty, celebrities, and musicians alike.
Next, we’ll step into the very ground where Bossa Nova was born, exploring the neighborhood’s bohemian past and musical legacy.
Next, we’ll stop for a tropical favorite — a pineapple sandwich, a surprising local delight that captures the fresh and playful side of Rio’s food culture.
From there, you’ll taste one of Brazil’s most beloved seafood dishes, a recipe influenced by African and Indigenous traditions that showcases the city’s cultural blend.
Our journey continues with galeto, Rio’s iconic charcoal-grilled chicken, crispy on the outside, tender inside, and a true staple of the carioca dining scene.
Then it’s time for a savory pastel, a deep-fried pastry with a unique filling, perfectly paired with fresh-pressed caldo de cana (sugar cane juice), a classic street food combination.
And, finally, as with all our tours, we’ll conclude with our delicious Secret Dish.

## WHY COPACABANA?
With its world-famous beach, vibrant nightlife, and bohemian spirit, Copacabana is the perfect place to experience the heart of Rio de Janeiro.
Sip coconut water along the golden sands, admire the legendary Copacabana Palace, and explore the very streets where Bossa Nova was born.
What better place to discover Rio’s culture than in its most iconic neighborhood? Here, African, Indigenous, and Portuguese influences come together, offering some of the city’s best food in a setting that blends history, music, and breathtaking scenery.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/rio/food-tours-rio-de-janeiro/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at Group & Private Tours and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the "Voucher Code" section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) We offer a [upgraded drinks package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/rio/upgraded-drinks-package/) that gives you 3 drinks.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/rio/food-tours-rio-de-janeiro/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
We will meet at Av. Atlântica, 00 - QC 05 - Copacabana, Rio de Janeiro - RJ, 22010-000. Your guide will be holding an orange Secret Food Tours umbrella.
Where will the tour end?
In Leme
Is the tour suitable for vegetarians or people with dietary restrictions?
Yes, we do our best to accommodate dietary needs. Just let us know in advance when booking so we can plan suitable tastings for you.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome to our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us: you will not need to go to the restaurant after the food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drink we buy is top-quality, fresh and local!
What happens if it rains?
Our tours run rain or shine! Just bring an umbrella or light rain jacket — the food will taste just as good.
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
What should I wear?
Comfortable shoes are a must, we’ll be walking through Copacabana’s lively streets. Light clothing, sunscreen, sunglasses, and a hat are also recommended.
Can I bring phones or cameras?
Yes, absolutely! We recommend having a small bag or purse so you can keep your belongings secure.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.

## What You'll Do
Discover the flavors, stories, and spirit of Iceland’s capital on our Reykjavík Food Tour through the city’s vibrant downtown. Nestled between mountains and sea, this colorful Nordic city blends centuries-old traditions with bold culinary innovation. From the comforting warmth of local bakeries to the hearty flavors of Icelandic seafood, every bite tells a story of resilience, creativity, and connection to nature.
As we explore Reykjavík’s charming streets, you’ll experience the city through its food and its people. We’ll begin with a delicate Icelandic pastry enjoyed in view of the famous Hallgrímskirkja church before strolling along the lively Rainbow Road and bustling Laugavegur. Along the way, the aroma of fresh bread, grilled lamb, and sizzling seafood fills the air. You’ll taste beloved local favorites like Iceland’s famous hot dog and a traditional fish-and-potatoes dish served in one of the city’s oldest diners—true comfort food that embodies the flavors of the North Atlantic.
To finish, we’ll slow down with a cup of rich hot chocolate and a slice of Icelandic Marriage Cake, a sweet symbol of love and hospitality. And of course, no Secret Food Tour would be complete without our delicious Secret Dish, revealed only on the day of your adventure.
Our Reykjavík Food Tour is more than a meal—it’s a chance to taste the real Iceland, uncover hidden gems, and connect with the culture that makes this city so unforgettable.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll begin with a warm, freshly baked Icelandic pastry while taking in views of the stunning Hallgrímskirkja church—one of Reykjavík’s most iconic landmarks.
As we continue down lively Laugavegur, filled with restaurants, nightlife, and shops where we'll stop for a quick taste of Icelandic's rich chocolate.
Next, we’ll stroll along the colorful Rainbow Road and learn how this vibrant street became a symbol of creativity and inclusion in Iceland’s capital.
Then, we’ll visit one of Reykjavík’s oldest diners for a comforting plate of seasonal fish and potatoes, showcasing Iceland’s deep connection to the sea.
We’ll stop to enjoy the city’s most famous snack—the beloved Icelandic hot dog, a simple yet unforgettable local favorite.
To end on a sweet note, you’ll savor a slice of traditional Icelandic Marriage Cake paired with a rich, creamy hot chocolate.
And of course, as with all our tours, there’s our delicious Secret Dish, revealed only on the day of your experience.

## Why Downtown Reykjavík?
Downtown Reykjavík is the heart and soul of Iceland’s culinary and cultural scene. Set against a backdrop of colorful rooftops, mountain views, and the sparkling Faxaflói Bay, this compact and walkable neighborhood is where the city’s food story truly comes alive. From bustling fish markets near the old harbor to cozy cafés and cutting-edge Nordic restaurants, every corner reveals a new taste of Icelandic creativity.
Here, traditional flavors meet modern innovation. You’ll find family-run bakeries serving warm rye breads and classic pastries, local chefs reinventing classic dishes like lamb soup and cod, and small bars pouring craft beers and locally distilled spirits. Along the way, the streets echo with Reykjavík’s vibrant energy, filled with music, art, and the friendly warmth of its people.
So, come explore with us. Downtown Reykjavík is where Iceland’s past and present meet—an inspiring place to taste, learn, and connect through food.

## What you'll do
Discover the true flavors of Modena on an immersive food journey that blends history, tradition, and unforgettable tastes. Your experience begins at the city’s oldest bakery with a sampling of iconic cakes and sweet pastries, before continuing to a local bottega for a guided tasting of authentic, aged balsamic vinegar—one of Modena’s proudest culinary treasures. Inside Mercato Albinelli, you’ll savor multiple ages of celebrated Parmigiano Reggiano, followed by a taste of the city’s beloved round bread with local pesto, perfectly paired with sparkling Lambrusco. A classic Modenese pasta dish awaits at a historic restaurant, complemented by a distinctive local wine, before finishing with artisanal gelato from a favorite neighborhood shop—and a delicious Secret Dish, revealed exclusively to our guests.
### SEE ITINERARY
We begin our delicious food tour at a fascinating local meeting point, where you’ll learn the story of one of Italy’s most iconic operatic tenors, Luciano Pavarotti.
Our first tasting stop is a historic pastry shop loved by locals: Antica Pasticceria San Biagio. Here, the scent of freshly baked pastries and traditional Modenese specialties fills the air.
Next, we explore one of Modena’s most famous treasures: Aceto Balsamico di Modena. We’ll enjoy a tasting inside a family-owned Bottega, discovering the craftsmanship behind this world-renowned product.
We then head to the lively Mercato Albinelli, Modena’s historic covered market founded in 1931. With around 65 stalls offering fresh local products, it’s the perfect place to discover Modena’s beloved cheese and its traditional round flatbread — and learn how locals enjoy them.
Our walk continues into the ancient Piazza Grande, Modena’s historic main square and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Surrounded by the Cathedral and the Ghirlandina Tower, this medieval square is full of atmosphere and local life. Here, we’ll stop at a unique traditional trattoria to taste a classic Modenese pasta dish.
We finish the tour on a sweet note with an artisanal gelato at the local GIOELIA Cremeria.

## Why We Visit Mercato Albinelli?
Opened in 1931, Mercato Albinelli is Modena’s historic covered market, designed in elegant Art Nouveau style with iron and glass architecture. At its center stands a bronze statue of a woman carrying fruit—crafted by sculptor Giuseppe Graziosi—which has become a symbol of Modena’s culinary heritage.
Home to more than 60 local vendors, the market is a lively hub offering fresh produce, handmade pasta, cheeses, cured meats, pastries, flowers, and regional specialties. Many stalls have been run by the same families for generations, giving the market its warm, authentic character.
More than a marketplace, Mercato Albinelli is a beloved social and cultural gathering place—a perfect setting to experience Modena’s deep culinary traditions firsthand.

## Why We Visit Mercato Albinelli?
Opened in 1931, Mercato Albinelli is Modena’s historic covered market, designed in elegant Art Nouveau style with iron and glass architecture. At its center stands a bronze statue of a woman carrying fruit—crafted by sculptor Giuseppe Graziosi—which has become a symbol of Modena’s culinary heritage.
Home to more than 60 local vendors, the market is a lively hub offering fresh produce, handmade pasta, cheeses, cured meats, pastries, flowers, and regional specialties. Many stalls have been run by the same families for generations, giving the market its warm, authentic character.
More than a marketplace, Mercato Albinelli is a beloved social and cultural gathering place—a perfect setting to experience Modena’s deep culinary traditions firsthand.

## Why Vienna Old Town?
Vienna’s historic First District isn’t just the city center it has been the core of Viennese life since Roman times. Today, it remains the gastronomic heart of Vienna, home to everything from 500-year-old bakeries to royal cafés that shaped the city’s world-famous coffeehouse culture.
This is where legendary writers, thinkers, musicians, poets, and revolutionaries once gathered in lively bars and cafés, creating the atmosphere that defined Vienna for generations. Many of these venues still operate today, offering an authentic look into the city’s past. Though its not just culinary history.
As we explore, your local food guide won’t just show you the major landmarks and grand boulevards you’ll also step into hidden passageways, family run restaurants, old taverns, and locals favourites that most tourists walk past without noticing. These tucked-away spots are where you’ll taste some of the city’s best traditional dishes and discover its real character.
Vienna Old Town is the ideal place to experience the city’s food, history, and everyday culture all in one walk both the famous icons and the secret corners that bring Vienna to life.

## What you'll do
Discover the elegance, flavour, and Imperial charm of Austria’s capital on our Vienna Food Tour, winding through the grand boulevards and story-filled lanes of the historic city centre. Known for its grand palaces, iconic cafés, and timeless culinary traditions, Vienna blends refined imperial sophistication with local hearty, soul-warming comfort food. Highlights include warm apple strudels from timeless cafés, to schnitzels with rich and warming goulash, as well as local wine spritzes from the city’s very own vineyards. Every sip and bite reflects Vienna’s history and love for food, drinks and gemütlichkeit, the warm feeling of comfort and togetherness.
With your own local expert, explore authentic and hidden spots in the city center. Learn what makes a true Schnitzel, dine in century-old wine cellars, and sample workers’ classics and beloved street-stand favorites. Discover the fascinating culinary history of Vienna’s historic quarter as you waltz past the city’s most famous landmarks and hidden gems. You’ll experience the city through its most beloved dishes and the stories behind them.

Enjoy a warm Apple Strudel and learn about Vienna's iconic café culture.
Indulge in the beloved Buchteln, soft and pillowy sweet buns that showcase Vienna’s love for comforting desserts.

English
## What you'll do
Experience the culinary soul of Mexico’s Riviera Maya on our Playa del Carmen Food Tour, a journey that takes you through the city’s leafy pathways, local favorites, and lively community spaces. While Playa del Carmen is famous for its beaches and vibrant energy, its most authentic flavors live in the places where locals gather to cook, share, and celebrate everyday life. Blending Mayan heritage, coastal traditions, and contemporary creativity, this tour invites you to savor the dishes, and the stories, that make this seaside town unforgettable.
Our adventure begins at a charming local spot where we’ll explore how Playa del Carmen evolved from a quiet ferry crossing along ancient Mayan trade routes into a beloved destination that still carries the warmth and spirit of its origins.
We’ll continue through one of the town’s vibrant residential areas. Here, you’ll learn about the importance of coastal ingredients, the ancient Mayan practice of using volcanic stone tools for grinding, and how Caribbean influences shape the region’s culinary identity today.
As we walk, we’ll pass a lively local sports hub that reflects how community, culture, and soccer intertwine in everyday Mexican life. It’s also the perfect moment to share stories about the Maya’s historic ballgame, pok-ta-pok, a ritual sport deeply tied to cosmology, ceremony, and communal tradition.
Our path leads to a beloved family restaurant where we’ll discuss how regional recipes blend influences from Maya cooking, Spanish settlement, and centuries of culinary adaptation.
Next, we’ll visit a bustling neighborhood market filled with color, aromas, and local flavor. Along the way, we’ll highlight ancient Mayan ingredients still central to regional cuisine, maize, cacao, chiles, and achiote and their roles in daily life and ceremonial traditions.
Our journey concludes in a lively pedestrian area filled with creativity, crafts, and the cultural rhythm of Playa del Carmen, bringing together everything we’ve tasted and discovered. Our Playa del Carmen Food Tour is more than a tasting; it’s a rich journey through the flavors, stories, and history of the Riviera Maya. From ancient Mayan legacies to modern culinary artistry, you’ll discover the true spirit of Playa del Carmen through its food, its people, and its traditions.
### SEE ITINERARY
We kick off our adventure with our exciting Secret Dish, revealed only on the day of your tour. A fun and delicious surprise to start your Playa del Carmen food journey.
Next, enjoy a lively tequila tasting, celebrating Mexico’s most beloved spirit and a perfect way to dive into local culture.
Our third stop brings you the most incredible guacamole served in a traditional molcajete along with a super-fresh pescado taco. Here, you’ll discover how coastal ingredients, ancient Mayan grinding tools, and Caribbean influences shape the vibrant flavors of the region.
We’ll then visit a traditional family-run restaurant to enjoy homemade-style al pastor quesadillas and classic nopal. As you eat, we’ll chat about how local recipes blend Mayan traditions with Spanish influences.
At the lively local market, you’ll cool off with a refreshing glass of horchata de piña, a tropical Mexican drink that perfectly captures the Riviera Maya’s sunny spirit.
Get ready for the highlight of Yucatecan cuisine: cochinita pibil. This tender, slow-cooked pork marinated with achiote and sour orange connects directly to ancient Mayan cooking techniques.
We finish with a sweet flourish as you enjoy helado de chocolate maya along with a Mayan chocolate tasting. Here, you’ll discover how cacao, once considered sacred by the Maya, played an essential role in rituals, trade, and royal celebrations.
Upgrade your Playa del Carmen Food Tour with three iconic regional drinks that perfectly complement the vibrant flavors and coastal dishes you'll enjoy.
Margarita
Michelada
Coctel Manati

# Cancun Food Tour
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide
Discover Cancun with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
Also recommended by
 3 - 3.5 hrs
 Downtown Cancún
 6 stops
 12 people max
 English
## What you'll do
Discover the flavors, stories, and spirit of Mexico’s Caribbean coast on our Cancún Food Tour through the city’s lively downtown. Beyond the beaches and turquoise waters, Cancún’s heart beats in its bustling markets, family-run eateries, and colorful plazas where tradition and everyday life meet. Rooted in Mayan heritage and enriched by coastal bounty, this tour invites you to taste authentic Yucatán flavors while uncovering the fascinating history and culture that make this city so unique.
Our journey begins at Mercado 23, Cancún’s oldest and most authentic local market. As we enjoy a torta de lechón, a tender, slow-roasted pork sandwich from a beloved family eatery serving locals since 1975, we’ll step back in time to explore the roots of the Yucatán Peninsula, once home to the mighty Maya civilization. Among the market’s colorful stalls and aromas, you’ll also sip horchata de piña, a refreshing rice-and-pineapple drink that perfectly captures the region’s tropical soul.
Next, we’ll savor the comforting sopa de lima, a fragrant Yucatecan chicken-and-lime soup that embodies the balance of brightness and warmth found in local cuisine. As you enjoy this traditional dish, you’ll hear stories of Spanish colonization and how it reshaped the region’s identity and cuisine, blending Indigenous and European influences into something distinctly Yucatecan.
Our adventure continues to Mercado 28, another of Cancún’s most important and bustling markets. Here, we’ll taste a panucho de cochinita pibil, a beloved local bite that represents centuries of culinary evolution. We’ll also trace Cancún’s own remarkable history, from its humble beginnings as a small fishing village in the 1970s to its rebirth as a world-class destination known not just for its beaches, but for its vibrant local life, resilience, and enduring traditions.
Then comes our Secret Dish, a surprise treat revealed only on the day of your tour. A delicious bite that bridges the flavors of the past and present, offering a taste of Cancún’s ever-evolving culinary spirit.
Finally, we’ll end at Parque de las Palapas, the cultural heart of the city. As we enjoy quesadillas fritas and paletas de frutas con crema, you’ll experience Cancún at its most authentic, filled with families, music, and laughter. Here, we’ll share stories of Mexico’s colorful celebrations, including Día de los Muertos, a heartfelt tradition that honors life, memory, and community.
Our Cancún Food Tour is more than a tasting, it’s a journey through history, culture, and resilience. From ancient Maya legacies to modern creativity, you’ll discover the true spirit of Cancún through its food, its stories, and its people.
### SEE ITINERARY
We’ll begin our journey at the charming park across from Mercado 23. Here, you’ll enjoy a flavorful torta de lechón and a refreshing horchata de piña, a beloved combination that has delighted generations of Cancún locals.
As we make our way through downtown Cancún, we’ll stop to taste sopa de lima, a fragrant Yucatecan soup that perfectly balances the brightness of lime with the warmth of local spices.
Our next destination is Mercado 28. Here, you’ll try a panucho de cochinita pibil, a crisp tortilla filled with refried beans and topped with marinated pork, pickled onions, and fresh herbs. Truly a dish that represents the heart of Yucatecan cuisine.
This stop will surprise you with our Secret Dish, made specially for you. Be prepared for a unique and unforgettable flavor experience!
Next, we’ll visit Parque de las Palapas, the cultural heart of Cancún. Here, you’ll experience the city’s most traditional street foods, including quesadillas fritas, golden and crispy, a true Mexican delicacy.
We’ll conclude our tour at the plaza by the Ayuntamiento, enjoying paletas de frutas con crema, creamy fruit popsicles that offer a sweet, refreshing finish. Surrounded by families and the vibrant energy of local life, it’s the perfect way to end your Cancún adventure.
Upgrade your Cancún Food Tour with three classic drinks that go great with the authentic Yucatecan flavors and market street food you’ll be trying.

Mezcal

## Why Downtown Cancún?
Colorful, lively, and full of flavor, Downtown Cancún is where the true soul of the city reveals itself. Beyond the resorts and beaches, this vibrant neighborhood beats with local life, a place where history, culture, and cuisine come together in every sight, sound, and aroma. The streets are lined with bustling markets, cheerful food stalls, and family-run eateries serving authentic Yucatecan dishes that capture the essence of Mexico’s Caribbean coast.
Here, ancient traditions meet everyday creativity. You’ll taste regional favorites like cochinita pibil and sopa de lima, sip refreshing horchata de piña, and watch skilled hands craft tortillas and tamales just as they have for generations. Around you, murals, music, and warm smiles tell the story of a community proud of its roots, from its Mayan heritage to its transformation into a modern, thriving city.
This is Downtown Cancún!! A great place to eat, to connect, to celebrate local life, and to discover the authentic flavors that make this city unforgettable.

BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/cancun/food-tours-cancun/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) We offer a [upgraded drinks package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/cancun/upgraded-drinks-package/) that gives you 3 drinks.
Where is the meeting spot?
Your guide will meet you at Sm 23 Park, Supermanzana 23, 77500 Cancún. If you have any questions, just email us.
Where the tour will end?
The tour will end at Ayuntamiento De Cancún, Av. Tulúm 5-5, 77500 Cancún, Q.R.
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
We can accommodate vegetarians at all stops, as well as guests requiring gluten- or dairy-free options. Please note that cross-contamination cannot be fully guaranteed.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
Great question! Our food tour does involve some walking, as we explore various food and historical stops along a 45mins walk. To fully enjoy the experience, we highly recommend wearing comfortable shoes that will keep you at ease throughout the tour. Additionally, we encourage you to bring sunscreen to protect yourself from the sun, as we'll be exploring both outdoor and indoor locations!
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drink we buy is top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Is this tour wheelchair accessible?
No, unfortunately, this tour is not wheelchair accessible.
What about the weather? What should I wear?
Tours run rain or shine and we will be stopping inside markets, shops, and restaurants throughout the tour. Cancún is very sunny, so we recommend wearing comfortable shoes, bringing an umbrella for sun or rain, and keeping some cash handy for public bathrooms at the markets.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Cancun?

Discover the flavors, stories, and soul of Oslo’s trendiest neighborhood on our Grünerløkka Food Tour, a journey through the vibrant cafés, creative kitchens, and Nordic traditions that define this beloved district. Once an industrial quarter, Grünerløkka has transformed into the cultural heart of Oslo, blending bohemian charm with cutting-edge Scandinavian food culture. Here, local craftsmanship, sustainability, and deep respect for nature guide every dish.
From local bakeries with fresh warm cinnamon buns, sustainable farm to table restaurants with responsibly sourced foods, bustling food markets and traditional dishes, follow your local guide through Oslo’s coolest neighbourhoods to taste the best of Nordic cuisine.

Culinary Tours Bilbao
Skip the tourist traps and truly discover Bilbao with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals.
On our Secret Food Tour: Bilbao, you’ll enjoy some of the best pintxos in the Basque Country while exploring the city’s rich culinary culture. You’ll discover an authentic foodie experience and the unique way locals eat and socialize, indulging in a carefully selected variety of pintxos, local red wine, txakoli and cider, and, of course, a sweet treat.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated Upgraded Drink Package is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
You’ll start on an authentic pintxos bar opened in 1982, a true local favorite in Bilbao. Here, you’ll taste iconic Idiazabal cheese, served in a giant homemade croquette—crispy on the outside and rich and melted inside.
We’ll take you to a mix of classic and modern pintxos bars in Bilbao, where you’ll learn how to pour natural Basque cider and enjoy two delicious pintxos: a traditional talo with txistorra and a fresh anchovy and avocado pintxo.
We’ll continue the tour at a classic French-style pintxos bar, where you’ll enjoy a pulled beef pintxo with rich gravy on soft brioche bread, perfectly complemented by a crisp glass of txakoli.
We’ll head to a modern, new-wave pintxos bar, where you’ll enjoy a spider crab pintxo and a melting cheddar and beef pintxo, perfectly paired with a glass of Rioja.
Our next stop is a historic spot opened in 1903, featuring unique modernist décor, where you’ll taste a luxurious Iberian ham mousse.
We’ll finish the tour at a historic pastry shop running since 1852, where you’ll indulge in their bestselling chocolate Palmera, flaky, buttery, and irresistibly sweet.
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.
Marianito – Classic Bilbao Cocktail

Meeting Spot
Your guide will meet you in front of **Iglesia de San Antón**, next to Mercado de la Ribera. If you have any questions, just [email us](mailto:bilbao@secrettours.com).
Iglesia de San Antón, Mercado de la Ribera

Mon - Sat @ 11:00a.m. Sun @ 10:30a.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella and a big smile, so that they are easy to spot.
## Why Bilbao?
We believe that if you are an avid foodie and an enthusiastic traveler, Bilbao should be on your list. This dynamic Basque city offers a perfect balance of culture, history, and exceptional food. From its industrial roots to its modern transformation, Bilbao showcases the essence of Basque identity through its architecture, traditions, and gastronomy. The city’s strong cultural heritage and proud local character will make you fall in love not only with Bilbao, but with the Basque Country and its people.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/bilbao/food-tours-bilbao/#BOOKING)

€89.99
Secret Food Tours - Bilbao
Favorito de los locales
En nuestro Bilbao Food Tour, probarás auténticos pintxos vascos y especialidades regionales en los animados bares y plazas de la ciudad.
3 - 3.5 horas
Bilbao
6 stops
12 people max
[BOOK NOW](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/bilbao/food-tours-bilbao-es/) [Learn More](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/bilbao/food-tours-bilbao-es/)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) We offer a [upgraded drinks package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/bilbao/upgraded-drinks-package/) that gives you 1 drink, tax included.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/bilbao/food-tours-bilbao/#CONTACT) here and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
In front of Iglesia de San Antón. Next to Mercado de La Riviera. If you have any questions.
[»»CLICK HERE FOR DIRECTIONS««](https://maps.app.goo.gl/YZrmSftDXaxhi24WA)
Where the tour will end?
The tour finish next to [Palacio de la Diputación Foral de Bizkaia](https://maps.app.goo.gl/63fm8FEpifcCVx9YA)
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please contact us [bilbao@secrettours.com](mailto:bilbao@secrettours.com) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
Think of the tour as a tasting menu. As we travel you get many delicious treats that comprise a meal with dessert. But there is a lot of food, so you will not be hungry after the tour.
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 30 mins of walking but it is not too difficult and is broken up between food stops. During the duration of the tour we will walk just over 2 kilometers. The guide walks at the pace of the tour members, you will never be rushed.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drinks are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Bilbao. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Bilbao?
Book our award-winning Secret Food Tours - Bilbao before it fills up!

## What you'll do
On our Ljubljana Food Tour, join your expert local guide and taste your way through Ljubljana, discovering its flavours, stories, and culture on a journey through the heart of the city. Led by passionate locals who truly live and breathe Ljubljana’s food scene, we take you beyond the obvious and straight to the places locals love most cosy café corners, tucked-away bakeries, and neighbourhood favourites that quietly define the city’s culinary soul.
Stroll through this leafy capital of cobblestone streets and story-filled lanes, uncovering unforgettable flavours along the way. From traditional Slovenian comfort dishes to modern interpretations that reflect the city’s creative, evolving food culture, each stop reveals a new side of Ljubljana. And of course, there’s our Secret Dish a signature surprise revealed only on the day.
From local wines and pastries to hearty stews, artisan cured meats, cheeses, and seasonal produce, this is the ultimate way to experience the real Ljubljana. Whether you’re a curious traveller or a devoted food lover, you’ll discover the city one flavour, one story, and one delicious surprise at a time.
Come hungry. Leave happy.
### SEE ITINERARY
Hot-from-the-oven, freshly baked burek, crisp on the outside and soft inside
A colourful narezek platter stacked with locally cured meats, regional cheeses, and house pickles, paired with a glass of Slovenian white wine
A guided tasting of Slovenian truffles, extra virgin olive oil, and pumpkin seed oil at a family-owned local speciality shop
An authentic Slovenian sausage, served warm with traditional sauces on the side
A rich, slow-cooked Slovenian goulash, served with soft, golden-baked štruklji
Traditional Slovenian dumplings, finished with walnuts and cinnamon, seasonal berries, cream, and vanilla ice cream, paired with a glass of Belica (traditional Slovenian Muscat) and Keroš, a local Slovenian sweet wine
As with all our tours, enjoy our Secret Dish, revealed on the day of your tour.

# Orlando Food Tour
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide
Discover Orlando with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
Also recommended by
 3 hrs
 Downtown Orlando
 6 stops
 14 people max
 English
## What you'll do
Experience the authentic soul of the "City Beautiful" on our Secret Food Tour: Orlando. While most travelers never leave the theme parks, we invite you to explore the historic heart of the city—the vibrant Downtown district and the lush, swan-filled shores of Lake Eola Park. As we walk beneath historic oaks draped in Spanish moss, you’ll discover how Orlando transformed from a quiet citrus hub into a world-class culinary crossroads, blending Old Florida charm with a sophisticated, modern food scene.
Your journey features a curated selection of Orlando’s most legendary local eats. We’ll dive into the city's celebrated Asian fusion scene and savor the authentic, hand-crafted flavors of Colombian street food and traditional pastries. You will also enjoy a local favorite sandwich, a staple that has defined the downtown lunch scene for years. Each stop is chosen to highlight the diverse cultures and passionate artisans that make this neighborhood a true foodie destination.
Beyond the plate, this tour offers an insider’s perspective on Orlando’s history and architecture. We’ll navigate hidden walkways and vibrant street art while sharing the stories behind the city’s most iconic landmarks. To conclude our adventure, we’ll visit a final location for our Secret Dish, a decadent dessert that perfectly captures the sweet spirit of Central Florida. Join us for a delicious exploration of the "Real Orlando" that will leave you both full and inspired.
### SEE ITINERARY
We begin our adventure in the heart of the Central Business District. Here, you’ll dive into the city's celebrated Asian fusion scene with a serving of Sticky Pork Bites which are perfectly glazed, tender, and packed with bold, umami flavor.
Next, we explore Orlando's rich Latin influence with a fresh Quesito. This classic Caribbean pastry features flaky, golden puff pastry filled with sweetened cream cheese, offering a delicate balance of textures that locals crave.
We continue our exploration with authentic Colombian Street Food. You'll experience the vibrant, soul-warming flavors that have made Colombian cuisine a cornerstone of the Central Florida community.
After our savory starts, we’ll take a guided walk through the iconic Lake Eola Park. Enjoy the breeze off the water and the sight of the famous swan boats as your guide shares stories of Orlando’s transformation from a citrus town to a bustling metropolis.
Tucked away near the park, we’ll visit a true "hidden gem" to sample a unique sandwich favorited by locals. This isn't just a meal; it’s am Orlando institution, known for its quality, no-frills approach to Floridian comfort food and the loyal following it has built over the years.
Every adventure needs a sweet ending so we'll wrap up our exploration of Orlando tastes with an icy treat to cool us down at our signature Secret spot.
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances.

Meeting Spot
Your guide will meet you outside A La Cart – City at 274 N Orange Ave, Orlando, FL 32801. If you have any questions, just [email us](mailto:orlando@secrettours.com).

Daily 11:00a.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella and a big smile, so that they are easy to spot.
## Why Downtown Orlando?
Step away from the neon lights of crowded theme parks and into the historic heart of the "City Beautiful." Downtown Orlando and Lake Eola Park represent the authentic soul of Central Florida, where century-old brick streets meet a cutting-edge culinary landscape. This neighborhood is the perfect backdrop for a food tour because it offers a sense of place and history that most visitors never get to see, all centered around the stunning centerpiece of the city’s most famous park.
Downtown Orlando is a vibrant mix of heritage and innovation. As we walk through the Lake Eola Park and the central business district, you’ll see beautifully preserved 1920s architecture standing alongside modern art installations and lush tropical gardens. This area has become a magnet for the city’s most talented independent chefs, who have moved away from the tourist hubs to create a grassroots food scene fueled by local ingredients and global heritage.
From the iconic white swans of Lake Eola to the bustling energy of the local restaurant scene, this neighborhood is where Orlando’s diverse community comes together. Whether it’s the rich influence of the city’s Latin American residents or the bold creativity of its Asian-inspired kitchens, the flavors found here are as varied as the people who call the city home. On our tour, you aren't just eating; you are experiencing the true culture and community of Orlando’s most storied district.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/orlando/food-tours-orlando/#BOOKING)
## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above.
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot here](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/orlando/food-tours-orlando/#CONTACT) and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
You'll meet your guide outside the A La Cart - City at 274 N Orange Ave, Orlando, FL 32801. Your guide will be there with an orange umbrella and a big smile!
Where the tour will end?
The tour will end at Thornton Park neighborhood; about 1 mile from starting location
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please contact us at [orlando@secrettours.com](mailto:orlando@secrettours.com) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 45 minutes walking but it's not too difficult and there are no hills or stairs. If you walk slowly, then our guide will walk slowly as well.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 14 people maximum. If you are more than 14 people, please do not hesitate to email us and will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drinks are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Orlando. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Orlando?
Book our award-winning Secret Food Tours - Orlando before it fills up!

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour Helsinki, get onboard a journey through one of the most secretive country of the Nordic Regions.
Step into the heart of authentic Helsinki life with our visit to Kallio, meaning "The Rock" in suomi. This district pulses with a vibrant energy, fueled by the diverse spirits of its residents. It is a world away from the glossy, commercial core of the city center and the predictable rows of well-known international brand shops.
Kallio offers a stark, captivating contrast—a neighborhood where history, community, and contemporary Finnish bohemian culture thrive. We invite you to immerse yourself in this unique atmosphere.
Our guides will take you deep into the streets, uncovering the hidden narratives and local hangouts that define this area. This is more than just a sightseeing tour; it is a cultural deep dive that will reveal the Finnish life and identity. Discover why "The Rock" is the true beating, counter-cultural heart of Helsinki.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/helsinki/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.

The journey starts with our welcome bite as the guide explains the journey ahead
Visiting the market you learn all about the original Karelian Piirakka
Time for some cold cuts, depending on the season bear, reindeer, moose and more
Then some salmon and fish toast on the famous arctic bread accompanied with a small glass of beer
After a short walk, the guide will take you to a local institution for a heart warming soup and a Finnish classic longdrink
Time to burn some calories going uphill to reach our secret location for our last sweet tasting with a comforting drink
Upgrade your Secret Food Tour: Helsinki and enjoy a drink selection chosen to pair with the food you’ll be served.
Go large on the beer at the Ekstromin and try another Finnish Beer from the tap at Juttutupa

## Why Kallio?
Kallio, meaning "the Rock" in Finnish (Suomi), is a former working-class district separated from the affluent city center by just one passage: the Pitkäsilta, or "long bridge."
We designed this tour to offer you a glimpse into the authentic local lifestyle, moving beyond the well-trodden, tourist-heavy path of Esplanadi's grand avenues.
Much like iconic neighborhoods such as Soho or Pigalle, Kallio—which was the workers’ district in the early 1900s—still retains a distinct village atmosphere. It pulses with life thanks to its vibrant mix of clubs, bars, and nightlife, where locals, students, and artists actively push back against the area's slow march toward gentrification.

## Why Carmel-by-the-Sea?
Carmel-by-the-Sea is a destination for travelers seeking beauty, charm, and a slower, more romantic pace of life. On our Secret Food Tour of Carmel by the Sea, you will enjoy its fairy-tale cottages, tree-lined streets, and European village feel that create an atmosphere unlike anywhere else in California.
Visitors are drawn to its pristine white-sand beach, dramatic coastal views, and peaceful ambiance, all just minutes from iconic destinations like Big Sur and the 17-Mile Drive. With a deep artistic heritage and a commitment to preserving its character, Carmel feels both timeless and intimate.
The town also offers exceptional food, wine, and culture in a compact, walkable setting. Award-winning restaurants, cozy bistros, and elegant wine-tasting rooms highlight local ingredients and Monterey County vintages. Art galleries, historic landmarks like the Carmel Mission, and boutique shopping add depth to the experience, making it easy to spend days exploring at a relaxed pace. Whether you’re visiting for romance, coastal scenery, culinary indulgence, or creative inspiration, Carmel-by-the-Sea delivers a refined and unforgettable California escape.

Where does your food come from?

Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.

## What you'll do
Get ready to uncover the sun-kissed flavors of the "Venice of America" on our Fort Lauderdale food tour. We’ll escape the beach crowds to explore the palm-lined elegance of the Las Olas district, where a vibrant mix of historic charm and modern culinary innovation awaits. From artisanal treats to savory coastal classics, you’ll discover why this neighborhood is the true heart of the city’s food scene.
Your journey begins with a sweet, pillowy introduction to South Florida’s bakery culture, featuring everything from tropical-inspired donuts to traditional, cream-filled Italian pastries. As we stroll along the breezy New River, we’ll dive into the city's Mediterranean heritage with a perfectly pressed caprese panini and authentic coal-fired pizza, blistered to perfection. We’ll finish our adventure on a cool note with a scoop of handcrafted, silky gelato that tastes just like a summer afternoon.
Along the way, your local expert will share insider stories of Fort Lauderdale’s transformation from a quiet river outpost to a world-class culinary destination. Between bites, you’ll navigate hidden courtyards and admire the Mediterranean Revival architecture that gives Las Olas its unique soul. Traveling with a group? We also offer private tour options tailored to your party’s needs.
### SEE ITINERARY
A Sweet Tropical Welcome – We kick off our journey with a signature donut that captures the sunny spirit of Florida through creative, vibrant flavors and a light, airy dough.
A Stroll Through History – Between bites, we’ll walk the palm-shaded sidewalks of Las Olas Boulevard to learn how this iconic thoroughfare transformed from a simple dirt road into the city’s premiere destination for style and flavor.
The Perfect Press – We’ll head to a local bakery for a classic caprese panini, featuring fresh mozzarella, ripe tomatoes, and fragrant basil pressed to crispy, melted perfection.
Coal-Fired Excellence – Experience the deep, smoky char of authentic coal-fired pizza, topped with premium ingredients and baked in a traditional high-heat oven for a signature blistered crust.
Handcrafted Silk – To round out our savory adventure, we’ll stop for a scoop of artisan gelato, churned daily using old-world techniques for a dense, silky finish.
The Secret Dish – Shhh! It’s a surprise. We’ve tucked away one extra special local bite that we only reveal to our guests on the day of the tour.

## Why Las Olas?
Stroll down Las Olas Boulevard and you’ll quickly realize why it’s considered the soul of Fort Lauderdale. This iconic thoroughfare, whose name aptly translates to "The Waves," serves as a stunning bridge between the city’s shimmering skyscrapers and the Atlantic shore. Originally a simple dirt path carved through the mangroves in the early 1900s, it has evolved into a world-class destination defined by Mediterranean Revival architecture, swaying palms, and a sophisticated, breezy energy.
The neighborhood is a tapestry of hidden courtyards, independent boutiques, and prestigious art galleries, all framed by the charming New River. While the yachts gliding through the nearby "Venice of America" canals capture the eye, the true magic of Las Olas lies in its culinary evolution. It’s a place where old-school Florida charm meets a daring new food scene, making it the perfect backdrop for an unforgettable afternoon of discovery.

## Why Santa Barbara?
Santa Barbara is a true food lovers paradise. Its culinary scene thrives on fresh coastal ingredients, local vineyards, and a commitment to farm-to-table quality. Visitors can savor everything from world-class seafood—like uni, halibut, and spot prawns—to handcrafted tacos, gourmet bakeries, and innovative California cuisine.
It is a destination where natural beauty and rich cultural history come together, creating an unforgettable experience for any visitor. Known as the “American Riviera,” the city offers stunning Mediterranean-style landscapes—red-tile roofs, palm-lined streets, and sweeping views of the Pacific Ocean.
Santa Barbara invites exploration through its historic sites, including the iconic Old Mission, the Spanish Colonial architecture of El Presidio, and the lively waterfront that reflects centuries of coastal heritage. Whether you're strolling State Street, wandering through the Funk Zone’s art-filled district, or eating your way through the city, Santa Barbara offers a delicious and distinctly local taste of California’s Central Coast at every turn.

## What you'll do
Join Secret Food Tours on a food tour through downtown Santa Barbara. You'll dine in several locally owned restaurants, trying some of their signature and unique dishes.
Learn the story of a city that started with the native Chumach, grew with the Spanish and became an epicenter for film making, shopping and food. At the end of your Downtown food tour of Santa Barbara, you'll have sampled some of this city’s most delicious dishes and gained a better understanding of its culture and history.
Your Santa Barbara food tour starts here as you travel back through history and exhilarate your taste buds. All the food will be served with water.

## Why Santa Barbara?
Santa Barbara is a true food lovers paradise. Its culinary scene thrives on fresh coastal ingredients, local vineyards, and a commitment to farm-to-table quality. Visitors can savor everything from world-class seafood—like uni, halibut, and spot prawns—to handcrafted tacos, gourmet bakeries, and innovative California cuisine.
It is a destination where natural beauty and rich cultural history come together, creating an unforgettable experience for any visitor. Known as the “American Riviera,” the city offers stunning Mediterranean-style landscapes—red-tile roofs, palm-lined streets, and sweeping views of the Pacific Ocean.
Santa Barbara invites exploration through its historic sites, including the iconic Old Mission, the Spanish Colonial architecture of El Presidio, and the lively waterfront that reflects centuries of coastal heritage. Whether you're strolling State Street, wandering through the Funk Zone’s art-filled district, or eating your way through the city, Santa Barbara offers a delicious and distinctly local taste of California’s Central Coast at every turn.

How do I order?

Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?

How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.

All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
