Where does your food come from?
The food and drinks are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable. Bring an umbrella and we will try to be more indoors than out. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.

Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.

Wander through the lively City Market, then make your way down iconic Broughton Street, where history, culture, and local flavor collide.

## Why Savannah’s Historic Center?
In the heart of Savannah’s Historic Center, lined with beautifully preserved architecture, shaded squares, and walkable streets, this neighborhood creates an inviting backdrop for exploring Savannah’s food scene on foot.
The Historic District is home to a diverse collection of locally owned restaurants, bakeries, and speciality food shops, many of which draw inspiration from Southern, coastal, and Lowcountry traditions. This concentration of culinary stops allows our tour to showcase a wide variety of flavors within a short, easy walk, making the experience historic, relaxed, and engaging.

Where the tour will end?

Can you accommodate dietary restrictions or allergies?

I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?

I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?

Is there enough food during your tour?

If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
What should I bring with me?
We recommend bringing water, comfortable shoes, weather-appropriate gear, and a positive appetite for food and history!
How early should I arrive?
Please arrive 10–15 minutes early to allow time for check-in and introductions.
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable. Bring an umbrella and we will try to be more indoors than out. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Is the tour family-friendly?
Yes, the tour is family-friendly. However, alcohol tastings are reserved for guests 21 and over with valid ID.
Do I need to bring an ID?
Yes. A valid government-issued ID is required for guests who would like to participate in any alcohol tastings.
Where does the tour take place?
The tour takes place entirely within Savannah’s Historic District, including famous squares, historic streets, and lively neighborhoods.
Is there seating at each stop?
Some local food stops may not offer seating, so guests should be comfortable standing for short periods.
Does the tour run rain or shine?
Yes! Tours operate rain or shine, so please dress for the weather and bring an umbrella or rain jacket if needed.
What kind of shoes should I wear?
Comfortable walking shoes are strongly recommended. Some streets and sidewalks in Savannah’s Historic District include cobblestones, uneven pavement, and brick paths.
How far do we walk on the tour?
The tour covers approximately 1.7 miles at a relaxed pace with multiple stops along the way.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.

We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.

Pressed for time but craving a real Austin feast? This food tour through the East Side is built for travelers who want to skip the fluff and dive straight into the city’s coolest, most colorful backyard without losing their whole afternoon.
We’ve curated a global mashup of flavors that defines modern Austin, from savory falafel and award-winning Tex-Mex to Asian fusion and local ice cream. You’ll weave through a landscape of vibrant murals and hidden food trucks discovering exactly where the locals hide away.
In just two hours, you’ll master the East Side’s creative culinary scene and still have time to explore or catch a show.
### SEE ITINERARY
Meet up in front of Cosmic Saltillo. A lush garden oasis serving up coffee, brews and cocktails in a unique setting that is the definition of what makes East Austin a top destination for locals to hang out at.
We begin our 2 hour walking food tour with a leisure journey through a small part of the east side and stopping for the first food bite at a local favorite food truck that has grown with the neighborhood. There we will try a delicious falafel bite.
Our journey will continue with a walk through the neighborhood taking in the Historic sites and street art to our 2nd destination, a place that was once a local convenient store turned brewery. There we try a Tex-Mex bite while learning about how this place became a local favorite brewery that has won multiple awards for their craft and a place that is literally off the beaten path.
After that we will then take a short journey to an Asian Fusion food truck that has grown from one food truck to multiple. Try their Asian curry dip with homemade Malaysian-style flatbread.
We then end our food journey with a sweet, unique bite you can’t find anywhere else, from a local place with an Austin cult following.

Monday - Saturday (minus Thursday afternoon) 4pm and 11:00am
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## Why We Visit the Quadrilatero?
The Quadrilatero Market is the historic and gastronomic heart of Bologna, located between Piazza Maggiore and Piazza Santo Stefano. Dating back to the Middle Ages, this area developed around trade guilds, with streets dedicated to meat, fish, bread, and spices.
Rather than a single enclosed market, the Quadrilatero is a lively network of narrow medieval streets—such as Via Pescherie Vecchie, Via Drapperie, and Via Caprarie—lined with family-run salumerie, fresh pasta laboratories, bakeries, cheese shops, fishmongers, and traditional taverns.
The aromas of Parmigiano Reggiano, mortadella, cured meats, fresh bread, and handmade pasta fill the air, creating an atmosphere that feels both timeless and alive. Locals still shop here daily, preserving culinary traditions passed down for generations.
Visiting the Quadrilatero is essential to understanding Bologna’s deep connection to food. It offers an authentic glimpse into everyday local life and the craftsmanship behind the city’s most iconic flavors—making it an unforgettable experience at the very soul of Bologna.
Join Us!
Discover the best of Bologna’s food, culture, and history with passionate local guides who love their city. Whether you’re a dedicated foodie, a culture enthusiast, or simply curious about authentic Bolognese cuisine, this tour offers an unforgettable journey into the flavors and traditions that make Bologna a true culinary capital.

## What you'll do
Join Secret Food Tours on a food tour through the heart of Marseille, starting at the Vieux-Port. You'll sample a variety of local specialties from artisan shops, eateries, and historic bakeries, trying some of their signature and unique dishes. Learn the story of a city that started with Greek sailors from Phocaea around 600 BC, grew into a beautiful, resilient, and multi-layered port, and became a true crossroads of Mediterranean food and culture. At the end of your Marseille food tour, you'll have sampled some of this city’s most delicious dishes and gained a better understanding of its culture and history. Your Marseille food tour starts here as you travel back through history and exhilarate your taste buds.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/marseille/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
We'll start at a historic port landmark to set the scene in France's oldest city and introduce the historic harbor.
Next, you'll bite into the classic street food, a crispy chickpea-flour delicacy, at a local eatery.
Moving on, we'll explore the rich Tapenade, savor the local Pastis, and discuss a third essential Provençal flavor.
We then step inside an artisan wine shop for a superb cheese and wine pairing guided by a local expert.
Our walk will take us past an elegant landmark, a symbol of the city's resilience.
For a sweet treat, you'll choose between two traditional Provençal desserts at a pastry shop.
A savory interlude follows for a perfect Croque Monsieur at a bistro stop.
We'll then visit a vibrant modern market hall before a stop at an artisan workshop to learn the history of the region's famous soap.
Our final treat awaits at an artisan chocolatier with a unique bean-to-bar tasting.
Finally, you’ll taste the iconic Navette biscuit at the city's oldest bakery, before concluding at a scenic viewpoint over the city and its harbor.

## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) We offer a upgraded drinks package that gives you extra drinks.
Where is the meeting spot for Marseille’s tours?
We’ll meet outside of Vieux Port metro station. If you have any questions. Please [email us](mailto:marseille@secrettours.com).
Where will the Marseille tour end ?
Just outside the Saint Victor Abbey.
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
You can always tell our guide of any food allergies or special dietary requirements.
This tour is vegetarian friendly but some tasting might not be replaced . Please take note that this tour is NOT adapted for Vegans or Celiac.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome to our tour. We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone has enjoyed the tour. However, please be informed that any accompanying underage children must be carried, as the area is not suitable for strollers. You will have to carry and keep your baby on your lap as well when seated to enjoy our picnic at the end of the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like!
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
There will be around 1 hour and half walking but it is not too difficult.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese?
No, our guide will take care of everything.
Where does your food come from?
We work with small shops and local artisan hand picked for the quality of their products and services.
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Paris. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we go through with the tour with 4 people minimum! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Accessibility wheelchair
This tour is accessible to people in wheelchairs but note that there is a bit of walking going upwards.
Is the tour suitable for pregnant women?
Pregnancy can be accomodated, some tasting might change to meet with your restrictions.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.

Book our award-winning Secret Food Tours - Marseille before it fills up!

text:
# Express Food Tour: Boston

# Express Food Tour: Boston
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide
Discover Boston with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
Also recommended by
 2 hours
 East Austin
 5 stops
 20 people max
 English
## What you'll do
Dive into the historic grit and gourmet soul of the "Cradle of Liberty" on our Express Food Tour in Boston. We’ll bypass the typical tourist traps to explore the city’s oldest corridors, shifting from the maritime majesty of the waterfront to the legendary cobblestone streets of the North End.
Our journey begins at the iconic Quincy Market, the crown jewel of the city’s 19th-century urban renewal. Standing beneath its massive copper dome, you’ll discover the market's transformation from a wholesale meat center to a world-class gathering spot. We’ll skip the standard food court fare to savor a cup of award-winning New England Clam Chowder, thick with local cream and hand-shucked clams, while admiring the Greek Revival architecture that has stood for nearly 200 years.
As we cross into the North End, the aroma of roasted garlic and fresh-baked bread signals our arrival in Boston’s oldest residential neighborhood. We’ll duck down a narrow, hidden alleyway to find a traditional salumeria, a local treasure where the rafters are hung with aging provolone and spicy meats. Our walk continues past the home of Paul Revere to a neighborhood institution for a slice of thin-crust pizza, known for its massive size and perfectly charred crust. Along the way, we'll spot the "Skinny House"—a local curiosity built entirely out of sibling spite—and hear the strange history of the Great Molasses Flood.
We conclude our adventure at the Boston Public Market, a vibrant indoor hub that celebrates the rugged spirit of New England’s farmers and fishers. Surrounded by stalls of fresh Atlantic seafood and artisan crafts, we’ll gather for our Secret Dish, a savory specialty that captures the essence of Massachusetts heritage. To end on a sweet note, we’ll grab a warm, sugar-dusted boston cream donut, ensuring you leave with a full stomach and a true local's perspective.
### SEE ITINERARY
We begin our adventure in the bustling granite corridors of Quincy Market. Here, you’ll dive into the city's legendary seafood scene with a cup of New England Clam Chowder which is perfectly velvety, herb-infused, and packed with the kind of bold, oceanic flavor that has fueled this port city for centuries.
Next, we explore the narrow, sun-dappled "vias" of the North End with a visit to a tucked-away Salumeria. This classic neighborhood deli features hand-selected Italian meats and sharp, aged provolone sliced right under the hanging rafters, offering a delicate balance of smoky and salty textures that locals crave.
We continue our exploration by weaving through the historic residential streets to enjoy an authentic North End Pizza. You'll experience the vibrant, soul-warming flavors of a perfectly charred, thin-crust slice that has remained a cornerstone of the community’s daily life for generations.
After our savory starts, we’ll take a guided walk past the ancient brickwork of Paul Revere’s House. Enjoy the atmosphere of the cobblestone alleyways and the sight of the Old North Church as your guide shares stories of Boston’s transformation from a revolutionary stronghold to a sophisticated culinary destination.
Tucked away near the rose gardens of the Greenway, we’ll visit a true "hidden gem" inside the Boston Public Market to sample a hand-crafted savory bite. This isn't just a snack; it’s a showcase of regional agriculture, known for its quality, no-frills approach to New England soul food and the loyal following of local artisans it has built over the years.
Every adventure needs a sweet ending, so we'll wrap up our exploration of Boston tastes with a decadent Boston Cream Donut at our signature Secret spot. Featuring a rich custard filling and a smooth chocolate glaze, this treat perfectly captures the indulgent spirit of the city's most famous dessert.
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances.
Book Your Tour
$69.99 / person
Lowest price guarantee
If you have any issues with payment, please email us at [contact@secrettours.com](mailto:contact@secrettours.com) with the city, date and time of tour, and how many adult and child's tickets you need so we can assist you.
**If a tour is fully booked, or you are looking to book a \*Group or Private Tour - please email us about [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) options.**
\*Please note - Group & Private Tours are specifically organised for your group and therefore subject to a premium price.
Looking to gift a memorable experience?
We've got you!
[Buy as a gift](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/buy-gift/)
What you'll drink
### THE FOOD
Clam Chowder
Cheese and Prosciutto Di Parma
Pizza
Boston Cream Donut
Meeting Spot
We'll meet in front of Samuel Adams Memorial, 6 Faneuil Hall Sq, Boston, MA 02109, United States. If you have any questions. Please [email us](mailto:austin@secrettours.com).
Samuel Adams Memorial, 6 Faneuil Hall Sq, Boston, MA 02109, United States
[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/fTycbHrG3JjiExSq5)
Daily 1.00p.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## Why North End?
The North End is Boston’s oldest residential neighborhood and one of its most flavorful. Known as the city’s Little Italy, this historic area is packed with narrow streets, brick buildings, and generations-old family businesses that have shaped Boston’s food culture for over a century. Just steps away, you’ll find Paul Revere’s House, a powerful reminder of the city’s revolutionary past and the deep historical roots that surround every bite in this neighborhood.
What makes the North End especially ideal for a food tour is its connection to some of Boston’s most iconic gathering places. The neighborhood flows naturally toward Quincy Market and the Boston Public Market, where local producers, bakers, and artisans showcase the best of New England flavors. Within a short walk, you experience classic Italian specialties, historic landmarks, and modern market culture all in one compact area, making the North End a perfect blend of history, atmosphere, and unforgettable food.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/boston/express-food-tours-boston/#BOOKING)

Join us for an unforgettable culinary adventure in the heart of Parma, Italy’s refined capital of taste. On this Secret Food Tour, you’ll explore Piazza Garibaldi and the historic center, where elegant architecture, warm hospitality, and exceptional food traditions come together.
Guided by a passionate local expert, you’ll discover why Parma is considered one of the world’s great food cities. Along the way, enjoy a carefully curated tasting of iconic local charcuterie, aged Parmigiano Reggiano, handmade pasta, and traditional sweets, all thoughtfully paired with regional wines.
As you stroll through historic streets and lively squares, you’ll hear stories of craftsmanship, family tradition, and culinary passion that have shaped Parma’s legendary flavors for centuries.
And of course, just like on every Secret Food Tour, you’ll indulge in our exclusive Secret Dish, revealed only on the day of the tour!
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/parma/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Piazzale San Francesco
Begin your journey in one of Parma’s most elegant and vibrant squares, surrounded by historic palaces and the rhythm of daily local life.
Traditional Salumeria Board
Taste a carefully selected board of Parma’s finest cold cuts, including Prosciutto di Parma, paired with the region’s signature puffy fried bread and a glass of local sparkling white wine.
Parmigiano Reggiano Tasting
Learn the time-honored techniques behind the world’s most famous cheese and sample two different aged varieties, discovering how time transforms flavor and texture.
Erbazzone – Regional Savory Pastry
Enjoy the most iconic savory pastry of the region: a creamy, vegetarian baked specialty made with greens, cheese, and herbs, a true taste of everyday Emilia-Romagna comfort food.
Handmade Pasta Stop
Savor a signature local pasta dish, handmade daily and served in a traditional city-center osteria, representing the heart of Parmense cuisine.
Traditional Sweet Treats
End with a classic local sweet  from a historic shop, a proud expression of Parma’s home-style dessert tradition.
Secret Dish
An exclusive local specialty you won’t find anywhere else, revealed only on your tour.

## Why We Visit Piazza Garibaldi?
Piazza Garibaldi is the elegant and historic heart of Parma, a place where architecture, culture, and gastronomy intertwine beautifully. Surrounded by striking 17th- and 18th-century landmarks such as the Palazzo del Comune and Palazzo del Governatore, the square reflects the city’s refined identity.
Just beyond its arcades and cobblestone streets, you’ll find artisanal salumerie, traditional bakeries, and family-run cafés that have served locals for generations. The air is filled with the aromas of aged cheese, freshly baked pastries, and fine cured meats, unmistakable symbols of Parma’s devotion to quality and craftsmanship.
More than a landmark, Piazza Garibaldi is where Parma’s heart truly beats, making it the perfect setting to begin your journey into the city’s legendary food culture.

## Frequently Asked Questions
How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Just email us at [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) and we will do our best to arrange something that suits you. After your booking is made, we will send you an automatic e-mail confirmation. If you have any questions before booking, feel free to contact us via email
\*Please note if you are using a special promo code then enter it in the **"Voucher Code"** section before you select the amount of tickets you would like to purchase\*
What is included in the food tour?
All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (who doesn't?) We offer an [Upgraded Drinks Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/modena/upgraded-drinks-package/) that gives you 2 additional glasses of locally selected wine
Where is the meeting spot?
You can find the [meeting spot](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/parma/food-tours-parma/#CONTACT) here and if it's not clear you can always contact us.
We'll meet at Piazzale San Francesco (in front of Casa della Musica), 43121 Parma, Italy.
[»»CLICK HERE FOR DIRECTIONS](https://maps.app.goo.gl/Bj2ardjqnjgu8vWk8)
The tour guide will be there with an orange umbrella and a big smile!
Is your food tour suitable for a vegetarian or someone with other dietary requirements?
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomy experience, many of our tours are unable to accommodate certain dietary restrictions. Please contact us at [parma@secrettours.com](mailto:parma@secrettours.com) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs.
I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?
Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.
Is there enough food during your tour?
We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be approximately 45 minutes of walking, but it is not too difficult and is broken up between food stops. Our guide walks at the pace of the tour members, you will never be rushed, but if you are on a wheelchair, write to us before booking.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to accommodate you.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drinks are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel and we cannot run the tour due to extreme weather for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Parma?
Book our award-winning Secret Food Tours - Parma before it fills up!

# Palma de Mallorca Food Tour
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps

## What you'll do
On our Secret Food Tour: Palma de Mallorca, you’ll dive into the island’s rich culinary traditions while exploring the historic heart of the city. You’ll experience how locals truly eat and socialize, enjoying a carefully curated selection of Mallorcan specialties along the way. From savory bites like sobrassada and local cheese to fresh seafood and traditional pastries, each stop reveals a different side of Mallorca’s food culture. Your tastings are perfectly paired with local wines, and of course, a sweet treat to finish.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drink Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/mallorca/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
You’ll start the tour at Mercado del Olivar, the city’s most iconic food market and a true hub of local life.
Your first stop will be at a traditional seafood stand, where you’ll enjoy a perfectly grilled scallop and a fresh oyster, simple yet refined bites that highlight Mallorca’s close connection to the Mediterranean.
Next, you’ll make your way to a local charcuterie counter inside the market to sample a carefully selected platter of island favorites. You’ll taste Mahón cheese, Iberian acorn-fed ham, sobrassada de Mallorca, fuet, salami, and camaiot, all thoughtfully paired with a glass of local red wine — a true introduction to Mallorca’s rich charcuterie tradition.
After leaving the market, we’ll enjoy a short walk through the surrounding streets before stopping at a traditional local forn, where you’ll taste a classic coca de trampó—a beloved island flatbread topped with tomato, green pepper, and onion, simple, fresh, and full of flavor.
We’ll then continue to a traditional Mallorcan restaurant that has been family-run for over 68 years and was originally a wine cellar. Here, you’ll sit down to enjoy the Daily Mediterranean special, prepared using time-honored recipes, accompanied by a glass of house wine. The experience is rounded off with a homemade dessert and a traditional digestive, a perfect reflection of Mallorca’s culinary heritage.
To end the tour, we’ll visit a rustic, artisanal bakery. This cozy spot is dedicated to traditional island pastries, and you’ll savor a warm, freshly baked ensaimada,
As with all our tours, there's also a delicious Secret Dish.

## Why Palma de Mallorca?
If you’re a passionate foodie and an adventurous traveler, Palma de Mallorca is a must-visit destination. This vibrant Mediterranean city blends centuries of history with a thriving culinary scene, where traditional island flavors meet fresh, seasonal ingredients. From its sun-drenched squares and colorful historic neighborhoods to its lively markets and hidden taverns, Palma offers a perfect mix of culture, charm, and gastronomy.
The city’s rich heritage is rooted in Mallorca’s island traditions, with centuries-old customs and artisanal craft shaping its unique Mediterranean identity. Discover Palma’s warmth, charm, and vibrant culture, and let the island captivate you at every turn.

All the delicious food and drinks mentioned above, but if you want more alcohol (and who doesn't?) We offer a [upgraded drinks package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/mallorca/upgraded-drinks-package/) that gives you 1 drink, tax included.

I am traveling with my family, is the tour ok for infants/children?

I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?

Is there enough food during your tour?

Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 30 mins of walking but it is not too difficult and is broken up between food stops. During the duration of the tour we will walk 2 kilometers. The guide walks at the pace of the tour members, you will never be rushed.
How many people max are on this tour?
We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.
I cannot find a way to print the confirmation. Is it OK if I have it on my phone?
You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and they will do their best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
The food and drinks are top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Palma de Mallorca. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.

We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.

## What you'll do
Escape the generic resorts to uncover the authentic soul of the city. Our Puerto Vallarta food tour takes you where locals eat, from mountain-sourced market stalls to the salt-aired stands of the Malecon.
Savor the history of the Banderas Bay through nine unique tastings. You’ll witness ancient traditions and modern coastal life collide in the city’s most vibrant neighborhoods.
Private tours are available for a personalized experience.
### SEE ITINERARY
Market Kitchen Chilaquiles:
Start your morning inside a bustling local market with a plate of crispy, salsa-drenched chilaquiles topped with fresh cream and local cheese.
The Artisanal Tortilleria:
Go behind the scenes to see the rhythmic machinery of a traditional tortilleria and taste a warm, earthy corn tortilla seconds after it leaves the press.
Family-Run Panaderia:
Breathe in the scent of yeast and sugar at a neighborhood bakery where you’ll sample golden, flaky Mexican sweet breads.
Sizzling Carnitas Taco:
Bite into a masterfully seasoned pork taco, slow-cooked in its own juices until tender and served with bright, hand-made salsas.
Coastal Shrimp Taco:
Taste the Pacific with a plump, ocean-fresh shrimp taco prepared at a beloved local stand near the historic center.
Catch-of-the-Day Tostada:
Experience the crunch of a perfectly fried tostada piled high with marinated local fish and zesty lime.
Regional Birria:
Dive into a bowl of hearty, slow-roasted birria, a Jalisco state specialty known for its rich, aromatic broth and tender meat.
Our Secret Dish:
A surprise culinary gem known only to Vallarta insiders, revealed only on the day of your tour.
Michoacana Paleta:
Cool down on the Malecon with a refreshing, fruit-filled popsicle crafted from seasonal Mexican ingredients.

# Bangkok Daytime Food Tour
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide
Discover Bangkok with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
Also recommended by
 2 ½ hours
 Ruam Sab Market
 6 stops
 12 people max
 English
## What you'll do
On the Ruam Sab Market Lunch & Canal Boat Experience, you’ll step straight into Bangkok’s midday rhythm, eating where local office workers actually have lunch. Your guide will walk you through the Ruam Sab food court, introducing classic Thai comfort dishes like stir‑fried holy basil with chicken, papaya salad, braised pork leg over rice, noodle soup, and stir‑fried wide rice noodles, explaining what makes each one special and how locals customize flavor with chilies, lime, and fish sauce. You’ll also enjoy traditional sweets such as grilled banana and crispy Thai pancakes, plus your choice of Thai iced tea or fresh fruit smoothie to cool down like a true Bangkokian.
After lunch, you’ll swap the food court for the water, boarding a Khlong Saen Saep taxi boat for a short ride through one of Bangkok’s busiest canals, used daily by commuters escaping the city’s traffic. As you glide toward Pratunam, you’ll see canal‑side neighborhoods, bridges, and hidden corners of the city that most visitors never notice, before stepping off right by Bangkok’s central shopping district with easy access to CentralWorld and the BTS for your onward adventures.
### SEE ITINERARY
You’ll start the tour at Phetchaburi MRT Station, Exit 3, where your guide will meet the group and lead you on a short walk to nearby Ruam Sab Market,
Ruam San Market, a busy lunch spot beloved by Bangkok office workers. As you enter the food court, your guide will introduce how this canteen works, pointing out different stalls and explaining how locals choose their perfect combination of rice dishes, noodles, and snacks.
Your first tastings will focus on classic wok dishes. You’ll share a plate of stir‑fried chicken with holy basil over rice and a bright, zesty papaya salad, learning how chilies, garlic, lime, and fish sauce come together to create Thailand’s famous balance of flavors. Then you’ll move on to the “secret dish” of braised pork leg over rice, followed by comforting bowls of stewed‑pork noodle soup and stir‑fried rice noodles with pork, the kind of satisfying dishes that fuel Bangkok’s workday.
To finish your meal, you’ll enjoy two traditional Thai sweets: smoky grilled banana and crispy Thai pancakes, each guest receiving their own portion so they can truly savor the textures and sweetness. You’ll wash it all down with your choice of Thai iced tea or a fresh fruit smoothie, just like the locals cooling off on their lunch break.
After lunch, your guide will lead you to the nearby pier on Khlong Saen Saep, one of Bangkok’s busiest commuter canals, where you’ll board a public taxi boat used every day by thousands of residents. As the boat speeds toward Pratunam, you’ll see canal‑side homes, small bridges, and glimpses of neighborhood life that most visitors miss, while your guide shares stories about Bangkok’s history as the “Venice of the East.”
You’ll disembark at Pratunam pier, right in the heart of Bangkok’s shopping district, a short walk from CentralWorld and the BTS stations at Chit Lom and Siam, perfectly placed to continue exploring, shopping, or simply relaxing after your local‑style Bangkok lunch adventure.
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances. See Menu below for day of tour specifics.

Mon - Sat 10:45p.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## Why Ruam Sab Market?
Why Ruam Sab Market Lunch & Canal Boat Experience
If you love eating like a local and discovering how a city really works, this experience is a perfect fit. Ruam Sab Market isn’t a tourist attraction – it’s where Bangkok office workers actually line up for their everyday lunch, choosing from wok‑fried basil dishes, curries, noodles, and street‑style sweets at true local prices. Sitting among them, you feel the real midday rhythm of the city: fast, friendly, a little chaotic, and absolutely full of flavor.
The canal ride adds a second layer to the story. Instead of staying in malls and on main roads, you’ll ride a Khlong Saen Saep taxi boat, one of Bangkok’s busiest commuter routes, gliding past canal‑side homes, small temples, and hidden backstreets that most visitors never see. This combination of lunch in a local market and traveling by commuter boat reveals Bangkok as locals experience it every day – practical, surprising, and deeply connected to its food.

## What you'll do
Experience Auckland's culinary and cultural evolution on our Secret Food Tour. This circular journey encompasses the city's key areas, guiding you to the Shoreline and harbour back through the CBD to the iconic Sky Tower.
You'll taste classic dishes brought by the first migrants' cuisine and influenced by New Zealand's unique wildlife. Discover how the city developed and expanded beyond its original boundaries, witnessing the architectural blend of Victorian and modern styles. Despite this urban development, you'll see how the enduring Maori influence remains a vibrant part of Auckland's design and public display.
Quality drinks will be served at some stops, however if you would like more, a thoughtfully curated [Upgraded Drink Package](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/auckland/upgraded-drinks-package/) is available.
### SEE ITINERARY
Our culinary adventure begins at the foot of the iconic Sky Tower on Victoria Street. First up, you'll choose a delicious savoury meat pie to enjoy al fresco in a beautiful park, offering a pretty combined view of St Patrick’s Cathedral and the Sky Tower.
Next, we journey to the harbour, where you'll discover how the original 1840 shoreline has moved and transformed over time to accommodate waves of migrants and the "floating cities" of today's cruise ships.
Midway, we'll take a refreshing pause for a "secret dish," preparing our palates for the next tasting experience.
At the bustling fish market food court, get ready for a classic Kiwi treat: freshly cooked fish and chips paired with a beloved local soda.
As we loop back towards the CBD, it’s time to sample some local brews with a tasting at a micro-brewery.
Finally, to wrap up a lovely morning stroll, we'll enjoy a last touch of sweetness: a smooth, creamy flat white coffee accompanied by a traditional Afghan cookie.
Upgrade your Auckland food tour with extra local beer tastings and a glass of Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc for the perfect Kiwi food pairing. You must be 18 or older and have a valid ID.

## Why Auckland CBD & The Harbour?
Auckland's shorelines have been significantly reshaped to facilitate the arrival and settlement of migrants. This constant architectural evolution continues today, with increasingly modern styles that reflect a growing Maori influence.
The Central Business District (CBD) is continually transforming, where the two-storey buildings of the early settlers stand in sharp contrast to modern high-rise condos and office blocks. Throughout the city, the Sky Tower serves as an unmistakable, visible landmark.

How do I order?
That is very easy! Go to our "Book Now" page, where we detail our tours. Select your tour and the time of your tour and book it! Are you looking for something special or are you organizing an event? Please contact us at [auckland@secrettours.com](mailto:auckland@secrettours.com) prior to booking your tour to see if we are able to accommodate your dietary needs.

Children are more than welcome on our tour and it's free for any child 2 years or under! We have had many families attend from all over the world and everyone enjoyed the tour.
I don't drink alcohol. Can I book your tour?
This is not a problem. Just inform our guide before the tour starts. During the tasting you can select another beverage you like.

We give enough food to all our customers. Trust us, you will not need to go to any restaurant after the Food tour!
Where do I add my discount voucher?
I would like to take your tour but it is already fully booked. Is there a way I can do the tour?
Our tours are often sold out very quickly. If the date you requested is not available, please do not hesitate to email us and we can see if there is anything we can do.
Is there a lot of walking?
To join our food tour, you must wear comfortable shoes. There will be around 45 mins of walking but it is not too difficult and is broken up between food stops. The guide walks at the pace of the tour members, you will never be rushed.

We take 12 people maximum. If you are more than 12 people, please do not hesitate to email us and we will try to see if we can accommodate.

You do not have to print your voucher. A confirmation on your phone will be fine!
If I purchase a voucher as a gift, or am given a voucher how long is it valid for?
All of our vouchers expire one year from the date of purchase.
Can I choose my own cheese and meat?
Our guide will take care of everything. Should you wish to try something special, please inform our guide and he will do his best to make you happy.
Where does your food come from?
We work with small shops in the Auckland. The food and beer we buy is top-quality, fresh and local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
Unfortunately, the weather is unpredictable in Auckland. Bring an umbrella and we will try to be more indoors than out. We do not issue refunds as per our terms and conditions. We also inform you that we never cancel a booking even if we have only 2 people! However we do reserve the right to cancel the tour if we feel the weather is extreme, and we cannot run the tour due to this for more info see our [Terms and Conditions](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
Can I pay the tour in cash?
You must book directly on our website. We do not accept cash payments. Our online payment is safe, secured and SSL protected.
Why do we need to pay for a booking fee?
The booking fee that you have to pay is up to our ticket provider and we can't change it. All our clients need to pay for it upon booking. The booking fee charged contributes to the cost of the ticketing system to provide an excellent service to our customers.
Foreign Transaction Fees
A few banks have charged their customer an extra fee while booking with us (this isn't the booking fee but another fee added by the bank). We think this is unfair, if this happens let us know by showing us in your bank statement and we will refund this fee as a gesture of good will.
Are the food tours wheelchair and/or stroller accessible?
Many of our tours are not wheelchair or stroller accessible. We recommend contacting us before booking to discuss this specific tour and accessibility options.

## What you'll do
On this Secret Food Tour, we’ll take you beyond the guidebooks and into the vibrant heart of Cape Town’s food scene, tasting the many faces, cultures and cuisines Cape town has to offer. Follow your local guide in discovering hidden gems, local eateries, bustling markets, braai spots, and elegant wine bars along the way.
From Cape Malay cuisine, to real South African Food, sample refined wine tastings, taste tea in botanical gardens and learn to eat pap with your hands, on this culinary tour of Cape Town you will unpack the real side of the city.
Our journey begins in the colourful streets of Bo-Kaap, where the bright houses and cobbled lanes tell stories of Cape Town’s Cape Malay heritage. As we wander through this historic neighbourhood and into the city’s lively backstreets, we’ll taste the cultural influences that have shaped South African cuisine from indigenous African traditions and Cape Malay spices to Dutch, British, and modern global flavours that make the city’s food scene so unique.
Along the way, we’ll stop at tucked-away local spots loved by Capetonians, meet the locals, watch chefs prepare dishes, and sample everything from comforting street food favourites to refined wine and cheese pairings in stylish city venues. With every stop, you’ll experience a different side of Cape Town’s culinary culture bold flavours, warm hospitality, and recipes passed down through generations.
As we explore, we’ll meet passionate local vendors and food artisans who bring these dishes to life, tasting our way through a variety of must-try South African specialities that showcase the diversity of the Rainbow Nation.
Due to the nature of creating a well-balanced and thoughtful gastronomic experience, some dietary restrictions may not be accommodated. Please contact us prior to booking so we can assist you. Menu subject to seasonality, availability and time.
### SEE ITINERARY
Start your Tour in a hidden oasis at a locals home for Cape Malay Cuisine, a selection of the best sweet coconut buns, rich samosas and fried malay snacks
Head into the heart of Cape Town for Rooibos tea in the cities historic botanical gardens
Meet the locals in a whole in the wall spot for real African Food, try Pap with your hands and a selection of South African delicacies
Head over to beloved local bar for real boerewors the nations beloved Braai (bbq) sausage, washed down with a cold local beer
Taste the Cape Winlands with a refreshing wine tasting paired with local home made breads, cheeses, charcuterie and oil.
Finish with an indulgent treat at an artisanal chocolate shop for a sweet take of South Africa's Bunny Chow, a banana bread lava cake filled with ice cream and local chocolate.
To make your experience extra special, we’ve included a surprise Secret Dish that perfectly captures the heart of the city's cuisine.
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances
\*Please note: We can’t accommodate pets on our food tours.

Maheu - A creamy maize drink
A local IPA beer
Water and soft drinks available
Non-alcoholic beverages for those who don’t consume alcohol

## Why Cape Town Historic Centre & Bo-Kaap?
We chose Cape Town’s Historic City Centre and Bo-Kaap because together they represent the cultural and culinary soul of the city. Bo-Kaap, with its iconic colourful houses and cobbled streets, is the historic home of the Cape Malay community and the birthplace of many of Cape Town’s most beloved flavours and spices. Just steps away, the Historic City Centre blends African traditions with Dutch, British, and global influences, creating one of the most exciting food scenes in the world.
This area is a true melting pot where traditional braai spots, family-run eateries, and vibrant local cafés sit alongside stylish wine bars and modern restaurants. As you explore the lively streets, you’ll discover the stories behind the neighbourhood’s people, history, and food culture while tasting dishes that reflect the diverse communities that shaped Cape Town.
From the colourful streets of Bo-Kaap to the hidden corners of the city centre, the energy, heritage, and incredible flavours make this area a must-visit for any food lover.
By the end of the tour, you’ll have a deeper appreciation for Cape Town’s unique food culture, a full stomach, and plenty of insider tips on where to eat next.

title: Express Food Tour: Toronto
text:
# Express Food Tour: Toronto
Skip the tourist traps and truly discover Toronto with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
 2 hours
 Kensington Market
 4 stops
 20 people max
 English
# Express Food Tour: Toronto
[Home](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/)
[Toronto](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/toronto/)
Express Food Tour: Toronto
# Express Food Tour: Toronto
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide
Discover Toronto with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
Also recommended by
 2 hours
 Kensington Market
 4 stops
 20 people max
 English
## What you'll do
Strap on your walking shoes for a deep dive into the flavors of Kensington Market, Toronto’s ultimate destination for global street food. Over the course of two hours, we’ll eat our way through the neighborhood's storied history, using our taste buds to map out its evolution. Kensington is a living, breathing tapestry of immigrant culture, and there’s no better way to understand its bohemian soul than through the kitchens that define these streets. Between bites, we’ll explore the vibrant alleys and hand-painted storefronts of this National Historic Site, uncovering the rebellious spirit that keeps this market independent and authentic.
Our journey centers on four distinct culinary stops, each offering a different window into the world. You’ll kick things off with the bold, warming spices of East Asian comfort food before moving on to a crunchy, sustainably-sourced classic that proves "guilt-free" can be delicious. We’ll then tuck into a "secret" destination for a refreshing, fruit-filled handmade treat that’s as much an art form as it is a dessert. Finally, we’ll wrap up our trek by warming up with the soul-soothing, traditional staples of the Himalayas. By the time we’re through, you’ll have experienced the very best of the market’s multicultural menu and the stories behind the people who cook it.
### SEE ITINERARY
We begin our journey in the heart of Kensington Market at Original Ka Chi. Here, you’ll dive into a quintessential East Asian comfort dish with a classic Bibimbap. These vibrant "Korean rice bowls" feature your choice of beef, pork, chicken, or tofu, topped with a signature sunny-side-up egg and a colorful array of seasoned vegetables, showcasing the warming spices and rich, satisfying flavors that define the neighborhood.
Next, we continue through the lively market streets to Fresco’s Fish and Chips. At this stop, you’ll enjoy sustainably sourced cod paired with a fresh kale salad, a crunchy and flavorful bite that proves comfort food can also be light and guilt-free.
We then slip into a true hidden gem for our “secret stop” at Nomnom Fruit. Here, you’ll cool down with a refreshing, handmade mango mochi (one per two guests), a fruit-filled treat that’s as playful and artistic as Kensington itself.
Our journey continues to Golden Patty, a neighborhood staple known for its authentic Caribbean flavors. Here, you’ll experience a classic Toronto pairing: a flaky Jamaican patty served inside soft, slightly sweet coco bread (one per two guests).
After that, we’ll walk over for a taste of the rich Italian roots that exist not only in Toronto but all of Canada, where you'll enjoy a comforting trio of Alfredo, Pesto & Tomato Sauce Tortellini - a local favorite loaded with rich flavor.
Then we’ll head into the historic Distillery District to visit a local craft brewery, where you’ll devour a delicious serving of classic poutine - crispy fries topped with squeaky cheese curds and savory gravy, the ultimate Canadian comfort food.
And for a great end, you'll devour the mouth-watering goodness of a classic Canadian pastry, the famous Butter Tart.
\*Please note: the itinerary and menu is subject to change, based on the locations' availability, weather, and other circumstances.
Book Your Tour
C$75.99 / person
Lowest price guarantee
If you have any issues with payment, please email us at [contact@secrettours.com](mailto:contact@secrettours.com) with the city, date and time of tour, and how many adult and child's tickets you need so we can assist you.
**If a tour is fully booked, or you are looking to book a \*Group or Private Tour - please email us about [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) options.**
\*Please note - Group & Private Tours are specifically organised for your group and therefore subject to a premium price.
Looking to gift a memorable experience?
We've got you!
[Buy as a gift](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/buy-gift/)
What you'll taste
### THE FOOD
Bibimbap "Korean rice bowl" ( beef, pork, chicken or tofu)
Cod fish and kale salad
Jamaican patty with coco bread
Plus, our signature Secret Dish
Meeting Spot
We'll meet at 20 Kensington Ave, Toronto, ON M5T 2J7, Canada. If you have any questions. Please [email us](mailto: toronto@secrettours.com).
20 Kensington Ave, Toronto, ON M5T 2J7, Canada
[See on Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/1qRyngKsxf6maq3v7)
Daily 12:00p.m.
Your guide will be waiting with an orange umbrella so they are easy to spot.
## Why Kensington Market?
Kensington Market is one of Toronto’s most iconic and authentic neighborhoods, known for its colorful streets, independent spirit, and deep cultural roots. Just west of downtown, this National Historic Site feels like a small village within the city, where vintage shops, hand-painted signs, and lively street art set the tone. The area has long been a first home for immigrant communities, and that layered history is visible in every storefront, café, and market stall you pass.
What makes Kensington Market truly special is its unmatched culinary diversity. Within a few blocks, you’ll find flavors from East Asia, the Caribbean, Latin America, Europe, and the Himalayas, all represented by small, family-run businesses. Many of these kitchens are driven by tradition rather than trends, offering recipes passed down through generations alongside creative, modern twists. The food here reflects the people who built the neighborhood—bold, honest, and deeply connected to cultural heritage.
Kensington Market is an ideal choice for a food tour because it offers more than just great bites—it offers context and character. As you walk between stops, you experience the sights, sounds, and stories that shaped Toronto into the multicultural city it is today. This is not a polished or commercial food district; it’s a living, evolving community where every bite comes with a story, making it the perfect place to truly taste the soul of the city.
[\\
BOOK NOW!](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/toronto/express-food-tours-toronto/#BOOKING)

title: Lo que harás
text:
## Lo que harás
Únete a nuestro Secret Food Tour: Madrid y vive la experiencia culinaria definitiva, diseñada para quienes buscan sumergirse de lleno en la vida local. Pasearemos por sus calles y cruzaremos el umbral de tabernas históricas para saborear una cuidada selección de platos: desde el mejor jamón ibérico hasta exclusivos vinos de la región. Disfruta de un viaje donde cada bocado revela la esencia y la herencia de Madrid
Se servirán bebidas de calidad en algunas paradas; sin embargo, si deseas más, hay disponible un [paquete de bebidas mejorado](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/madrid/upgraded-drinks-package-es/) cuidadosamente seleccionado.
### SEE ITINERARY
Comenzarás en una charcutería que lleva más de 190 años en funcionamiento, donde probarás dos tipos de jamón ibérico, salami ibérico, lomo y chorizo, acompañados de aceite de oliva virgen extra.
A continuación, disfruta de un clásico bocadillo de calamares fritos, un favorito de los madrileños, servido con patatas bravas y aceitunas.
Degustarás un vermut con la tapa del día, uno de los favoritos entre los locales.
Entra en un restaurante ubicado en un edificio del siglo XVIII para disfrutar de tortilla española, queso manchego, pimientos de Padrón y dos tapas adicionales que cambian a diario, haciendo cada visita única.
El tour terminará en una pastelería histórica con más de 170 años de historia, donde disfrutarás de un pastel de temporada.
Como en todos nuestros tours, también disfrutarás de nuestro delicioso Plato Secreto.
Orujo - Licor de hierbas
Patxaran - Licor de endrinas
Licor de café

## Frequently Asked Questions
¿Cómo hago mi pedido?
¡Es muy fácil! Ve a nuestra página de “Book Now”, donde mostramos todos nuestros tours. Selecciona tu tour y la hora, y haz tu reserva. ¿Buscas algo especial o estás organizando un evento? Solo **email us** en [**Group & Private Tours**](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) y haremos todo lo posible por adaptarnos a lo que necesitas. Después de realizar la reserva, recibirás una confirmación automática por correo electrónico. Si tienes alguna pregunta antes de reservar, no dudes en contactarnos por email.
¿Qué incluye el tour gastronómico?
Toda la comida y bebidas mencionadas anteriormente están incluidas, pero si deseas más alcohol, ofrecemos un [**upgraded drinks package**](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/madrid/upgraded-drinks-package-es/) que incluye 1 o 2 bebidas, con impuestos incluidos.
¿Dónde es el punto de encuentro?
Puedes encontrar el [**meeting spot here**](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/madrid/food-tours-madrid-es/#CONTACT) y, si no está claro, siempre puedes contactarnos.
Comenzamos nuestros tours a las 11am. El punto de encuentro es fuera del metro Sol (líneas 1, 2 y 3), donde está la estatua del “Oso y Madroño”, justo frente a la tienda Apple, con un paraguas naranja y una gran sonrisa.
¿Dónde termina el tour?
El tour terminará en Calle Mayor, 10, Centro, 28013 Madrid, a solo 200 metros del punto de encuentro.
¿El tour es apto para vegetarianos o personas con requisitos dietéticos especiales?
Puedes informar a nuestro guía sobre cualquier alergia o requisito alimenticio para que podamos adaptar el menú y disfrutes del tour. Si tienes dudas, puedes **email us** en madrid@secrettours.com antes de reservar. Hemos tenido muchos vegetarianos en nuestros tours y todos lo han disfrutado.
Viajo con mi familia, ¿el tour es adecuado para bebés o niños?
Los niños son más que bienvenidos y es gratis para menores de 2 años. Muchas familias de todo el mundo han participado y todos han disfrutado el tour.
No bebo alcohol. ¿Puedo reservar el tour?
No hay problema. Solo informa a nuestro guía antes de comenzar. Durante las degustaciones puedes elegir otra bebida.
¿Hay suficiente comida durante el tour?
Ofrecemos suficiente comida para todos. No necesitarás ir a un restaurante después del tour.
¿Dónde agrego mi código de descuento?
Quiero hacer el tour pero está completo. ¿Hay alguna alternativa?
Nuestros tours se agotan rápido. Si la fecha no está disponible, escríbenos y veremos si podemos ayudarte.
¿Se camina mucho durante el tour?
Debes usar calzado cómodo. Se camina entre 20 y 25 minutos en total. No es difícil y no hay colinas ni escaleras. El guía se adapta al ritmo del grupo.
¿Cuántas personas como máximo hay en el tour?
El máximo es de 12 personas. Si sois más, escríbenos e intentaremos organizarlo.
No puedo imprimir la confirmación. ¿Puedo mostrarla en el móvil?
No necesitas imprimirla. La confirmación en tu móvil es suficiente.
¿Cuánto tiempo tengo para canjear mi voucher?
Todos nuestros vouchers tienen una validez de un año desde la fecha de compra.
¿Puedo elegir mis propios quesos y carnes?
No, el guía se encarga de todo. Si quieres probar algo específico, puedes comentarlo y hará lo posible por ayudarte.
¿De dónde proviene la comida?
¡De la mejor calidad, fresca y local!
What if it rains or I change my plans?
El clima en Madrid es impredecible. No ofrecemos reembolsos según nuestros términos. Nunca cancelamos un tour aunque haya solo 2 personas. Solo cancelamos en caso de clima extremo. Más información en [Términos y Condiciones](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/).
¿Puedo pagar en efectivo?
Debes reservar en nuestra web. No aceptamos pagos en efectivo. El pago online es seguro y protegido con SSL.
¿Por qué hay una tarifa de reserva?
La tarifa depende del proveedor de tickets. Todos los clientes deben pagarla al reservar. Cubre el sistema de reservas para ofrecer un buen servicio.
Comisiones por transacciones internacionales
Algunos bancos aplican cargos adicionales. Si esto ocurre, envíanos tu estado de cuenta y te reembolsaremos ese cargo como gesto de buena voluntad.
¿Los tours son accesibles para sillas de ruedas o cochecitos?
Muchos tours no son accesibles. Recomendamos contactarnos antes de reservar para revisar opciones disponibles.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
## READY TO TASTE THE REAL Madrid?

# Tour gastronómico exprés: Madrid
Huye de los sitios típicos para turistas y conoce el verdadero Madrid. Con nuestro tour gastronómico guiado. Descubrirás rincones ocultos y los lugares preferidos de los madrileños

# Tour gastronómico exprés: Madrid
 Skip the overpriced tourist traps
 Uncover the city's history and culture
 Exclusive food tours in over 90 cities worldwide
Discover Madrid with our top-rated guided food tour, which takes you to delicious, lesser-known food spots loved by locals
Also recommended by
 2 - 2 ½ horas
 El centro histórico de Madrid
 4 stops
 18 people max
 Inglés
## Lo que harás
Convierte un horario ajustado en una experiencia gastronómica llena de impacto. Nuestro Tour Gastronómico Exprés reúne toda la diversión, sabor y conocimiento local en solo dos horas. Descubre cómo comen realmente los locales mientras recorres las animadas calles de Madrid, haciendo paradas para disfrutar de deliciosas especialidades españolas. Dinámico, sabroso y lleno de carácter.
### SEE ITINERARY
Comenzarás en una delicatessen centenaria ubicada cerca de la Plaza Mayor, famosa por sus dulces escolares tradicionales que transportan a los locales a su infancia.
A continuación, saborearás un tradicional bocadillo de calamares fritos, un clásico favorito de Madrid, acompañado de patatas picantes y aceitunas.
Después, entraremos en un restaurante ubicado en un edificio del siglo XVIII para disfrutar de una variedad de tapas clásicas, como tortilla española, queso manchego y pimientos de padrón. También se te servirán dos tapas adicionales que cambian a diario, garantizando una experiencia única cada vez.
El tour concluirá en una pastelería histórica con más de 170 años de historia, donde disfrutarás de uno de los postres más secretos de Madrid, cerrando perfectamente la experiencia.
Para hacer tu experiencia aún más especial, hemos incluido un Plato Secreto sorpresa que captura perfectamente la esencia de la gastronomía de la ciudad.
\*Por favor ten en cuenta: el itinerario y el menú están sujetos a cambios según la disponibilidad de los lugares, el clima y otras circunstancias.
\*Por favor ten en cuenta: no podemos admitir mascotas en nuestros tours gastronómicos.
Book Your Tour

\*Please note - Group & Private Tours are specifically organised for your group and therefore subject to a premium price.

## ¿POR QUÉ EL CENTRO HISTÓRICO?
El centro histórico de Madrid es el corazón de la ciudad, lleno de energía, encanto y sabores inolvidables. Los locales amables y el ambiente animado lo convierten en el lugar perfecto para explorar la ciudad como un verdadero madrileño.
Hemos elegido esta zona porque combina ricas tradiciones culinarias con joyas culturales e históricas. Desde calles centenarias hasta plazas llenas de vida, alberga lugares emblemáticos y una destacada escena gastronómica, ofreciendo el escenario ideal para un tour divertido y sabroso.

## Qué incluye
_Debido a la naturaleza de crear una experiencia gastronómica equilibrada y cuidadosamente diseñada, muchos de nuestros tours no pueden adaptarse a ciertas restricciones dietéticas. Por favor, contáctanos antes de reservar para confirmar si podemos ajustarnos a tus necesidades alimentarias._
### SEE ITINERARY
Orujo - Licor de hierbas
El orujo se ha convertido en una elaboración artesanal para algunas familias que, tras hacer vino para consumo propio, destilan el orujo en pequeños alambiques. Se obtiene a partir de uvas de calidad y se produce siguiendo altos estándares. Tradicionalmente solo estaba disponible en pequeños pueblos y en los mejores locales de Madrid.
Patxaran - Licor de endrinas
Se sabe que existe desde la Edad Media y se comercializó por primera vez en 1956. El patxaran fue originalmente un licor casero y se popularizó a finales del siglo XIX. Se elabora macerando endrinas, recogidas del arbusto del endrino, junto con algunos granos de café y una rama de canela en anís.
Licor de café
Se sabe que existe desde la Edad Media y se comercializó por primera vez en 1956. Se elabora con café, azúcar y orujo. Normalmente se sirve en chupito con hielo.
\*Por favor ten en cuenta: el itinerario y el menú están sujetos a cambios según la disponibilidad de los lugares, el clima y otras circunstancias.
\*Por favor ten en cuenta: no podemos admitir mascotas en nuestros tours gastronómicos.

## ¿POR QUÉ EL CENTRO HISTÓRICO?
Madrid es una ciudad abierta donde los madrileños, amables y apasionados, hacen que todos se sientan como en casa. Hemos elegido el centro histórico de Madrid porque es el que más ofrece en términos de legado cultural, historia y, por supuesto, gastronomía española.
Antiguamente hogar de reyes y artistas reconocidos, el centro histórico de Madrid está lleno de historia y encanto. Hoy en día es conocido por su destacada escena gastronómica, sus monumentos emblemáticos y su ambiente animado, lo que lo convierte en el escenario perfecto para nuestro tour gastronómico.

## Frequently Asked Questions
¿Cómo reservo?
¡Es muy fácil! Ve a nuestra página “Book Now”, donde detallamos nuestros tours. Selecciona tu tour, elige la hora y resérvalo. ¿Buscas algo especial o estás organizando un evento? Escríbenos a [Group & Private Tours](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/private-bookings) y haremos lo posible por organizar algo que se adapte a ti. Después de realizar tu reserva, te enviaremos un correo de confirmación automático. Si tienes alguna pregunta antes de reservar, no dudes en contactarnos por email.
\*Ten en cuenta que si usas un código promocional, debes ingresarlo en la sección “Voucher Code” antes de seleccionar la cantidad de entradas que deseas comprar\*
¿Qué incluye el tour gastronómico?
Toda la comida deliciosa y una bebida mencionadas anteriormente.
¿Dónde es el punto de encuentro?
Puedes encontrar el [punto de encuentro aquí](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/madrid/upgraded-drinks-package-es/) y, si no está claro, siempre puedes contactarnos.
Comenzamos nuestros tours a las 18:15.
El punto de encuentro es en la Plaza Mayor, frente a la oficina de información turística, junto a la letra “i” destacada.
Tu guía estará esperando con un paraguas naranja para que sea fácil de reconocer.
[»»HAZ CLIC AQUÍ PARA VER LAS DIRECCIONES««](https://maps.app.goo.gl/vtPUWaQ6FBiwSGRQ7)
¿Dónde termina el tour?
El tour terminará en Calle Mayor, 10, Centro, 28013 Madrid, a solo 150 metros del punto de encuentro.
¿El tour es apto para vegetarianos o personas con requisitos dietéticos especiales?
Puedes informar a nuestro guía sobre cualquier alergia o requisito dietético para adaptar el menú y que puedas disfrutar del tour. Si tienes dudas, escríbenos a madrid@secrettours.com antes de reservar. Hemos tenido muchos vegetarianos en nuestros tours y todos han disfrutado la experiencia.
Viajo con mi familia, ¿el tour es adecuado para bebés o niños?
Los niños son más que bienvenidos y es gratis para menores de 2 años. Hemos recibido muchas familias de todo el mundo y todos han disfrutado el tour.
No bebo alcohol. ¿Puedo reservar el tour?
No hay problema. Solo informa a nuestro guía antes de comenzar. Durante la degustación puedes elegir otra bebida.
¿Hay suficiente comida durante el tour?
Ofrecemos suficiente comida para todos. No necesitarás ir a ningún restaurante después del tour.
¿Dónde agrego mi código de descuento?
Quiero hacer el tour pero está completo. ¿Hay alguna alternativa?
Nuestros tours se agotan rápidamente. Si la fecha no está disponible, escríbenos y veremos si podemos ayudarte.
¿Se camina mucho durante el tour?
Debes usar calzado cómodo. Se camina alrededor de 20 minutos. No es difícil y no hay colinas ni escaleras. El guía se adapta al ritmo del grupo.
¿Cuántas personas como máximo hay en el tour?
El máximo es de 18 personas. Si sois más, escríbenos e intentaremos organizarlo.
No puedo imprimir la confirmación. ¿Puedo mostrarla en el móvil?
No necesitas imprimirla. La confirmación en tu móvil es suficiente.
¿Cuánto tiempo tengo para canjear mi voucher?
Todos nuestros vouchers tienen una validez de un año desde la fecha de compra.
¿Puedo elegir mis propios quesos y carnes?
Nuestro guía se encargará de todo. Si deseas probar algo especial, puedes comentarlo y hará lo posible por ayudarte.
¿De dónde proviene la comida?
La comida y las bebidas son de alta calidad, frescas y locales.
¿Qué pasa si llueve o cambio mis planes?
El clima en Madrid es impredecible. No ofrecemos reembolsos según nuestros términos y condiciones. Nunca cancelamos un tour aunque haya solo 2 personas. Sin embargo, nos reservamos el derecho de cancelar en caso de clima extremo. Para más información consulta nuestros [Términos y Condiciones.](https://www.secretfoodtours.com/terms-and-conditions/)
¿Puedo pagar en efectivo?
Debes reservar directamente en nuestra web. No aceptamos pagos en efectivo. El pago online es seguro y está protegido con SSL.
¿Por qué hay una tarifa de reserva?
La tarifa de reserva depende del proveedor de tickets y no podemos modificarla. Todos los clientes deben pagarla al reservar. Esta tarifa contribuye al sistema de reservas para ofrecer un mejor servicio.
Comisiones por transacciones internacionales
Algunos bancos aplican cargos adicionales. Si esto ocurre, envíanos tu estado de cuenta y te reembolsaremos ese cargo como gesto de buena voluntad.
¿Los tours son accesibles para sillas de ruedas o cochecitos?
Muchos de nuestros tours no son accesibles. Recomendamos contactarnos antes de reservar para revisar las opciones disponibles.
What is the lowest price guarantee?
We always offer the lowest price on our Secret Food Tours. If you find one of our food tours listed at a lower price, contact us and we'll match it.
